items.question: What does Vestigio do?
items.answer: Vestigio analyzes your operation end-to-end and delivers a monthly Strategy Plan: where money is leaking, who needs to act, the dollar impact, and what to do first. Every Plan is signed by Vestigio and refreshes each cycle.

items.question: How long until I see results?
items.answer: The first analysis is ready in 60 seconds. Your full Strategy Plan (with Thesis of the month, prioritized Next steps, and Continuity) arrives at the start of the following month. Customers who act on the first steps usually see impact within the same cycle.

items.question: Do I need a developer to install it?
items.answer: No. You paste your URL and Vestigio does the rest. No code, no tag, no technical integration to start the analysis.

items.question: Is it free? What's the cost afterward?
items.answer: The first analysis is free, no credit card. After that, you subscribe to a plan with one Strategy Plan per month: continuous analysis of identified leakage, cycle updates, PDF export, and a shareable link. 4× Guarantee in the first 30 days.

items.question: Does it work with my platform?
items.answer: Yes. Vestigio analyzes any site: Shopify, WooCommerce, BigCommerce, WordPress, custom builds, and landing pages. If it has a URL, we analyze it.

mitm_unencrypted.name: Man-in-the-middle on non-HTTPS endpoints
mitm_unencrypted.narrative: Attacker on public WiFi intercepts credentials and cookies. Tool: bettercap. Result: immediate session theft.

https_downgrade.name: HTTPS downgrade without HSTS
https_downgrade.narrative: Attacker forces HTTP via SSL stripping. Without HSTS, the browser accepts silently. Session cookies exposed.

mixed_content_injection.name: Injection via HTTP assets on HTTPS page
mixed_content_injection.narrative: Attacker MITMs HTTP scripts loaded on the secure page and injects arbitrary payload. Executes in your domain context.

tls_known_bugs.name: Exploiting outdated TLS versions
tls_known_bugs.narrative: Pre-1.2 versions have documented vulnerabilities (BEAST, POODLE). Automated tools try these first.

xss_unrestricted.name: Persistent XSS in vulnerable fields
xss_unrestricted.narrative: Without protection headers, <script> payloads in comments or profiles execute in other users' browsers. Massive session theft.

csp_xss_open.name: XSS with no Content Security Policy
csp_xss_open.narrative: Attacker injects inline scripts with no block. Without CSP, any XSS found becomes full page control.

clickjack_ui_redress.name: Clickjacking on sensitive actions
clickjack_ui_redress.narrative: Attacker embeds your site in a transparent iframe over a malicious site. User clicks 'transfer' without realizing.

cors_data_steal.name: Data theft via misconfigured CORS
cors_data_steal.narrative: Wildcard or permissive origins let malicious sites read authenticated responses. Leaks data from any logged-in user.

iframe_embed_phishing.name: Iframe embed for phishing
iframe_embed_phishing.narrative: Without X-Frame-Options, attacker embeds checkout/login in a fake site. Victim enters credentials thinking it's the real one.

cdn_compromise_chain.name: CDN compromise without SRI
cdn_compromise_chain.narrative: If the CDN or provider account is compromised, malicious scripts run on your site. SRI blocks modified scripts from executing.

admin_endpoint_access.name: Direct access to exposed admin endpoints
admin_endpoint_access.narrative: Endpoints /admin, /debug, /.env or similar accessible without auth. Pentester finds via wordlist in minutes.

session_theft.name: Session theft via cookies without flags
session_theft.narrative: Cookies without HttpOnly can be read by XSS JS. Without Secure, transmitted over HTTP. Any user session compromised.

brute_force_credentials.name: Brute force login without rate limit
brute_force_credentials.narrative: Pentester runs known credential lists (combolists from prior breaches) without being blocked. Account takeover at scale.

resource_enumeration.name: Resource enumeration via predictable URLs
resource_enumeration.narrative: Sequential URLs (invoice/1, invoice/2...) or guessable IDs leak other users' data. Without auth check, classic IDOR.

stack_trace_recon.name: Infra recon via error leakage
stack_trace_recon.narrative: Stack traces, internal paths, and software versions in error responses. Pentester learns the whole stack without auth.

budget_forecast.subtitle: Estimated monthly cost of detected vendors across growth scenarios

budget_forecast.caveat: Conservative estimates from public pricing. Transactional fees (% rates) not included.

what_breaks_at_10x.subtitle: Known scaling pain points for the vendors detected on your stack
what_breaks_at_10x.empty_description: Either no vendors with documented scaling limits are detected yet, or the next cycle will pick them up.

points.vercel_hobby_bandwidth: Hobby tier caps at 100GB bandwidth/month. Moderate growth blows past this — upgrade to Pro before the next campaign.
points.vercel_edge_timeout: Edge function timeout is 25s. Long-running tasks start failing as request volume grows.
points.vercel_function_cold_starts: Serverless functions cold-start under sustained load. Acceptable today, painful at 10x.
points.netlify_starter_bandwidth: Starter plan caps at 100GB bandwidth/month. Reserved for static + light dynamic.
points.netlify_build_minutes: Starter includes 300 build minutes/month. CI-heavy workflows hit this fast.
points.heroku_dyno_sleep: Free/Eco dynos sleep after 30 min of inactivity, causing 5-10s cold starts on the next request.
points.shopify_api_rate_limit: Shopify Admin API limits to 2 req/sec on standard plans. Bulk operations require batching.
points.shopify_checkout_extensibility: Checkout customization only available on Shopify Plus. Standard checkout has limited control.
points.wordpress_plugin_bloat: Each active plugin adds queries and JS. Past 30 plugins, page weight + TTFB degrade noticeably.
points.wordpress_db_query_volume: MySQL query volume scales linearly with traffic. Without object caching, DB becomes the bottleneck.
points.woocommerce_checkout_throughput: Checkout creates DB writes per page. Under sale-day load, checkout queues and aborts.
points.stripe_rate_limit: Stripe rate limit is 100 read + 100 write req/sec. Bulk syncs need rate-limit-aware retries.
points.stripe_webhook_lag: Webhooks may lag by minutes under heavy load. Don't rely on webhook timing for revenue-critical flows.
points.stripe_radar_review_queue: Radar manual review queue grows with volume. Auto-decline rules reduce manual burden.
points.paypal_dispute_volume: PayPal disputes cluster on high-volume merchants. Each costs $15+ regardless of outcome.
points.mercadopago_chargeback_threshold: Mercado Pago suspends accounts above 1% chargeback rate. Monitor closely as volume grows.
points.cloudflare_worker_cpu: Free Workers limited to 10ms CPU per request. Compute-heavy code hits this fast at scale.
points.cloudflare_free_unmetered: Bandwidth is unmetered on free tier, but DDoS protection and rate limits are essential.
points.fastly_request_rate: Fastly charges per request after included quota. Predictable but watch the bill.
points.sendgrid_free_daily: Free tier caps at 100 emails/day. Any commerce activity exceeds this — upgrade required.
points.sendgrid_ip_reputation: Shared IPs share reputation. High volume or bounces can throttle delivery for everyone on the pool.
points.mailgun_flex_limit: Free trial sends only via a sandbox domain. Production requires Flex plan minimum.
points.klaviyo_active_profiles: Pricing scales by active profile count. Inactive cleanup is essential to control cost.
points.mailchimp_contact_pricing: Pricing tiers by contact count. Audience pruning keeps you in lower tiers.
points.hubspot_marketing_contacts: Marketing contact count drives tier pricing. Service-only contacts are free.
points.sentry_event_quota: Free plan caps at 5K events/month. High-error apps blow past this in hours — add rate limiters or upgrade.
points.sentry_replay_pricing: Session Replay billed separately. Sample rate matters as DAU grows.
points.datadog_host_pricing: Per-host pricing. Container density and rightsizing materially affect monthly bill at scale.
points.ga4_event_cap: GA4 free tier handles 10M events/month. Most SaaS won't hit this.
points.ga4_sampling_threshold: GA4 starts sampling at 10M events for explorations. Use BigQuery export for unsampled data.
points.facebook_pixel_match_quality: Match quality drops as cookie blocking grows. Implement Conversions API server-side as you scale ad spend.
points.hotjar_session_cap: Plan tiers cap daily sessions. Heavy traffic burns daily allowance early-day, missing afternoon.
points.intercom_active_people_pricing: Priced per active person/month. Inactive contacts drive cost without value — purge regularly.
points.zendesk_agent_seat_pricing: Per-agent seat pricing scales with support team. Self-service deflection becomes key.
points.optimizely_mau_pricing: Pricing by MAU exposed to experiments. Cap to test traffic only as volume grows.
points.launchdarkly_mau_pricing: Pricing by MAU. Use targeting rules to limit exposure during scale-up.

playbook_prompts.revenue_leak_full_audit: Run a complete revenue leak analysis. Show me every finding that causes revenue loss, ranked by monthly dollar impact from highest to lowest. For the top 5, explain the root cause and what fixing each one would recover. Then show the total combined monthly loss and the estimated recovery if I fix the top 3.
playbook_prompts.conversion_bottleneck: Map my conversion funnel from landing to purchase/signup. At each stage, show which findings create friction. Where is the biggest single drop-off? What's the root cause of that drop-off? If I could only fix one thing to improve conversion, what should it be and how much would it move the needle?
playbook_prompts.trust_signal_audit: Score my site's trust signals across these dimensions: SSL & security badges, reviews & testimonials, company information (about, team, address), contact options (phone, chat, email), policies (returns, privacy, terms), payment trust (logos, guarantees), social proof (customer count, media mentions). For each dimension, rate 1-10 and explain what's missing. What's my overall trust score and what would improve it fastest?
playbook_prompts.executive_summary: Create an executive summary suitable for a board meeting. Include: total monthly revenue at risk (sum of all findings), top 3 critical issues with dollar impact, 90-day priority roadmap (week 1-2: quick wins, month 1: critical fixes, month 2-3: strategic improvements), expected ROI of the full remediation plan, and a single health score from 0-100 for the business.
playbook_prompts.cross_pack_correlation: Analyze findings across ALL packs (revenue integrity, scale readiness, chargeback resilience). Find correlations: Which findings from different packs share the same root cause? Where does fixing a revenue issue also reduce chargeback risk? Where does improving trust also help conversion? Build a correlation map showing connected findings across packs with combined impact.

business_type.headline: We'll analyze {domain} as a {answer} business.
business_type.body: Now I need to understand your situation in ~3 quick questions — what's bothering you, how you get by today without Vestigio, and why now. That's what shapes your Monthly Plan and ranks the queue correctly.

why_now.headline: Got it — we have the full picture now.
why_now.body: You want to solve a specific pain, today you cope a certain way, and now is the moment. We'll prioritize findings from that angle, write your Monthly Plan with this context baked in, and feed Vestigio AI so it can answer you directly. Just one more thing to calibrate the dollar amounts.

revenue.headline: Now we can put a dollar amount on what's at stake.
revenue.body: With your revenue as a base, every finding ships with a real range — not a generic 'high severity'. You see what each problem costs.

industry.headline: We'll benchmark you against your sector.
industry.body: As we accumulate more audits in your category, we surface where you're an outlier — better or worse than peers.

conversion_model.headline: We know which journey to instrument now.
conversion_model.body: Vestigio will watch the path up to this conversion point as the most critical leg. Any leak there surfaces first.

ticket.headline: Estimates will fit your volume properly.
ticket.body: Combining average ticket with revenue, we calibrate what each missed conversion is worth. Stops estimates from being too big or too small.

root_cause_descriptions.trust_failure_at_checkout: Buyers reach the moment of payment but trust signals around them weaken instead of strengthen. Off-domain handoffs, unrecognized payment providers, and missing security cues at the wrong moment cause buyers to back out — exactly when conversion is closest. The buyer wanted to pay; the experience scared them off.
root_cause_descriptions.fragmented_conversion_path: Your purchase flow is split across multiple domains or requires too many steps. Every transition between surfaces is a fresh chance for the buyer to drop. The path that should feel like one continuous experience feels like a relay race with handoffs at every step.
root_cause_descriptions.friction_barrier_on_path: Technical obstacles on the path from intent to purchase — slow pages, broken or excessively long forms, redundant redirects — directly suppress conversion. Buyers don't push through friction; they bounce. Every step that asks too much is a step that loses revenue.
root_cause_descriptions.measurement_blindspot: The pages that generate revenue aren't being measured. Without analytics on the commerce path, every other improvement is guesswork — you can't optimize what you can't see. Decisions get made on intuition instead of data, and the leaks stay invisible.
root_cause_descriptions.policy_deficiency: Required consumer policies (privacy, terms, returns) are missing, incomplete, or buried where buyers can't find them. This creates legal risk, breaks trust at the purchase moment, and blocks paid traffic acceptance on platforms that require policy presence before serving ads.
root_cause_descriptions.active_revenue_leakage: Revenue is escaping right now through specific broken paths — dead checkout flows, broken forms, missing conversion routes. This isn't a hypothetical risk; it's money leaving today. Every cycle this remains is direct revenue loss with a quantifiable dollar tag.
root_cause_descriptions.weak_conversion_signal: Visitors land on your commercial pages but cannot see how to buy from you. The primary call-to-action is missing, unclear, or competing with louder secondary actions. Even buyers who arrived ready to convert can't find the path — and they leave.
root_cause_descriptions.support_gap: Customers can't reach you when something goes wrong. Hidden contact methods, missing chat, no phone or email near the purchase moment — buyers who can't reach support file disputes instead. Every chargeback is a failed conversation that should have been a support ticket.
root_cause_descriptions.expectation_failure: Buyers complete the purchase without knowing exactly what they bought, when it ships, or how to return it. The mismatch between expectation and reality is the precondition for chargebacks. Confirmation, transparency, and brand continuity at checkout aren't optional — they're the contract that prevents disputes.
root_cause_descriptions.dispute_defenses_absent: The basics that stop buyers from filing chargebacks are missing. Your return policy is unclear, support is hard to reach, buyers don't know exactly what they bought, and the payment page doesn't even look like your brand. Any one of these on its own causes disputes. You have all of them at once — and every dispute costs you money plus a fee.
root_cause_descriptions.channel_integrity_compromise: Outside scripts are running on your payment pages, and your forms send data to places you don't recognize. This opens the door for attackers to steal buyer information or redirect payments. Your store's safety depends on code you don't fully control — and your buyers pay the price if anything breaks.
root_cause_descriptions.commerce_operations_exposed: The admin pages, setup files, and internal tools you use to run your store are sitting out in the open where anyone can find them. Someone who stumbles on them can change your prices, break your checkout, or copy out your customer data. You think these tools are private. They're not.
root_cause_descriptions.weak_channel_posture: When buyers land on your site, their browser flags it as unsafe — missing padlock, security warnings, or "Not Secure" labels. Cautious buyers leave before they even read your offer. Their browser is telling them something is wrong, and they trust the browser more than they trust you.
root_cause_descriptions.commerce_abuse_exposure: Automated scripts are exploiting your discount codes, refund flow, and pricing rules to take money out of your business. They guess coupon codes, trigger fake refunds, and do it faster than you can notice. By the time it shows up in your numbers, you've already paid for it.
root_cause_descriptions.untracked_purchase_paths: Some of your buyers are paying through checkout paths you didn't intend to track or protect. You can't see the revenue they bring in, you can't tell if they're being abused, and you may not even know they exist. Money is moving through your store in places you're not watching.
root_cause_descriptions.runtime_commerce_fragility: Your store works when you test it, but breaks when real buyers open it. Checkout pages load too slowly. Landing pages stall. Buyers reach "Pay" before the button is ready and click nothing. These failures only show up for real buyers on real devices — and every one of them is a sale you lost without knowing why.
root_cause_descriptions.third_party_dependency_risk: Critical buyer experience — payment, trust badges, support widgets, measurement — depends on external services that fail, load late, or add too much weight. When a third party hiccups, your conversion path breaks with it. Reliability you don't control is reliability you can't promise to your buyer.
root_cause_descriptions.saas_activation_barrier: New trial users can't reach the moment where the product proves its value. High-complexity onboarding, missing next-step guidance, or a disconnect between landing promise and in-app reality kills trial-to-paid conversion before users see what they're paying for. They sign up curious and leave unconvinced.
root_cause_descriptions.saas_product_experience_gap: Once inside the product, users can't discover what it does for them. Empty states without guidance, buried features, and complex navigation prevent the activation moment. The product is capable; the experience hides that capability. Users churn for products that aren't actually better — just better at showing their value.
root_cause_descriptions.saas_expansion_blocked: Users who would happily pay for more cannot find the upgrade path or evaluate it. Hidden pricing, missing upgrade CTAs, or prompts shown without value context cap revenue at the current tier even when willingness-to-pay is higher. Money is leaving on the table because the path to give it to you isn't visible.
root_cause_descriptions.brand_inconsistent_in_previews: When your business shows up in Google, on social media, or inside AI assistants like ChatGPT, it looks different every time — and often weak. Missing titles, thin descriptions, and mismatched details shape buyers' first impression before they ever visit your site. You lose trust before you get a chance to earn it.
root_cause_descriptions.commerce_pages_invisible_to_search: Google and AI assistants like ChatGPT can't properly see your product and pricing pages. They're missing the signals search engines look for, so when a buyer searches for exactly what you sell, your competitors show up and you don't. Every search you don't appear in is a buyer who was ready to pay — and found someone else.
root_cause_descriptions.brand_impersonation_exposure: Your brand is being actively impersonated. Lookalike domains, typosquats, phishing surfaces, or fragmented brand variants intercept traffic, run fraudulent transactions, or capture mistyped visits. Every intercepted buyer is direct revenue you earned but never saw — and every fraudulent transaction damages the brand trust you spent years building.
root_cause_descriptions.behavioral_hesitation_at_commitment: Your buyers get to the payment step, then stop. They click around looking for something that will make them feel okay about paying — then they leave. They came to buy. Something made them doubt at the last moment. You can see it happen, and you can fix it.
root_cause_descriptions.behavioral_path_disconnection: Your checkout looks fine when you walk through it, but real buyers get stuck. They land on steps and never move forward. They see the "Buy" button but never click it. Mobile buyers hit dead ends. You only see it when you watch what actual visitors do — which is exactly what Vestigio is doing.
root_cause_descriptions.behavioral_value_justification_gap: Buyers look at your price, then bounce back to your features page, then to pricing again, then leave. They're trying to decide if it's worth it — and nothing on the page tells them clearly enough. They didn't leave because it costs too much. They left because you never made the case that it's worth the cost.
root_cause_descriptions.behavioral_trust_failure_at_input: Your forms ask buyers for card numbers, personal details, and contact info — but nothing on the page reassures them it's safe to share. They reach the field, hesitate, and walk away instead of typing. The moment you ask for something sensitive, buyers need to feel safe. Right now they don't.
root_cause_descriptions.security_posture_inadequate: Buyers see warning signs the moment they land on your site. The padlock is gone, the browser says "Not Secure," and payment forms quietly stop working. Buyers click "Pay" and nothing happens. At the same time, internal admin pages are sitting in the open, one step away from a full breach. This is costing you sales every single day — and setting you up for a much bigger disaster.
root_cause_descriptions.behavioral_first_session_failure: First-time visitors arrive with intent but stall before reaching any revenue milestone. Session data shows new users hitting trust barriers, missing CTAs, or encountering dead-end flows within their first interaction. The first session is the highest-leverage conversion window — when it fails, the buyer rarely returns. Every first-session stall is a permanent loss of the cheapest conversion you'll ever get.
root_cause_descriptions.behavioral_action_value_misalignment: Visitors are busy clicking around your site — just not on the things that lead to a sale. The pages that make you money get the least attention. Your buyers' eyes are in one place, and your "Buy" button is in another. You need to move the sale to where the attention already is.
root_cause_descriptions.paid_acquisition_waste: Every time someone clicks your ad, you pay. But your landing pages are slow, the trust cues are missing, and mobile makes it worse — so most of those expensive visitors never buy. You're paying full price for half the result, and the more you spend on ads, the more you waste.
root_cause_descriptions.mobile_conversion_failure: Mobile visitors encounter conversion barriers that desktop users never see — broken CTAs, form friction, timing gaps between intent and action. The mobile experience systematically underperforms against the same offer, same traffic quality, same buyer intent. Since mobile is typically the majority of traffic, this gap represents the single largest segment-level revenue loss most sites carry.
root_cause_descriptions.behavioral_friction_tax: No single page on your site is broken — but buyers have to work too hard at every step. They go back and forth between pages, re-enter checkout, and get tired of deciding. The effort adds up until they give up. Each page is fine on its own; together they ask more of buyers than they're willing to give.
root_cause_descriptions.behavioral_trust_revenue_gap: Your buyers are ready to pay — then they check your returns policy, look for a support contact, scan for trust badges, and leave anyway. They weren't hunting for reasons to say no. They were looking for one more reason to say yes, and they didn't find it.
root_cause_descriptions.behavioral_path_inefficiency: Buyers arrive ready to buy, but your site makes them walk through too many pages before they can. By the time they reach "Pay," the interest that brought them has faded. Shorter paths turn intent into sales. Longer paths let it slip away.
root_cause_descriptions.copy_strategy_gap: The words on your site — testimonials, error messages, onboarding steps, buttons — aren't saying what buyers actually need to hear. Your testimonials are generic. Your form errors read like computer code. Your onboarding never gives the new user a quick win. At every moment, buyers have a real question in their head, and your copy answers the wrong one.
root_cause_descriptions.vertical_fashion_purchase_barriers: Fashion buyers need to feel confident the product will fit and look right before they spend. Without size guidance, enough photos to judge the piece, and a visible return policy on the product page, they assume the worst and leave. The ones who buy anyway return at 3x the rate — costing you triple shipping on every uncertain purchase.
root_cause_descriptions.vertical_saas_evaluation_friction: Software buyers evaluate before they commit budget. When they can't try the product, can't see it works with their existing tools, and can't tell if it's actively maintained, they move on to a competitor who makes evaluation easy. The sale doesn't go to the best product — it goes to the one that removes doubt fastest.
root_cause_descriptions.vertical_food_ordering_friction: Hungry customers make fast decisions. If they can't see the menu instantly, can't tell whether you deliver to them, or can't estimate when food arrives, they order from the next restaurant in the list. Every extra second of uncertainty is an order lost to a competitor who answers those questions upfront.
root_cause_descriptions.vertical_health_trust_deficit: Health and beauty buyers put products on or in their bodies. Without visible ingredients, clinical backing, and proof of real results from real people, the perceived risk exceeds the purchase price. They won't gamble their health on a product page that doesn't prove safety and efficacy.
root_cause_descriptions.vertical_education_commitment_anxiety: Education buyers commit time, not just money. When they can't see the curriculum, don't know how long it takes, can't preview the content quality, and aren't sure they'll get recognized credentials, the perceived risk of wasted months outweighs the course price. They need proof of outcome before they'll commit.
root_cause_descriptions.vertical_b2b_internal_justification: B2B buyers don't decide alone — they have to convince their boss, their finance team, or a procurement committee. Without case studies with real metrics, a clear methodology, and signals that you serve companies their size, they can't build the internal business case. The sale dies in a Slack thread because your buyer had no ammunition.
root_cause_descriptions.first_impression_failure: Visitors land on your homepage and can't tell what you do, who it's for, or why they should care — within the 5 seconds that determine whether they stay or leave. The headline talks about what you built, not what the buyer gets. The screen is packed with competing messages. The first action is buried below the fold. And your copy sounds like every other competitor in the market. Every visitor who leaves in the first 10 seconds is someone you paid to bring but failed to hold. The first impression is the most expensive failure because it happens to 100% of your traffic.
root_cause_descriptions.consideration_friction: Visitors who survived the first impression try to learn more — but the experience works against them. There's no evidence that real customers exist. Support pages are dead ends with no path back to buying. Features are listed without explaining what they mean for the buyer. Nothing positions your product against alternatives. And your FAQ answers the questions you want to answer, not the ones buyers are actually asking. Each barrier erodes the confidence your first impression built.
root_cause_descriptions.decision_moment_anxiety: Buyers arrive at the pricing page or checkout with intent to pay — and everything amplifies their doubt instead of reducing it. Prices appear without ROI context, so buyers see cost instead of investment. The visual identity breaks at the payment moment. Accepted payment methods aren't visible. Your guarantee exists but is hidden when buyers need it most. And nothing tells the buyer why acting today is better than tomorrow.
root_cause_descriptions.post_purchase_abandonment: New customers complete the purchase but can't find value fast enough to justify staying. There's no clear path to their first result. The support page exists but doesn't promise response time. And no page explains billing cycle, cancellation, or data retention. The gap between paying and getting value is where retention dies.
root_cause_descriptions.expansion_ceiling: Your revenue depends entirely on bringing new customers because existing ones have no way to spend more. Higher plan features are listed without explaining their business value. No mechanism exists for satisfied customers to bring others. And customer success stories happen but are never captured as social proof. Without expansion revenue, your business runs on a treadmill — running faster just to stay in place.

strong_cta_clarity.title: Customers know exactly how to buy
strong_cta_clarity.description: Your buy button is clear and there is no competing call-to-action stealing attention. Visitors do not have to think twice about how to purchase.

trust_continuity_good.title: Customers stay on your domain through checkout
trust_continuity_good.description: The buyer never gets bounced to a strange domain during payment. The whole flow happens where they expect it — no surprise redirects, no broken trust.

policy_coverage_complete.title: Your policies are in place and easy to find
policy_coverage_complete.description: Privacy, terms, and refund policies are visible and accessible. Buyers can find what they need before clicking buy — and that builds trust.

low_friction_checkout.title: Your purchase path runs clean
low_friction_checkout.description: No broken forms, no redirect chains, no slow pages between cart and confirmation. The buyer who wants to buy can actually buy.

measurement_covered.title: You can see what is happening in your funnel
measurement_covered.description: Analytics are in place across the buying path. You can track where buyers come from, where they convert, and where they drop off.

support_reachable.title: Customers can find you when they have a problem
support_reachable.description: Contact options are visible and reachable. When something goes wrong, the buyer talks to you instead of opening a chargeback.

smooth_activation.title: New users can get to value fast
smooth_activation.description: Your onboarding shows clear next steps and does not pile complexity on day one. Users get to the "aha" moment without getting stuck.

navigation_clean.title: Your app is easy to navigate
navigation_clean.description: Users can find features without getting lost in menus. Nothing is buried behind layers of nesting or hidden behind confusing labels.

upgrade_path_visible.title: Users can see how to upgrade and why
upgrade_path_visible.description: Upgrade options are visible and explain what the user gets. People who want to pay you more can actually find the path to do so.

empty_states_guided.title: New users are not left staring at blank screens
empty_states_guided.description: Empty screens guide the user toward a first action — sample data, suggestions, or clear next steps. No one opens your app and feels lost.

parametric_titles.sensitive_input_abandonment: Users abandon after interacting with {field_kind} input
parametric_titles.surface_oscillation_before_dropoff: Back-and-forth between {surfaceA} and {surfaceB} before dropoff due to unresolved decision friction
