inference_effects.size_guide_missing: Buyers unsure about sizing don't buy — and those who gamble return 52% of the time, costing 3x shipping in reverse logistics
inference_effects.product_images_insufficient: Buyers who can't see the product from all angles don't trust the purchase — each missing photo reduces add-to-cart by up to 20%
inference_effects.no_urgency_indicators: With no reason to decide now, buyers close the tab promising to come back later — and 70% never do
inference_effects.cross_sell_absent: Each order carries just one product when it could carry two or three — average order value stays 30-40% below potential
inference_effects.return_policy_not_on_product: Buyers who don't know they can return assume they can't — and buy from the competitor who makes that clear on the product page
inference_effects.no_free_trial_offered: Without trying first, software buyers don't feel confident committing budget — 60% lower conversion compared to companies offering a trial
inference_effects.integration_ecosystem_invisible: Software buyers need to know it works with their existing tools — without that information, they assume it doesn't and move to a competitor
inference_effects.changelog_stale_or_missing: A product with no recent updates looks abandoned — software buyers avoid tools that might disappear tomorrow
inference_effects.annual_discount_not_highlighted: Customers who would pay 12 months upfront pay monthly because the annual advantage isn't visible — you lose predictability and upfront revenue
inference_effects.no_product_screenshot_visible: Software buyers who can't see inside the product imagine the worst — and 45% leave before creating an account
inference_effects.menu_requires_signup: Hungry customers won't create an account to see the menu — they open the next restaurant on Google in 3 seconds and never come back
inference_effects.no_food_photos: Food is a visual decision — restaurants without dish photos lose up to 35% of orders to competitors who show what they serve
inference_effects.delivery_area_unclear: Customers who don't know if you deliver to them won't spend time finding out — they go straight to the app that shows the delivery area on a map
inference_effects.delivery_time_not_shown: Hungry customers want to know when they'll eat — without an estimated time, they pick whoever promises 30 minutes even if the food is worse
inference_effects.allergen_info_missing: People with food allergies simply won't order from a restaurant that doesn't list allergens — and they influence the entire group's decision
inference_effects.ingredients_not_listed: Health and beauty buyers who don't see ingredients assume there's something to hide — the distrust kills conversion before price does
inference_effects.no_clinical_endorsement: Health products without clinical backing compete on price alone — those with scientific endorsement charge more and sell more
inference_effects.usage_instructions_absent: Buyers who don't know how to use the product imagine it's complicated — and prefer buying from the competitor who explains everything on the page
inference_effects.subscription_not_offered: Every manual reorder is a chance for the customer to forget or switch brands — subscriptions guarantee predictable revenue and cut retention costs
inference_effects.no_results_evidence: Beauty or health products without proof of results become empty promises — buyers compare against competitors who show before and after
inference_effects.curriculum_not_visible: Students who don't know what they'll learn won't risk the money — they buy from the course that shows exactly each module and outcome
inference_effects.instructor_credentials_missing: Students who don't know who's teaching don't trust the content — instructor credentials are the first decision filter in online education
inference_effects.completion_certificate_absent: Without a certificate, the course loses value for students who need to show results to their boss or the market — they go to the competitor that certifies
inference_effects.time_commitment_unclear: Students who don't know if they'll need 2 hours or 200 hours don't enroll — time uncertainty weighs more than the course price
inference_effects.no_sample_content: Students who can't preview a lesson before paying compare only on price and reviews — a free sample converts 3x more than any copy
inference_effects.no_case_study_with_metrics: B2B buyers need numbers to convince their boss — without a case study with metrics, they can't build the internal justification and the sale stalls
inference_effects.methodology_not_explained: Corporate buyers who don't understand how you work don't trust the result — they need to visualize the process before approving the budget
inference_effects.enterprise_signals_missing: Large companies that don't see signs you serve their size assume you're too small — and don't even reach out to find out
inference_effects.contact_form_excessive_fields: Each extra field in the contact form reduces completion by 10-15% — a long form scares off the lead before the first conversation
inference_effects.response_time_not_promised: Buyers who don't know when they'll hear back assume you're slow — and send the same request to the competitor who promises a response within 24 hours
inference_effects.meta_promise_content_mismatch: Organic visitors arrive expecting what Google showed them — when the page delivers something else, they bounce in seconds and the qualified click is wasted forever
inference_effects.pricing_terms_contradictory: Buyers who notice different prices on different pages lose trust immediately — price inconsistency signals either negligence or deception, and both kill the sale
inference_effects.urgency_claim_unverifiable: Savvy buyers recognize permanently displayed urgency as manipulation — once they see the trick, they distrust everything else on the page and never return
inference_effects.value_prop_diluted_by_navigation: Visitors who can't understand what you do in 5 seconds leave — each competing value proposition above the fold dilutes all others and converts nobody
inference_effects.checkout_copy_creates_anxiety: Anxiety-inducing text at the payment moment triggers immediate abandonment — buyers who were ready to pay close the tab instead of risking the unknown
inference_effects.faq_answers_wrong_questions: A FAQ that ignores buying objections wastes its conversion potential — visitors who came for reassurance leave with more doubt, not less
inference_effects.testimonials_feel_fabricated: Fabricated-looking testimonials damage trust more than having none at all — buyers who spot the pattern assume everything on the page is equally dishonest
inference_effects.staging_environment_publicly_accessible: Exposed staging environments let attackers find vulnerabilities in non-hardened code and access real customer test data — one breach away from a compliance violation and customer trust collapse
inference_effects.admin_panel_exposed_to_internet: Anyone can attempt credential stuffing or brute force against your admin panel — a single compromised account means total operational control by an attacker
inference_effects.subdomain_brand_visual_fragmentation: Customers navigating between your subdomains experience visual discontinuity — trust erodes at each surface transition, increasing bounce rate between properties
inference_effects.app_subdomain_disconnected_from_site: Existing customers who can't find how to log in from your site call support or simply churn — support costs rise while retention drops silently
inference_effects.whatsapp_channel_disconnected: Your WhatsApp sales channel exists but nobody can find it — pre-purchase questions go unanswered and buyers with intent leave without converting
inference_effects.multiple_payment_subdomains_fragmenting_: Buyer is redirected to an unfamiliar domain at the most sensitive moment — triggers fraud anxiety and suppresses conversion at the payment step
inference_effects.social_proof_ineffective: Generic testimonials without names, companies, or specific results fail to convince — buyers treat them as filler and trust erodes instead of building
inference_effects.copy_cross_page_inconsistent: Contradictory messaging across pages confuses buyers and breaks trust — if your homepage says one thing and your pricing page says another, buyers assume negligence
inference_effects.social_proof_expired: Social proof with old dates or discontinued metrics signals that the business stopped growing — buyers assume the product peaked and moved on
inference_effects.commercial_page_stale: Outdated content on commercial pages tells buyers the business is neglected — they assume the product is too and go to a competitor that looks alive
inference_effects.pricing_page_outdated: Expired offers and stale competitive claims on the pricing page undermine credibility — buyers who notice outdated info distrust all other claims
inference_effects.content_decay_progression: Progressive content staleness across audit cycles indicates systematic neglect — each cycle the business looks less maintained to returning visitors
inference_effects.value_proposition_buried: When the core value proposition is invisible above the fold, visitors leave in seconds — they never scroll far enough to discover what you actually do
inference_effects.objection_unaddressed: Unanswered buyer objections become reasons to leave — every common concern left unaddressed on commercial pages loses a percentage of ready buyers
inference_effects.cta_competing_or_unclear: When multiple CTAs compete for attention, buyers choose none — decision paralysis from too many options suppresses conversion more than having no CTA at all
inference_effects.trust_copy_absent_at_decision: The absence of trust language at the payment moment triggers last-second hesitation — buyers who were ready to pay close the tab looking for reassurance they never find
inference_effects.copy_funnel_misalignment: Copy that doesn't match the funnel stage creates friction — awareness-stage messaging on a checkout page confuses, while hard-sell copy on a blog post repels
inference_effects.localization_persuasion_lost: Translated pages that read like machine output lose the persuasive power of the original — international visitors bounce because the copy feels impersonal and mechanical
inference_effects.micro_copy_friction_high: Confusing form labels, vague button text, and unhelpful error messages create micro-frustrations that accumulate into abandonment
inference_effects.seo_conversion_conflict: Pages stuffed with keywords for search engines read poorly for humans — organic visitors arrive but bounce because the content doesn't speak to their buying intent
inference_effects.copy_stale_references: Outdated references to old years, discontinued features, or past events signal neglect — buyers who notice assume the whole business is equally unmaintained
inference_effects.hero_outcome_absent: Visitors can't see what they get within 5 seconds — bounce rate spikes because the headline speaks to you, not to them. Every visitor who leaves in the first 10 seconds is someone you paid to bring but failed to hold
inference_effects.cognitive_load_first_screen: Too many messages competing for attention in the first screen — visitors freeze, can't prioritize, and leave before scrolling. The page that should convert the most traffic converts the least
inference_effects.primary_cta_delayed: Visitors with purchase intent can't find the first action above the fold — every pixel of scroll reduces the chance they'll ever click. Mobile makes it worse because fold arrives faster
inference_effects.specificity_deficit: When your text sounds identical to every competitor, buyers have no reason to choose you — generic copy converts at a fraction of specific, outcome-driven copy
inference_effects.proof_of_work_missing: Buyers evaluating your product find zero proof that anyone bought and succeeded — without evidence, they assume your claims are aspirational and choose a competitor with case studies
inference_effects.navigation_dead_ends: Visitors who land on help or support pages hit a dead end — without a path back to buying, they leave instead of returning to the purchase flow
inference_effects.page_depth_before_conversion: Each additional click between landing and payment loses a percentage of buyers — long paths kill intent before it reaches the checkout
inference_effects.feature_benefit_disconnect: Technical feature lists mean nothing to buyers who think in outcomes — they can't connect your specs to their problems, so they leave unconvinced
inference_effects.comparison_absent: Buyers comparing options find nothing that positions your product — they open a competitor tab, find a comparison page there, and never come back
inference_effects.objection_echo_chamber: Your FAQ answers the questions you wish buyers would ask, not the ones they actually have — unanswered objections become reasons to leave
inference_effects.social_channels_decorative: Buyers who click social links expecting activity find abandoned profiles — the signal says the business is dormant, and dormant businesses don't get orders
inference_effects.pricing_without_context: Buyers see a number without understanding what it buys them — cost without context triggers sticker shock even when the price is fair
inference_effects.checkout_identity_break: When the checkout looks different from the rest of the site, buyers feel they've been handed off to a stranger — trust drops at the exact moment it matters most
inference_effects.payment_options_invisible: Buyers who don't see their preferred payment method before entering checkout assume it's not accepted — they leave rather than discover the option too late
inference_effects.guarantee_invisible_at_decision: Your guarantee — the strongest objection killer — is buried on a page nobody visits at the moment they decide to buy
inference_effects.urgency_mechanics_absent: Without a real reason to act now, buyers bookmark and never return — 70% of deferred purchase decisions are permanently lost
inference_effects.first_value_path_unclear: New customers who can't find their first win within the first session disengage permanently — the gap between paying and getting value is where retention dies
inference_effects.support_response_expectation_gap: Customers with problems don't know when help will arrive — uncertainty amplifies frustration, accelerates churn, and pushes dissatisfied buyers toward chargebacks
inference_effects.billing_transparency_absent: Customers who don't understand their billing feel trapped — surprise charges trigger chargebacks and the absence of clear cancellation info accelerates churn
inference_effects.upgrade_value_gap: Customers ready to upgrade can't justify the cost because features are listed without outcomes — they stay on the current plan and your ARPU stagnates
inference_effects.referral_path_nonexistent: Happy customers have no easy way to bring others — word-of-mouth happens in conversation but never converts to tracked acquisition
inference_effects.success_story_feedback_loop_broken: Real customer wins happen every day but are never turned into testimonials or case studies — your best marketing asset evaporates unused
inference_effects.tone_shift_across_journey: When each page sounds like a different company, buyers lose trust cumulatively — the inconsistency signals disorganization and makes every claim less credible
inference_effects.mobile_journey_friction_compound: Each individual mobile issue is tolerable but together they create an unbearable experience — tiny taps, slow loads, and layout shifts multiply into total abandonment
inference_effects.trust_gradient_inverted: Trust badges and reassurance pile up on the homepage but vanish on checkout and pricing pages — the gradient is backwards, strongest where buyers already trust and absent where they doubt

actions.default_primary: Review findings and address highest severity issues first.
actions.default_verification: Re-run analysis after changes to confirm resolution.

scale_readiness.block_primary: Do not scale traffic until critical issues are resolved.
scale_readiness.block_fix_checkout: Fix checkout integrity: ensure checkout flow stays on-domain or uses a verified provider.
scale_readiness.block_resolve_trust: Resolve trust boundary issues: reduce off-domain handoffs or verify external providers.
scale_readiness.block_add_policies: Add missing policies: privacy, terms, and refund policies are required for commercial sites.
scale_readiness.block_verify_full: Re-run full analysis after fixes to confirm readiness.
scale_readiness.block_verify_checkout: Verify checkout flow manually before scaling.
scale_readiness.fix_primary: Address high-priority issues before increasing traffic significantly.
scale_readiness.fix_improve_policy: Improve policy coverage to reduce compliance risk.
scale_readiness.fix_install_analytics: Install analytics tools to enable proper attribution and optimization.
scale_readiness.fix_strengthen_revenue: Strengthen revenue path: reduce redirects and friction in the conversion flow.
scale_readiness.optimize_primary: Traffic can be scaled. Monitor for regressions.
scale_readiness.optimize_measurement: Consider improving measurement coverage for better optimization.
scale_readiness.optimize_verify: Schedule periodic re-analysis to detect regressions.

revenue_integrity.compound_funnel_triple: CROSS-PACK: Revenue leaks at top (cart) + middle (failed payments) + bottom (churn). Fixing only cart while feeding the middle/bottom leaks wastes acquisition. Order: (1) failed payments first (Stripe Smart Retries + dunning = 30-50% recovery, 1 wk); (2) cancel-flow + retention (2 wks); (3) THEN attack cart with retention engine in place.
revenue_integrity.compound_pricing: CROSS-PACK: Pricing fails on both humans AND AI agents. Single rewrite covers both: (a) /pricing with exact monthly + annual prices visible without interaction, "what's included" table, recommended-plan badge; (b) /pricing.md mirroring same structure. 2-3 hours, double-layer impact.
revenue_integrity.close_leak: Close revenue leak points: fix broken forms, consolidate checkout to one domain, and ensure clear conversion entry.
revenue_integrity.consolidate: Consolidate conversion path: reduce external host fragmentation and eliminate unnecessary redirects before checkout.
revenue_integrity.restore_trust: Restore trust at checkout: add policies, verify providers, ensure brand continuity through the purchase flow.
revenue_integrity.reduce_friction: Reduce critical path friction: fix broken form actions, speed up slow pages, eliminate unnecessary redirects.
revenue_integrity.measurement_coverage: Install analytics on every commercial page (homepage, pricing, checkout, thank-you). Verify consent banner does not block primary measurement.
revenue_integrity.clarify_intent: Clarify conversion intent: one primary CTA above the fold, supporting CTAs only after the primary is visible.
revenue_integrity.language_continuity: Buyer sees language switch mid-purchase. Force the buying flow to stay in the entry language (homepage → checkout) with explicit locale handling on every redirect.
revenue_integrity.commercial_connectivity: Commercial pages (pricing, product, checkout) are not interlinked. Add direct CTA links from every commercial page to the next funnel step.
revenue_integrity.mobile_trust: Mobile buyers see less reassurance than desktop. Audit trust badges, testimonials, and policy links on mobile viewport — many are hidden below the fold or load late.
revenue_integrity.runtime_errors: Runtime JS errors are breaking purchase OR analytics on commercial pages. Open browser DevTools on /checkout (mobile + desktop) and triage every red console error.
revenue_integrity.checkout_latency: Checkout API calls are slow or failing. Audit the slowest 3 endpoints in /checkout — most common culprits: under-scaled payment provider, third-party widgets blocking submission.
revenue_integrity.deps_sequencing: Buyer can click Buy before payment libs or analytics finish loading. Defer the CTA enable until critical deps are ready.
revenue_integrity.secondary_flows: Alternate buying flows (WhatsApp checkout, /quick-buy, embedded forms) bypass your trust + measurement stack. Bring them under the same governance as the main flow.
revenue_integrity.dead_cta: Primary CTA is visible but nobody clicks. Re-test copy ("Get Started" vs "Start free trial"), increase button contrast, and remove competing actions in the same fold.
revenue_integrity.hesitation_trust: Buyers hesitate at checkout because trust signals are missing AT the decision moment. Add testimonials, guarantees, and security badges directly adjacent to the Buy button.
revenue_integrity.pricing_clarity: Pricing page is causing hesitation — too many options, unclear value, or hidden numbers. Cap visible plans at 3, lead with the recommended one, and put exact price above the fold.
revenue_integrity.sensitive_input: Buyers abandon at sensitive fields (CPF, card, address). Add a brief why-we-need-this note next to the field + visible security indicator (lock icon, HTTPS badge).
revenue_integrity.form_friction: Forms have too many fields or trigger retries. Cut to email-only first, postpone non-essential fields to onboarding, and make error messages specific (field-level inline, not toast).
revenue_integrity.retry_friction: Buyers retry the final step before giving up. Audit submit-button state (disabled timing, double-click protection, error toast clarity).
revenue_integrity.cta_timing: Primary CTA renders late on commercial pages. Inline-load the button HTML so it is visible at first paint; defer heavy JS that powers it.
revenue_integrity.abandon_feedback: Buyers start checkout, see no progress indicator, and leave. Add a 3-step progress bar (cart → details → payment) at the top of /checkout.
revenue_integrity.oscillation: Buyers bounce between pages before giving up (e.g. checkout ↔ FAQ). Embed the top 3 buying objections directly on /checkout — they should not have to leave to find answers.
revenue_integrity.stalled_step: A funnel step gets traffic but does not advance buyers. Treat as a UX/copy bug — run a 5-user usability test focused on that step.
revenue_integrity.cta_engagement: CTAs are seen but not clicked. Test outcome-focused copy ("Get my conversion audit") vs. action-focused ("Submit"). Outcome copy converts ~30% better.
revenue_integrity.social_proof: Social proof is generic or invisible where buyers decide. Replace stock testimonials with named customers + specific outcome numbers; place trust badges directly adjacent to the buy button.
revenue_integrity.form_errors: Form error messages are unhelpful. Replace "Invalid input" with field-specific guidance ("Phone must be 10 digits, no dashes").
revenue_integrity.checkout_trust_copy: Checkout copy lacks reassurance language. Add: payment encryption mention, money-back guarantee, cancellation policy link — all visible without scrolling.
revenue_integrity.product_copy: Product page copy is generic. Replace marketing slogans with: who this is for, what specific problem it solves, the one outcome a buyer will get.
revenue_integrity.ad_dead_link: A paid ad creative is sending traffic to a dead page (404 or wrong URL). Audit live ads + their destinations weekly — this is pure burnt spend.
revenue_integrity.ad_landing_trust: Ad clicks land on pages that look untrustworthy or disconnected from the ad promise. Add the ad headline verbatim to the landing H1, match imagery, and verify the page loads in <2s on mobile.
revenue_integrity.ad_form_friction: Ads send buyers to forms that are too long. Cut paid landing forms to email-only or phone-only; collect the rest in the follow-up.
revenue_integrity.ad_mobile_degraded: Paid mobile traffic hits a degraded checkout. Audit /checkout on a real mobile device (not Chrome DevTools) and verify CTA tap target, keyboard type per field, autofill.
revenue_integrity.ad_message_match: Ad promises X but landing page delivers Y. Each ad needs a 1-to-1 matched landing page (or component variation) with the same headline, image, and offer.
revenue_integrity.ad_attribution: Ads run without conversion tracking. Wire conversion pixel events (purchase, signup, lead) and verify in the platform debug tool before scaling spend.
revenue_integrity.shopify_abandonment: Shopify shows high cart abandonment. Audit checkout for: required-account barrier, surprise shipping cost, missing payment options. Enable Shopify Shop Pay if not on already.
revenue_integrity.repeat_purchase: Buyers do not come back. Set up a post-purchase email at day 30 + day 60 with restock reminder or related product; offer a 10% returning-customer code.
revenue_integrity.dead_weight: Products listed but not selling in 30 days are eating attention and SKU complexity. Delist or bundle them with bestsellers.
revenue_integrity.trust_cliff: Brand presentation collapses at the payment step. Audit the visual continuity from homepage → /pricing → /checkout — typography, color, header should match.
revenue_integrity.payment_subdomains: Payment flow hops between subdomains (pay.brand.com, secure.brand.com). Consolidate to one origin or display a clear "you are still on Brand.com" indicator.
revenue_integrity.nav_traps: Navigation steals attention from the buying flow. On /checkout, hide or minimize the global nav — just show logo + maybe a single Help link.
revenue_integrity.consent_position: Consent banner is obscuring the primary CTA. Move it to bottom or top strip, never center modal blocking the buy button.
revenue_integrity.price_visibility: Buyers see no price until they click. Surface the exact monthly/annual price on /pricing without requiring sign-up or contact form.
revenue_integrity.block_primary: Revenue is actively leaking on multiple fronts. Fix the highest-impact items before any traffic scale-up.
revenue_integrity.block_verify: Re-run revenue analysis after fixes to confirm leakage is resolved + monitor conversion rate week-over-week.
revenue_integrity.fix_primary: Revenue path has structural issues. Address before scaling ad spend.
revenue_integrity.fix_verify: Re-run analysis after implementing changes + track conversion delta in week 1-4.
revenue_integrity.optimize_primary: Revenue path is functional but has optimization opportunities.
revenue_integrity.optimize_verify: Schedule periodic revenue analysis to track improvements.
revenue_integrity.stable_primary: Revenue integrity is stable. No significant leakage detected.
revenue_integrity.stable_verify: Schedule periodic revenue analysis to detect regressions.

chargeback.block_primary: High chargeback exposure. Add refund policy, support channels, and trust signals immediately.
chargeback.block_refund_policy: Add a clear, accessible refund/return policy. This is the single most effective chargeback prevention measure.
chargeback.block_add_support: Add visible support channels (email, phone, or chat). Customers who can reach support resolve issues without disputes.
chargeback.block_clarify_pricing: Clarify pricing and add order confirmation. Expectation misalignment drives "unauthorized charge" disputes.
chargeback.block_verify_policies: Re-run analysis after adding policies and support channels.
chargeback.block_verify_checkout: Review checkout flow for brand continuity and charge clarity.
chargeback.fix_primary: Moderate chargeback risk. Strengthen policies and support before scaling.
chargeback.fix_refund: Improve refund policy clarity and accessibility.
chargeback.fix_support: Add additional support channels for better customer accessibility.
chargeback.fix_confirmation: Add order confirmation and pricing transparency.
chargeback.optimize_primary: Low chargeback risk. Minor improvements available.
chargeback.optimize_support: Consider adding more support channels for redundancy.
chargeback.optimize_communication: Improve post-purchase communication for better customer experience.
chargeback.strong_primary: Chargeback resilience is strong. Continue monitoring.

security_posture.incident_primary: You have critical security holes that buyers can see. Fix these before taking another payment.
security_posture.incident_sensitive_endpoint: Lock down or remove the admin pages, config files, and backups that are currently public.
security_posture.incident_mixed_content: Stop loading insecure content on your sale pages — every resource needs to come over HTTPS or buyers see broken padlocks.
security_posture.incident_open_redirect: Close the open redirects on your site — attackers can use them to send your customers to fake checkout pages.
security_posture.incident_security_headers: Add the basic security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options) so the browser stops flagging your site as risky.
security_posture.incident_verify_rerun: Run a new audit after the fixes so we can confirm the holes are closed.
security_posture.incident_verify_endpoints: Double-check that the previously exposed pages are no longer reachable from the open internet.
security_posture.fix_primary: Your security has gaps that will hurt you at scale. Close them before pushing more traffic.
security_posture.fix_security_headers: Tighten your security headers so the site is less exposed to injection and clickjacking attacks.
security_posture.fix_open_redirect: Fix the open redirect endpoints — they are easy to abuse.
security_posture.fix_verify_rerun: Run a new audit after the changes to confirm they took effect.
security_posture.optimize_primary: Your security holds up. There are still some hardening wins available.
security_posture.optimize_csp: Consider stricter Content-Security-Policy and Permissions-Policy headers for extra defense.
security_posture.optimize_verify: Re-check security posture every few months to catch regressions.
security_posture.observe_primary: Your security is in good shape. No significant exposures right now.
security_posture.observe_verify: Schedule a periodic security check to keep this clean.

payment_health.incident_primary: Your payment infrastructure is actively losing revenue. Failed payments and churn require immediate intervention.
payment_health.incident_failed_payments: Activate card updater and smart retry in Stripe. Send dunning emails on first failure with a direct link to update payment method.
payment_health.incident_churn: Implement cancellation survey and retention offers (pause, downgrade, extension) before final cancellation.
payment_health.incident_diversity: Add a secondary payment gateway with automatic failover to prevent single-point-of-failure outages.
payment_health.incident_verify: Re-pull Stripe data after 30 days to confirm failed payment rate dropped below 5%.
payment_health.fix_primary: Payment health has issues that will compound as you grow. Fix before scaling subscriber acquisition.
payment_health.fix_failed_payments: Configure dunning automation with progressive retry and customer notification.
payment_health.fix_churn: Analyze churn cohorts by tenure to identify the month where most subscribers cancel.
payment_health.fix_verify: Re-pull Stripe data after implementing changes to track improvement.
payment_health.optimize_primary: Payment health is acceptable but has room for improvement.
payment_health.optimize_dunning: Consider adding grace periods before access suspension on failed payments.
payment_health.optimize_diversity: Consider adding a backup payment gateway for resilience.
payment_health.optimize_verify: Monitor payment health metrics monthly via Stripe integration.
payment_health.stable_primary: Payment health is stable. Failed payment and churn rates are within acceptable ranges.
payment_health.stable_verify: Continue monitoring payment health through Stripe integration.

discoverability.claim_g2: Claim your G2 listing and complete the profile (logo, description, screenshots, pricing tier).
discoverability.compound_invisible_unclear: CROSS-PACK: Double leak — buyers shopping your category can't find you, and when the rare visitor lands, the value prop is buried. Sequence: clarify value prop above the fold FIRST (1 week), then drive category-intent SEO/SEM. Skipping step 1 wastes every paid click.
discoverability.compound_ai_invisibility: CROSS-PACK: AI agents comparing products can't parse you on llms.txt + Product schema + machine-readable pricing. Single 30-minute action covers all three. Highest 2026 lift in AI-mediated buying.
discoverability.compound_category_authority: CROSS-PACK: Bottom-of-stack visibility — invisible in category SERP AND no Wikipedia authority. AI assistants preferentially cite brands with both. Two parallel moves: (1) "best <category>" listicle on own domain; (2) Wikipedia article via WP:AfC with independent press refs.
discoverability.publish_llms_txt: Publish /llms.txt at the site root with a one-page summary of what the product does, who it is for, and links to /pricing + /docs. 15-minute action with measurable AI Overview citation lift in 30-60 days.
discoverability.publish_pricing_md: Publish /pricing.md (or /pricing.txt) at the site root with plan names, monthly prices, key limits, and what is included per tier. AI agents comparing tools programmatically depend on parseable pricing.
discoverability.unblock_ai_bots: Edit robots.txt to allow GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot, Google-Extended, and Bingbot. Each blocked crawler is a platform that physically cannot cite the brand.
discoverability.add_product_schema: Add Product (or SoftwareApplication) + Offer JSON-LD to /pricing first, then Organization on homepage, then FAQPage on any page with Q&A content. AI assistants prefer schema-rich content for citation.
discoverability.improve_wikipedia: Strengthen Wikipedia presence: collect 3-5 independent press references about the brand, then either expand the existing article or submit a new article via WP:AfC. Do not author your own edits — recruit independent editors with the sourced material.
discoverability.claim_capterra: Claim Capterra/GetApp/SoftwareAdvice profiles (all owned by Gartner, single onboarding). B2B buyers research here before vendor calls.
discoverability.claim_producthunt: Submit the product to Product Hunt with a coordinated launch (community pre-warming + day-of hunter outreach). Even without #1 placement, the page becomes a persistent third-party citation asset.
discoverability.fix_branded_serp: Branded SERP fix: ensure homepage <title> includes the brand name, canonical points to root, and the H1 uses the brand verbatim. Submit a Google Search Console "site:" query with the brand to detect indexation gaps.
discoverability.reclaim_branded_serp: Competitors are outranking you on your own brand name. Publish a brand "vs alternatives" page on your own domain that owns the comparison narrative; file trademark complaints on competitor pages using your trademark in ad copy.
discoverability.reclaim_affiliate_traffic: Affiliate/review sites earn commission on your branded traffic. Build stronger branded landing pages, partner directly with the highest-volume affiliates instead of paying via networks, and pursue trademark enforcement on misleading review pages.
discoverability.own_category_query: Buyers shopping the category never see you. Publish a "best [category] [year]" listicle on your own domain, target the alternatives keyword cluster with 1,500-word landing pages, and outreach to 3-5 independent "best of" listicle authors for inclusion.
discoverability.own_vs_query: Competitors author the "<brand> vs them" pages — they shape how AI describes you. Publish fair side-by-side comparison pages on your own domain for the top 3 competitors, with criteria buyers actually use.
discoverability.hn_engagement: Tech early-adopters have never discussed you on Hacker News. Plan a "Show HN" launch with a real engineering story OR publish one deep-dive technical post that earns front-page traction. Either creates a persistent HN citation surface.
discoverability.reddit_authentic_presence: Reddit recommendation threads never mention you while buyers ask for tools in your category. Identify 2-3 active subreddits, seed authentic founder presence over 30+ days, and respond helpfully to existing recommendation threads. Avoid promotional posts — Reddit moderation penalizes them.
discoverability.expand_third_party: Claim missing third-party listings and seed reviews. AI assistants are 6.5× more likely to cite via third-party sources than via your own domain — this is structural moat.
discoverability.improve_search_representation: Commercial pages have weak search representation. Add proper <title>, meta description, and OpenGraph tags that match buying-intent queries.
discoverability.improve_social_previews: Social previews fail to communicate commercial value. Add OG image, og:title with value prop, and og:description with the offer for every commercial page.
discoverability.unify_brand_surfaces: Brand name appears inconsistently across pages (e.g., capitalization, abbreviations). Standardize across <title>, H1, OG tags, and copy.
discoverability.expose_commercial_pages: Commercial pages (pricing, product, checkout entry) may not be indexed by search engines. Verify robots.txt allows crawling, add them to the sitemap, and link from the homepage.
discoverability.incident_primary: AI assistants and search engines cannot find or recommend the brand. Buyers researching your category find competitors instead. Address visibility gaps before any paid acquisition push.
discoverability.incident_verify: Re-run the external recon audit in 30 days to confirm AI Visibility Score moved above 60.
discoverability.fix_primary: Discoverability gaps are limiting growth. Each blocked AI crawler, missing listing, or unowned comparison query is buying-intent traffic going to competitors. Fix high-leverage items first (llms.txt + schema + Wikipedia).
discoverability.fix_verify: Re-run external recon in 30-60 days and confirm AI Visibility Score improved by ≥10 points.
discoverability.optimize_primary: Core discoverability is in place — refine for AI search to compound your visibility.
discoverability.optimize_verify: Monitor AI Visibility Score quarterly; investigate any drops promptly.
discoverability.strong_primary: Discoverability is healthy. Continue feeding fresh content + structured data; monitor AI Visibility Score for regressions.
discoverability.strong_verify: Schedule quarterly external recon to catch citation losses early.

copy_alignment.incident_primary: Your copy is actively losing revenue. The messaging fails to convert high-intent visitors.
copy_alignment.incident_vp_buried: Rewrite your hero section: the value proposition is buried below the fold or hidden behind generic language.
copy_alignment.incident_cta_unclear: Consolidate competing CTAs: visitors see too many actions and choose none. Pick one primary CTA per page.
copy_alignment.incident_trust_copy: Add trust copy at the decision point: testimonials, guarantees, and security language near your checkout or signup.
copy_alignment.incident_objection: Address buyer objections on the page: missing FAQ, no risk-reversal language, unaddressed pricing concerns.
copy_alignment.incident_verify_rerun: Re-run copy analysis after rewriting to confirm alignment improves.
copy_alignment.incident_verify_conversion: Monitor conversion rate lift after copy changes to measure revenue impact.
copy_alignment.fix_primary: Your copy has structural gaps that will bleed revenue at scale. Fix before increasing ad spend.
copy_alignment.fix_funnel_mismatch: Align copy to funnel stage: awareness pages should educate, decision pages should reassure and convert.
copy_alignment.fix_social_proof: Strengthen social proof: add specific numbers, named testimonials, and logos instead of generic claims.
copy_alignment.fix_cross_page: Harmonize messaging across pages: your landing page promises one thing, but pricing and checkout say another.
copy_alignment.fix_verify: Re-run copy analysis after changes to confirm gaps are closed.
copy_alignment.optimize_primary: Copy is functional but has optimization opportunities. Refining messaging could lift conversion.
copy_alignment.optimize_social_proof: Consider upgrading social proof from generic to specific and quantified.
copy_alignment.optimize_vp: Test alternative headline framings to make the value proposition more immediate.
copy_alignment.optimize_verify: Re-check copy alignment periodically as you update pages.
copy_alignment.aligned_primary: Copy is well-aligned with commercial intent. No significant gaps detected.
copy_alignment.aligned_verify: Schedule periodic copy reviews to catch drift as products evolve.

brand_integrity.compound_reputation_ai: CROSS-PACK: Your reputation problem is actively blocking AI search citation. Schema markup + llms.txt won't fix this — AI assistants route around brands with public unresolved complaints. ORDER MATTERS: respond to outstanding reviews FIRST (1-2 weeks), then invest in AI visibility infrastructure.
brand_integrity.compound_brand_crisis: CROSS-PACK: Brand authority crisis on multiple fronts (branded SERP + competitor hijack + affiliate dominance). Three-prong response in parallel: (a) SEO/technical — fix title/canonical/schema; (b) IP enforcement — file Google Ads Trademark Complaints; (c) affiliate partnership — convert top affiliate domains from commission-takers to direct partners.
brand_integrity.respond_trustpilot_complaints: Negative Trustpilot reviews are sitting unanswered for prospects to read. Assign someone to respond within 48 hours of each new review with an empathetic acknowledgment + concrete next step. Reply to existing unanswered negatives now — even a 5-month-old reply restores credibility.
brand_integrity.trustpilot_response_cadence: Set up a Trustpilot alert that pages someone for any new review. Target sub-48h response rate above 70% — silence is the #1 reason high-intent prospects pick a competitor with similar features but visible care.
brand_integrity.reclame_aqui_recovery: Reclame Aqui flags the brand as critical — BR buyers verify before paying. Resolve pending complaints publicly (status = Resolvido), respond to every new complaint within 5 business days, and target index acima de 7/10 within 90 days.
brand_integrity.competitor_hijack_enforcement: Competitors outrank your own domain on your brand name. Publish a press kit + about page that aggressively owns brand signal. For repeat offenders running paid ads on your trademark, file Google Ads Trademark Complaints + Meta brand reports.
brand_integrity.affiliate_traffic_recovery: Affiliate/review sites earn commission on traffic that should be direct. Negotiate direct deals with the top 3 affiliate sites (better margin than network commissions), file trademark enforcement on misleading review pages, and invest in your own branded landing pages.
brand_integrity.lookalike_domain_response: A lookalike domain is competing for branded traffic. File a UDRP complaint with the relevant registrar, or buy the domain directly if it is for sale and redirects to a competitor.
brand_integrity.phishing_response: External sites are mimicking the brand — phishing exposure for customers. Submit takedown requests via Google Safe Browsing + Microsoft Defender, notify affected customers via email, and monitor for new lookalikes weekly.
brand_integrity.deceptive_surface_audit: Suspicious domains are capturing brand-intent traffic. Run brand monitoring queries weekly, file trademark complaints on confirmed bad actors, and create a "report a fake site" link on the footer.
brand_integrity.unify_brand_presence: Brand presence is diluted across name variants (.com, .io, country TLDs). Consolidate to one canonical domain with 301 redirects from variants, and unify brand spelling across all surfaces.
brand_integrity.branded_ai_overview_recover: When AI summarizes searches for your brand, it cites competitors first. Publish a definitive brand page (homepage + /about + press kit) with consistent entity signals + Wikipedia + Organization schema. AI Overview rebalances within 60-90 days.
brand_integrity.incident_primary: Brand integrity is critically compromised. Negative reputation, hijacked search, or phishing exposure are actively costing buyers and revenue. Address this before any acquisition spend.
brand_integrity.incident_verify: Re-run external recon in 30 days; confirm reputation labels improved and unanswered complaints addressed.
brand_integrity.fix_primary: Brand integrity has real gaps. Unanswered reviews, weakened SERP control, and inconsistent brand surfaces compound — fix before scaling brand awareness spend.
brand_integrity.fix_verify: Re-run external recon in 60 days and confirm reputation + SERP signals improved.
brand_integrity.optimize_primary: Brand integrity is largely intact — maintain response cadence on review platforms and watch for new lookalike threats.
brand_integrity.optimize_verify: Monthly brand-monitoring sweep + quarterly external recon.
brand_integrity.strong_primary: Brand integrity is strong. Continue monitoring third-party review platforms + lookalike domains.
brand_integrity.strong_verify: Schedule quarterly external recon and brand monitoring.

saas_growth_readiness.compound_saas_loop: CROSS-PACK: SaaS loop broken — users don't activate, those who do don't see upgrade, those on paid have no expansion path. Paid acquisition is a leaky bucket. Fix in order: (1) cut activation to 3 steps + 60-second quick win; (2) add usage-triggered upgrade prompts (not in billing); (3) build seat expansion or premium add-ons.
saas_growth_readiness.activation_blocked: New users cannot complete activation — empty screens, missing data, or blocked next step. Identify the FIRST point where the user hits a wall and ship a workaround within 7 days.
saas_growth_readiness.activation_friction: Activation has too many steps before first value. Cut to 3 essential steps; defer everything else to in-app prompts.
saas_growth_readiness.next_step: Users land on the app and do not know what to do. Add a persistent "next step" prompt in the dashboard until first activation milestone is hit.
saas_growth_readiness.empty_state: Empty screens leave users stuck. Every empty state needs: explanation of what would appear here + sample data button + CTA to create real data.
saas_growth_readiness.nav_complex: Navigation has too many top-level items. Flatten to 5-7 max, group secondary items under settings/profile.
saas_growth_readiness.feature_discovery: Users cannot find features they pay for. Add a feature-tour modal on first login OR a search bar in the app shell that surfaces features.
saas_growth_readiness.upgrade_visible: Upgrade path is hidden. Show plan limits + upgrade CTA at the moment the user hits the limit (in-context, not just in billing).
saas_growth_readiness.upgrade_timing: Upgrade prompts fire before users see value. Delay the first upsell until the user has completed 3 core actions, then prompt with usage-justified copy.
saas_growth_readiness.expansion_path: Existing customers have no upgrade path beyond their plan. Add: seat expansion, premium feature add-ons, or annual prepay discount.
saas_growth_readiness.landing_mismatch: Marketing landing promises X, in-app delivers Y. Align: every feature mentioned on the landing must appear in the trial onboarding within 10 minutes.
saas_growth_readiness.quick_win: Onboarding has no quick win. Engineer a 60-second activation: user signs up → sees one tangible result within a minute.
saas_growth_readiness.incident_primary: Trial-to-paid conversion is leaking before users see value. Activation gaps must be fixed before any acquisition push.
saas_growth_readiness.fix_primary: SaaS activation has structural gaps. Address before scaling trial signups.
saas_growth_readiness.optimize_primary: Activation works but has optimization opportunities for first-value time + expansion.
saas_growth_readiness.strong_primary: SaaS growth readiness is healthy. Continue monitoring activation + expansion metrics.
saas_growth_readiness.verify: Re-run audit in 30 days; track trial-to-paid conversion + 7-day activation rate.

channel_integrity.payment_surface: Payment surface has tampering exposure — third-party scripts on checkout, missing CSP. Lock down scripts on /checkout to a known allowlist with SRI hashes.
channel_integrity.traffic_diversion: Traffic on commercial pages can be diverted (open redirects, weak referrer policy). Audit redirect handlers + set Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin.
channel_integrity.commerce_exposed: Promotion / discount logic is exposed in client-side code. Move price + discount calculation server-side; never trust client-submitted prices.
channel_integrity.landing_trust: Paid traffic lands on pages with weak technical trust posture (missing HTTPS strict, weak headers). Run securityheaders.com on every paid landing and fix every red.
channel_integrity.checkout_infra: Checkout depends on third parties that fail under load. Identify the slowest 2 third-party scripts and async-defer or self-host them.
channel_integrity.cart_variant: Cart accepts variants with weak price safeguards (negative quantity, alternative price). Add server-side validation: minimum price per SKU, max quantity per cart.
channel_integrity.hidden_routes: Hidden discount/refund routes are accessible to anyone who guesses the URL. Move behind auth or behind a one-time signed token.
channel_integrity.endpoint_guessable: Business endpoints (orders, billing, admin) follow guessable patterns. Switch to UUIDs and audit access logs for enumeration attempts.
channel_integrity.deep_exploitation: Deep crawl found buying-flow variants with weak guards. Treat every URL returned by Katana as a candidate for the same auth + rate-limit + validation as the main flow.
channel_integrity.trust_deps: Trust signals (badges, testimonials) depend on third-party widgets that fail. Self-host or remove fragile widgets; trust must not flicker.
channel_integrity.discount_abuse: Discount codes are being abused (mass redemption from same IP/email pattern). Add per-customer use limits + IP rate-limiting on coupon endpoint.
channel_integrity.ad_concentration: Ad spend is concentrated on one platform — a policy change kills your revenue. Diversify: split spend between Meta + Google + a third channel.
channel_integrity.whatsapp_channel: WhatsApp channel exists but is disconnected from main funnel. Add WhatsApp CTA to /pricing + /checkout for buyers who hesitate.
channel_integrity.economic_exploit: Active economic exploitation detected (coupon abuse, price manipulation, refund fraud). Escalate to security team + freeze affected accounts within 24h.
channel_integrity.incident_primary: Channel integrity has critical exposures — attackers can manipulate prices, divert traffic, or compromise payment surfaces. Treat as security incident.
channel_integrity.fix_primary: Channel integrity has elevated risk. Fix exposed business endpoints + script supply chain before scaling traffic.
channel_integrity.optimize_primary: Channel integrity is functional. Hardening opportunities exist around third-party dependencies + endpoint guessability.
channel_integrity.strong_primary: Channel integrity is solid. Continue monitoring third-party scripts and access patterns.
channel_integrity.verify: Re-run external scan + security scan in 30 days; verify exposed endpoints are now protected.

friction_tax.step_friction: One specific funnel step is costing the most friction. Identify the step (via funnel analytics) and run usability test focused exclusively on it.
friction_tax.oscillation: Buyers bounce between 2 pages before deciding (e.g. pricing ↔ FAQ). Embed the answers on the decision page so they do not need to leave.
friction_tax.checkout_entry: Checkout entry has visible friction (gate, login wall, account required). Allow guest checkout + remove pre-payment account creation.
friction_tax.incident_primary: UX friction is measurably costing revenue across multiple funnel steps.
friction_tax.fix_primary: UX friction is elevated. Fix the highest-cost step before scaling acquisition.
friction_tax.optimize_primary: Friction is moderate. Iterating on entry points will compound CR improvements.
friction_tax.strong_primary: Friction tax is low. Continue monitoring funnel step velocity.
friction_tax.verify: Re-run behavioral analysis in 14-30 days; measure step-level conversion delta.

content_freshness.commercial_stale: Commercial pages have stale content (old year, outdated copy). Add a quarterly review calendar — every /product and /pricing page touched at least once per quarter.
content_freshness.pricing_outdated: Pricing page is outdated — old prices, old plan names, dated comparison points. Update immediately; outdated pricing is the #1 reason buyers email sales asking "is this still accurate?"
content_freshness.social_proof_stale: All testimonials and case studies are dated >18 months. Refresh: collect 3 new customer quotes this quarter + add a date to every testimonial.
content_freshness.content_decay: Content staleness is increasing audit-over-audit. Hire a part-time content owner OR add a "last reviewed" auto-prompt that nags after 90 days.
content_freshness.incident_primary: Stale content is actively eroding trust + AI search visibility. Refresh commercial pages immediately.
content_freshness.fix_primary: Multiple commercial pages have stale content. Update before scaling acquisition.
content_freshness.optimize_primary: Content freshness is mostly good but has decay risk. Schedule quarterly refresh cadence.
content_freshness.strong_primary: Content is fresh. Maintain quarterly review cadence.
content_freshness.verify: Re-audit in 90 days; verify all commercial pages have been touched.

mobile_revenue_exposure.compound_mobile_broken: CROSS-PACK: Mobile commerce broken on multiple dimensions (conversion + form + CTA timing). This is structural mobile UX failure leaking the majority of paid traffic. Walk through signup-to-checkout on a real Android + iOS device — not DevTools mobile mode (it lies about font, keyboard, tap zones). Consider shipping a separate mobile-first /checkout.
mobile_revenue_exposure.conversion_gap: Mobile conversion is materially lower than desktop. Audit /checkout on a real mobile device (not DevTools): viewport, font size, input keyboard types, submit button reach zone.
mobile_revenue_exposure.form_friction: Mobile forms cause more friction than desktop. Each input needs the right inputmode (tel, email, decimal); add autocomplete=cc-number on payment fields.
mobile_revenue_exposure.cta_timing: Mobile CTA renders late, delaying clicks. Inline-load button HTML so it is paint-ready in <1s on slow 3G; defer JS that powers it.
mobile_revenue_exposure.incident_primary: Mobile experience is compounding revenue loss. Most paid traffic is mobile — this is a top fix.
mobile_revenue_exposure.fix_primary: Mobile has structural conversion gaps. Address before scaling mobile-heavy paid channels.
mobile_revenue_exposure.optimize_primary: Mobile works but has friction. Iterate on form inputs + CTA timing.
mobile_revenue_exposure.strong_primary: Mobile experience is solid. Continue monitoring mobile conversion delta.
mobile_revenue_exposure.verify: Re-audit on real mobile device in 30 days; track mobile-vs-desktop conversion ratio.

trust_revenue_gap.compound_trust_collapse: CROSS-PACK: Trust collapses progressively — weak first impression → weaker mobile → broken at checkout. Place the single most powerful trust signal (named customer testimonial with photo OR specific outcome number) adjacent to primary CTA on homepage AND adjacent to Buy button on checkout. Then audit mobile-specific trust placement.
trust_revenue_gap.trust_deficit: Buyers hesitate at conversion because trust signals are missing AT the decision moment. Add testimonials + guarantee + security badge directly adjacent to the Buy button.
trust_revenue_gap.reassurance: Buyers seek reassurance (FAQ, policies) before converting. Surface the top 3 buying objections on the conversion page as inline FAQ — they should not have to leave.
trust_revenue_gap.sensitive_trust: Sensitive fields (CPF, card, address) lack trust framing. Add a brief "why we need this" note + visible HTTPS/security indicator next to each sensitive input.
trust_revenue_gap.incident_primary: Trust deficit is blocking conversion. Buyers want to pay but cannot get reassurance.
trust_revenue_gap.fix_primary: Trust gaps are dragging conversion. Add reassurance at decision moment before scaling.
trust_revenue_gap.optimize_primary: Trust signals are present but timing could improve. Place them adjacent to CTA, not in the footer.
trust_revenue_gap.strong_primary: Trust signals support conversion well. Continue monitoring sensitive-input completion.
trust_revenue_gap.verify: Re-run behavioral analysis in 30 days; track sensitive-field completion + abandonment delta.

first_impression_revenue.milestone_stall: First-session visitors stall before hitting the first conversion milestone. Audit homepage above-fold: is the value prop clear in 5 seconds? Is there one obvious next step?
first_impression_revenue.trust_barrier: First-time visitors hit a trust barrier early. Move social proof (customer logos, testimonial snippet) above the fold; remove anything that signals "early stage" or "experimental".
first_impression_revenue.cta_timing: First-session visitors do not see the CTA in time. Primary CTA should be visible in the first paint, not behind a scroll or after JS hydration.
first_impression_revenue.incident_primary: First impression is losing buyers before they engage. Address before any acquisition spend.
first_impression_revenue.fix_primary: First-session experience has gaps. Refresh hero + above-fold trust + CTA timing.
first_impression_revenue.optimize_primary: First impression is functional. Continue iterating on hero conversion.
first_impression_revenue.strong_primary: First-session metrics are healthy. Continue monitoring.
first_impression_revenue.verify: Re-audit in 30 days; track first-session conversion + bounce rate.

action_value_map.low_value_dominates: Most user actions are low-value (newsletter signup, blog reads) while high-value paths get fewer clicks. Re-prioritize CTA placement: high-value actions in primary positions, low-value in footer.
action_value_map.high_value_hidden: High-value actions (pricing, demo, signup) are underexposed. Audit homepage + key pages: each should have ≥1 visible high-value CTA above the fold.
action_value_map.dead_weight: Some pages get traffic but produce zero conversions. Either redirect them to higher-value pages, add conversion paths, or remove from primary navigation.
action_value_map.incident_primary: User action mix is dominated by low-value moves while high-value actions are invisible.
action_value_map.fix_primary: Action value distribution is suboptimal. Re-prioritize CTAs before scaling traffic.
action_value_map.optimize_primary: Some pages absorb traffic without producing value. Audit + redirect.
action_value_map.strong_primary: User action value distribution is healthy.
action_value_map.verify: Re-audit in 30 days; track high-value action conversion rate.

acquisition_integrity.compound_dead_spend: CROSS-PACK: DARK WASTE — ads sending traffic to dead pages AND no conversion tracking to see it. STOP all paid spend NOW until (1) every active ad destination returns 200 + loads as expected AND (2) conversion events fire correctly in Meta/Google. 4-hour fix blocking accurate ROI on last 90 days of spend.
acquisition_integrity.compound_paid_burn: CROSS-PACK: Paid acquisition compounding waste across friction + trust + mobile. CR is multiplicative across layers — small losses compound to 50%+ effective waste. Pause OR ship a separate paid-only landing page (sub-2s mobile load, single CTA, no nav, message-match headline, trust strip above fold).
acquisition_integrity.paid_friction: Paid traffic hits more friction than organic. Match landing page promise to ad copy verbatim + cut form fields to email-only.
acquisition_integrity.paid_trust: Paid traffic lands on pages without the trust signals organic visitors get. Audit: testimonials, customer logos, security badges, money-back guarantee — all should be on paid landings.
acquisition_integrity.paid_mobile: Paid mobile traffic compounds friction + trust issues = burnt spend. Build mobile-first paid landings (separate from desktop) — sub-2s load, single primary CTA, no nav.
acquisition_integrity.incident_primary: Paid acquisition is wasting spend at scale. Pause campaigns until landing quality matches ad promise.
acquisition_integrity.fix_primary: Paid acquisition has measurable waste. Fix landing pages before increasing ad budget.
acquisition_integrity.optimize_primary: Paid landings work but have room for CR uplift. Iterate on message match + mobile.
acquisition_integrity.strong_primary: Paid acquisition is efficient. Continue monitoring per-channel CAC.
acquisition_integrity.verify: Re-run paid landing audit in 30 days; track per-campaign CAC delta.

path_efficiency.path_length: Path to purchase has too many steps. Audit each step: is it essential or is it asking for info that could come post-purchase?
path_efficiency.intent_absorber: One specific page is absorbing buying intent without converting it (e.g. FAQ, support). Add a clear "ready to buy?" CTA at the end of that page.
path_efficiency.intent_decay: Time from intent-expressed to conversion is too long. Trigger a follow-up: exit-intent modal + email reminder within 24h for cart abandoners.
path_efficiency.incident_primary: Path to purchase is too long — buyers lose intent before converting.
path_efficiency.fix_primary: Conversion path is inefficient. Shorten before scaling acquisition.
path_efficiency.optimize_primary: Path is acceptable but specific steps can be tightened.
path_efficiency.verify: Re-audit funnel in 30 days; track time-to-conversion + step drop-off.

summaries.fix_before_scale: Issues should be fixed before scaling traffic.
summaries.ready_with_risks: Traffic can be scaled with caution. Some risks remain but are not blocking.
summaries.safe_to_scale: Safe to scale traffic. No significant risks detected.
summaries.revenue_leakage_detected: Active revenue leakage detected. Conversion path has critical structural issues losing money.
summaries.revenue_at_risk: Revenue path has significant issues. Fix before increasing ad spend.
summaries.revenue_path_fragile: Revenue path is functional but fragile. Optimization opportunities exist to reduce leakage.
summaries.revenue_integrity_stable: Revenue integrity is stable. No significant leakage detected.
summaries.high_chargeback_risk: High chargeback risk. Missing policies, support gaps, and expectation issues create significant dispute exposure.
summaries.moderate_chargeback_risk: Moderate chargeback risk. Some protective measures are missing.
summaries.low_chargeback_risk: Low chargeback risk. Minor improvements available but not blocking.
summaries.chargeback_resilience_strong: Chargeback resilience is strong. No significant dispute risk detected.

summaries.payment_health_at_risk: Payment health is at risk. Elevated failed payments or subscriber churn are creating preventable revenue loss.
summaries.payment_health_stable: Payment health is stable. Failed payment and churn rates are within acceptable ranges.
summaries.copy_misaligned: Copy is misaligned with commercial intent. Messaging is failing to convert high-intent visitors across multiple dimensions.
summaries.copy_significant_gaps: Copy has significant alignment gaps. Multiple CRO dimensions need attention before scaling.
summaries.copy_minor_gaps: Copy has minor alignment gaps. Messaging is mostly effective but refinements could lift conversion.
summaries.copy_aligned: Copy is aligned with commercial intent. No significant gaps detected.
summaries.channel_integrity_critical: Critical channel integrity compromise detected. Payment surfaces, scripts, or infrastructure are exposed to tampering or hijack.
summaries.channel_integrity_elevated: Elevated channel integrity risk. Abuse vectors or brittle infrastructure need attention before scaling.
summaries.channel_integrity_weak: Channel integrity has minor gaps. Hardening opportunities exist but are not blocking.
summaries.channel_integrity_strong: Channel integrity is strong. No significant compromise vectors detected.
summaries.discoverability_critically_weak: Discoverability is critically weak. Commercial pages are invisible to search engines and social platforms.
summaries.discoverability_gaps_significant: Significant discoverability gaps. Search representation and social previews need improvement before scaling traffic.
summaries.discoverability_improvable: Discoverability has optimization opportunities. Pages are indexable but not well-represented.
summaries.discoverability_adequate: Discoverability is adequate. No significant gaps detected.
summaries.brand_integrity_critical: Critical brand integrity threat. Active impersonation or phishing surfaces are diverting customer trust and revenue.
summaries.brand_integrity_elevated: Elevated brand integrity risk. Lookalike domains or deceptive surfaces are competing for brand traffic.
summaries.brand_integrity_weak: Brand integrity has minor exposure. Brand presence is diluted but not under active threat.
summaries.brand_integrity_strong: Brand integrity is strong. No significant impersonation or dilution detected.
summaries.security_posture_critical: Critical security exposures detected. Sensitive files or mixed content on commercial pages create immediate financial risk.
summaries.security_posture_elevated: Elevated security posture risk. Weak headers or open redirects increase exposure.
summaries.security_posture_weak: Security posture has minor gaps. Hardening opportunities exist but are not blocking.
summaries.security_posture_adequate: Security posture is adequate. No significant exposures detected.
summaries.funnel_journey_critical_gaps: Critical funnel journey gaps. Exit points and drop-offs are blocking conversion at key funnel moments.
summaries.funnel_journey_significant_gaps: Significant funnel journey gaps. Multiple funnel moments need attention before scaling traffic.
summaries.funnel_journey_minor_gaps: Minor funnel journey gaps. Optimization opportunities exist but are not blocking.
summaries.funnel_journey_healthy: Funnel journey is healthy. No significant breaks detected in the funnel.
summaries.vertical_critical_gaps: Critical vertical-specific gaps. Important industry patterns are missing or broken.
summaries.vertical_significant_gaps: Significant vertical-specific gaps. Multiple expected patterns need fixes before scaling.
summaries.vertical_minor_gaps: Minor vertical-specific gaps. Refinement opportunities available.
summaries.vertical_healthy: Vertical patterns are aligned. No significant gaps detected.

confidence_narrative.high_high: Analysis is structurally sound and economic estimates are well-calibrated.
confidence_narrative.high_not_high: Structural analysis is reliable, but economic estimates carry uncertainty. The issues identified are real; the dollar amounts should be treated as directional.
confidence_narrative.not_high_high: Economic data is strong but structural evidence could be stronger. Consider verification to strengthen confidence in the identified issues.
confidence_narrative.low_low: Both structural evidence and economic data carry uncertainty. Findings are directional — verify before taking action on specific dollar estimates.

names.root_cause: What is actually causing the problems

remediation_steps: Move the payment form to the same domain as the store, or use the gateway's embedded checkout.
If the domain switch is unavoidable, add the store logo and a security badge to the external page.
Ensure HTTPS and a valid certificate on both domains — verify in incognito browsers.
Add copy to the checkout button explaining that the next page is the payment processor's secure page.
trust_boundary_crossed.verification_notes: We will run checkout in a headless browser, follow redirects to the payment page, and check whether the URL stays on your domain or whether there's a logo + badge on the external page.

remediation_steps: Publish separate pages for privacy policy, terms of use, and refund policy.
Link all three policies in the footer and also in checkout near the payment fields.
Include at minimum: refund window, return process, GDPR compliance, and a contact channel.
Mention the refund policy explicitly on the product page near the buy button.
policy_gap.verification_notes: We will re-crawl the site and check whether dedicated policy pages exist, are linked from the footer, and are accessible from checkout.

remediation_steps: Install analytics (GA4, Plausible, or similar) on every commercial page.
Verify the page-view event fires on homepage, pricing, product, checkout, and thank-you.
Confirm the consent banner doesn't block the primary measurement script.
Set up conversion events (purchase, signup) and validate them in the platform's debug tool.
measurement_blindspot.verification_notes: We will fetch the commercial pages in a browser and inspect the network tab + dataLayer for analytics events fired on each.

remediation_steps: Define one primary CTA per commercial page above the fold.
Move supporting CTAs (newsletter, blog, social) below the primary fold.
Use the same primary CTA copy across pricing, product, and homepage to compound recognition.
Remove or hide competing actions in the same viewport as the primary CTA.
unclear_conversion_intent.verification_notes: We will analyze each commercial page's above-the-fold layout for CTA clarity and count competing actions in the primary viewport.

remediation_steps: Add a direct CTA link from the homepage to /pricing or /product.
Add a direct CTA from /pricing to /checkout (or the primary buy action).
Add a direct CTA from /product back to /pricing or /checkout.
Verify each commercial page links to the NEXT step in the funnel within one click.
commercial_pages_disconnected.verification_notes: We will re-crawl the commercial pages and check whether each has at least one CTA link to the next funnel stage.

remediation_steps: Activate Stripe Card Updater and Smart Retries to automatically retry failed payments.
Send dunning emails on first failure with a direct link to update the payment method.
Add a 3-day grace period before suspending access on failed renewals.
Monitor failed payment rate weekly and aim to keep it below 5%.
failed_payment_revenue_drain.verification_notes: We will re-pull Stripe metrics 30 days after the fixes and confirm whether the failed payment rate dropped below 5%.

remediation_steps: Reconcile the month's total revenue by summing EVERY collection channel — Stripe, boleto, PIX, MercadoPago, bank transfer, in-person payment, PayPal, and any other gateway. Compare that total against what Meta + Google are reporting as attributed.

If the sum of ALL channels matches the platform-attributed total: the discrepancy was just Stripe not seeing the other channels. No over-attribution. Next step is to wire those off-Stripe channels into Vestigio (Wave 6.x: MercadoPago, boleto gateway, etc.) so the comparison stays honest on future cycles.

If the sum of ALL channels is still substantially lower than the platform-attributed total: NOW you have real over-attribution. Run a holdout test — pause 20-30% of campaigns for 14 days and measure the real total-revenue drop vs the platform-projected loss. The difference is the actual incremental lift.

Set up multi-touch attribution (data-driven in GA4 or Triple Whale/Northbeam) and compare ROAS under data-driven vs last-click. Only re-tier ad spend by the more conservative model AFTER the channel reconciliation above is complete.
revenue_attribution_mismatch.verification_notes: We'll re-pull Meta + Google + Stripe in the next cycle. The finding disappears when the attributed-vs-Stripe-MRR gap falls below 2x (the threshold under which off-Stripe channels stop being a plausible explanation).

remediation_steps: Implement a cancellation survey to capture why subscribers leave.
Offer retention paths before final cancel: pause subscription, downgrade plan, or extend trial.
Trigger a win-back email 14 days after cancellation with a personalized offer.
Analyze churn cohorts by tenure to identify the month where most cancellations happen.
subscriber_churn_unsustainable.verification_notes: We will re-pull Stripe subscription data 60 days post-implementation to compare churn rate against the prior cohort.

remediation_steps: Add Content-Security-Policy with explicit allowed sources for scripts, frames, and connections.
Add Strict-Transport-Security with max-age=31536000 and includeSubDomains.
Add X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff and Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin.
Add Permissions-Policy to restrict camera, microphone, and geolocation defaults.
security_header_weakness.verification_notes: We will re-fetch the homepage and checkout and parse the response headers to confirm each policy is set with the expected directives.

remediation_steps: Add a secondary payment gateway (Adyen, Braintree, or local provider) as failover to Stripe.
Configure automatic failover routing when the primary gateway returns 5xx errors.
Test the failover with a deliberate primary-gateway outage on staging.
Document the failover playbook for the on-call engineer.
payment_diversity_insufficient.verification_notes: We will check the integration list and confirm at least two active payment gateways are configured.

remediation_steps: Identify whether the contraction comes from failed payments (involuntary churn) or cancellations (voluntary churn) via a Stripe cohort.
For involuntary churn: enable Card Updater + Smart Retries + dunning emails on first failure — recovers 30-50% of cases.
For voluntary churn: implement a cancellation survey + retention offers (pause, downgrade, extension) before final cancel.
Configure a weekly MRR delta alert to catch contraction before it compounds across 3+ cycles.
mrr_contraction_detected.verification_notes: We will re-pull Stripe data on the next cycle and confirm whether MRR resumed growth or stabilized.

remediation_steps: Run the checkout end-to-end in at least 3 browsers (Chrome, Safari, mobile) and note every error / unexpected redirect.
Fix off-domain redirects and HTTP errors before any conversion optimization.
Add an SSL security seal + 2 trust seals (Trustpilot, Google Reviews, etc.) visible at checkout.
Publish the mandatory policies (privacy, terms, refund) and link them in the checkout footer.
Configure uptime monitoring on the /checkout and /cart URLs with sub-5-min alerts.
checkout_integrity.verification_notes: We will run the checkout in a headless browser, trace redirects, measure latency, and revalidate the presence of policies + trust seals.

remediation_steps: Identify the 3 most critical endpoints on the purchase path (product -> cart -> checkout) and set up 1-minute health checks.
Remove third-party scripts that block checkout render -- move them to async / defer.
Implement a checkout fallback for when the primary gateway fails (automatic retry + user-facing message).
Add client-side error logging on the checkout to gain visibility into which requests fail in production.
revenue_path_fragile.verification_notes: We will navigate the full path (home -> product -> cart -> checkout) headlessly, measure per-request timings, identify blocking third-parties and return an updated report.

remediation_steps: Install GA4 via GTM on every page including the confirmation URL (/thank-you, /success).
Configure the `purchase` event with transaction_id, value, currency, and items.
Add Meta Pixel + server-side Conversions API to survive cookie loss.
Validate in Tag Assistant / Meta Events Manager that all 4 tags (GA4, GTM, Pixel, CAPI) fire on a real purchase.
measurement_coverage.verification_notes: We will open the store in a headless browser, trigger synthetic events, and check whether GA4 / GTM / Pixel / CAPI receive them. We report which are present and which are still missing.

remediation_steps: Investigate the status code returned by /checkout, /cart and product pages -- prioritize URLs with 4xx/5xx.
Restore broken URLs immediately (via revert of a recent deploy or a routing hotfix).
Set up an alert in Sentry / Datadog / UptimeRobot to fire when the status code is not 2xx on any critical URL.
Review the release pipeline to block deploys if the checkout smoke-test fails.
critical_path_broken.verification_notes: We will re-fetch the critical URLs (/checkout, /cart, representative product pages) and confirm whether the status code is back to 2xx.

remediation_steps: Audit the `action` attribute of every form -- list which ones point to external domains.
Whitelist legitimate forms (OAuth, hosted payment processor) with documentation explaining why they leave the domain.
Non-essential forms that leave the domain: migrate them to internal endpoints that proxy to the external service.
Add aria-label to every form describing its purpose (signup, checkout, support, search) to make auditing easier.
form_data_leaves_domain.verification_notes: We will re-parse the HTML of the relevant pages, list every form with a cross-domain action, and verify that the non-whitelisted ones have been migrated.

remediation_steps: Audit embeds (iframes, third-party scripts) present on product, cart and checkout pages.
Remove non-essential embeds from the purchase path -- decorative trust badges, generic chat widgets.
For essential embeds (payment gateway, official chat), validate that they come from recognized domains with a valid certificate.
Configure a strict Content-Security-Policy on checkout allowing only the necessary domains.
untrusted_embeds_near_purchase.verification_notes: We will re-parse the HTML of the purchase path to list every external iframe/script and classify essential vs non-essential.

remediation_steps: Explicitly document which checkout platform you use (Shopify, Nuvem, hosted gateway) and the version / plan.
Set up a health check endpoint against the platform and monitor uptime via UptimeRobot or similar.
Have a written contingency plan: if the platform is down for >15min, how do you process orders manually.
Check whether your platform plan includes an SLA and compensation for downtime.
platform_checkout_risk_unaddressed.verification_notes: We will re-project the finding after your next audit -- without new data, this is a structural assessment of platform dependency risk.

remediation_steps: Compare the current audit with the previous one -- identify which findings got worse (severity or confidence went up).
Isolate the deploy or product change that coincides with the regression window.
If the regression came from a deploy: consider a rollback while you investigate. If it came from an operational change: review the process that caused it.
Add a smoke test on the revenue path to prevent that specific type of regression in the next deploy.
revenue_path_regressed.verification_notes: We will re-project against the current evidence to confirm whether the regression is still present or was resolved after your fix.

remediation_steps: Add at least 3 trust markers visible on the home: SSL seal, payment seals (accepted card brands), testimonials / reviews.
On the product page, include: customer reviews, refund policy, contact information, delivery time.
At checkout, reinforce: explicit SSL seal, linked refund policy, visible support channel, gateway logos.
Avoid generic trust markers without context (badges without real certification behind them) -- they can weaken more than help.
