trust_surface_too_thin.verification_notes: We will re-crawl home + product + checkout to count present trust markers and compare against the baseline of audited stores.

remediation_steps: List all active traffic channels (organic, paid, social, email) and which tag each one needs for attribution.
Install and validate: GA4, GTM, Meta Pixel (if Meta Ads), Google Ads tag (if Google Ads), TikTok Pixel (if TikTok).
Implement CAPI / server-side for Meta and Google -- critical mitigation for cookie loss on Safari/iOS.
Document which event (purchase, add_to_cart, initiate_checkout) each tag must capture and test in Tag Assistant.
tracking_stack_gaps.verification_notes: We will trigger a synthetic checkout in a headless browser and check which tags fire -- returns a list of present vs expected tags.

remediation_steps: Run the full checkout on an iPhone and an Android -- note every point where the flow gets stuck (viewport, keyboard, button).
Fix the viewport meta tag (`width=device-width, initial-scale=1`) and ensure the checkout does not require horizontal scrolling.
Test buttons: they should have at least a 44x44px clickable area and >=8px spacing from other elements.
Eliminate overlays / modals that break on mobile (scroll trapped, close button off-viewport).
mobile_commercial_path_blocked.verification_notes: We will navigate the checkout in a headless browser with a mobile viewport (375x667) and report every point where the flow breaks.

remediation_steps: Compare home + product + checkout on desktop vs mobile -- trust markers that appear on desktop should also appear on mobile.
Mobile typically hides trust markers to save space -- prioritize keeping the SSL seal, refund policy, and contact always visible.
Use accordions / drawers to surface trust markers on demand instead of hiding them entirely.
Test in a 375px-wide viewport that the checkout safety information is not truncated.
mobile_trust_weaker_than_desktop.verification_notes: We will navigate headlessly in mobile and desktop viewports and compare how many trust markers each layout exposes.

remediation_steps: Identify the secondary flows: WhatsApp purchase, quote form, deep link from ads, external app.
For each secondary flow, validate that the buyer crosses the same trust markers (policy, SSL seal, contact) before purchase.
If a secondary flow skips the official checkout, add an intermediate page with the essential trust markers.
Measure each secondary flow's conversion separately in GA4 -- compare with the official path to isolate where trust is eroding.
secondary_flows_bypass_trust_path.verification_notes: Phase 3 will introduce probes on the secondary flows (WhatsApp, form) -- today verification is re-projecting against existing evidence.

remediation_steps: Configure error tracking (Sentry, Rollbar, Datadog RUM) on the checkout and cart frontend -- not yet on every page, focus on the revenue path.
Review the last 50 captured JS errors -- prioritize those firing on product, cart, and checkout pages.
Add try/catch around external calls in the checkout (gateway, CAPI, anti-fraud) with fallback that does not block the user.
Configure source maps in the build so production stack traces are legible.
runtime_errors_interrupt_purchase.verification_notes: We will navigate the checkout in a headless browser collecting JS console errors -- returns a list of errors found vs the previous baseline.

remediation_steps: Open the checkout in Chrome DevTools and look in the Network tab: are the GA4 / Pixel / CAPI requests arriving (status 200)?
Add debug logging on the analytics tags in staging to confirm the payload sent.
Validate with official tools: Tag Assistant (Google), Pixel Helper (Meta), GA4 DebugView.
If the issue is consent (GDPR): review the banner logic to ensure tags fire after consent, not before nor never.
runtime_measurement_broken.verification_notes: We will trigger a synthetic checkout headlessly, capture network requests, and confirm which tags send valid data.

remediation_steps: Identify the highest commercial-intent pages (product, pricing, comparison) and validate that all of them are GA4 + Pixel tagged.
Configure scroll tracking and time-on-page specifically on those surfaces -- intent must be measured beyond pageview.
Add custom engagement events: view_item, add_to_cart, scroll_75, time_on_page_60s.
Build a funnel dashboard scoped to those surfaces to visualize drop-off by intent stage.
high_intent_surfaces_blind.verification_notes: We will navigate the high-intent pages headlessly and confirm which events fire vs expected.

remediation_steps: Map the current funnel: home -> product -> cart -> checkout -> confirmation. Identify where more than one path is possible.
Consolidate duplicate CTAs on home and product -- one clear primary CTA per page.
Remove optional steps that break momentum (newsletter popup before checkout, post-add-to-cart survey).
Standardize the checkout layout: single column on mobile, logically grouped fields, progress indicator if multi-step.
conversion_flow_fragmented.verification_notes: We will navigate the full funnel and count alternative paths + CTAs competing for attention.

remediation_steps: Count how many form fields exist between "view product" and "pay" -- reduce to <=8 required.
Eliminate forced logins before checkout -- offer guest checkout with the option to create an account afterward.
Remove modals, popups and overlays from the purchase path -- only what is strictly necessary to complete payment.
If there are multiple checkout steps, ensure that going back to edit each step does not lose previously entered data.
friction_on_critical_path.verification_notes: We will run the critical path headlessly and count frictions: fields, modals, redirects, forced logins.

remediation_steps: Review the list of highest-impact findings -- prioritize those with confidence >=70% and severity >=high.
For each priority finding, mark the responsible owner and implementation window.
Block deploys that affect the revenue path until critical findings are resolved.
Build a weekly dashboard with the sum of monthly_range.mid for open findings to track accumulated leakage.
revenue_leakage.verification_notes: Re-project against current evidence to recalculate aggregated leakage -- without new data, only the current sum.

remediation_steps: Open the checkout and identify every moment where the buyer could hesitate (domain change, missing seal, hidden policy).
Add an explicit SSL seal ("Secure connection") next to the credit card field.
Surface the refund policy one click away from the checkout -- do not hide it in a generic footer.
Show accepted card brand logos + the gateway logo (Stripe, Mercado Pago, etc.) to reinforce credibility.
trust_break_in_checkout.verification_notes: We will navigate to the checkout headlessly and count trust markers visible in the payment viewport.

remediation_steps: Audit the /checkout redirect chain -- use curl -L -I or DevTools Network to list every hop.
Eliminate unnecessary redirects (http->https->www->subdomain) -- ideally a single redirect or zero.
If the gateway requires leaving the domain, ensure the final redirect is direct and does not pass through intermediaries.
Configure HSTS on the root domain to force HTTPS without a redirect round-trip.
redirect_chain_erodes_checkout_trust.verification_notes: We will re-fetch /checkout following redirects and count hops -- the report includes every intermediate URL.

remediation_steps: Audit whether every commercial surface (home, product, checkout, confirmation) is in the same language.
Resolve mixes: product in pt-BR but checkout in English is an immediate trust-break for the Brazilian buyer.
Set `<html lang="pt-BR">` on every commercial page to signal to search engines and screen readers.
If you have a multi-language version, implement hreflang to prevent Google from indexing the wrong version per region.
commercial_journey_language_break.verification_notes: We will re-parse the HTML of the commercial surfaces and extract the lang attribute + a copy sample to detect language mixes.

remediation_steps: Audit the post-purchase confirmation page: it should include the order number, a summary of what was bought, and the estimated delivery time.
Configure a transactional confirmation email to fire immediately after the purchase event.
Include in the email: invoice (or its dispatch ETA), support channel, and tracking link when available.
Configure retry + fallback on email delivery -- do not rely on a single provider without backup.
post_purchase_confirmation_absent.verification_notes: We will load the confirmation URL headlessly and verify the presence of essential elements (order ID, summary, ETA).

remediation_steps: Rewrite the refund policy page with the structure: timeframe -> process -> contact channel -> exceptions.
Include concrete examples ("If you received the wrong product, email X with a photo") -- do not leave it as abstract rules.
Add a refund FAQ at checkout or near the pay button, with a link to the full policy.
Measure average time to first customer contact via the refund channel -- under 2 business hours is a good benchmark.
refund_process_unclear.verification_notes: We will re-fetch the refund policy page and analyze structure + content density.

remediation_steps: Configure a professional confirmation email (branded template, not plain text) that fires within <1min of purchase.
Include an electronic invoice / PDF receipt attached or linked for download.
Add a tracking code as soon as it is available (carrier webhook -> automatic email).
Send a post-delivery follow-up email asking for a review -- reinforces the closure of the trust cycle.
post_purchase_proof_too_weak.verification_notes: Post-purchase proof happens via email/SMS outside the site -- it cannot be verified via crawl. Phase 3 explores ESP integration to validate transactional templates.

remediation_steps: Position a visible support channel before checkout -- footer with WhatsApp/chat on every commercial page.
Add microcopy next to the pay button: "Questions? Talk to our team on WhatsApp".
If you have a chat widget, ensure it loads fast (without blocking the checkout render) and responds in <5 business minutes.
Include a purchase FAQ directly on the product and checkout pages -- answer the 5 most common doubts before the buyer needs to ask.
support_reassurance_too_late.verification_notes: We will re-parse home + product + checkout and detect support channels visible on each surface.

remediation_steps: Test each reassurance route: does clicking "Refund policy" from checkout open in a new tab or take you to a generic footer?
Ensure each trust marker (policy, contact, testimonials) opens CONTEXTUALLY -- modal or dedicated page with a way back.
Avoid policies on loose footer URLs -- anchor them in the purchase context (direct link from checkout to refund policy).
Measure the return rate after clicking those links -- if >50% of clicks do not come back, the link is diverting the buyer.
reassurance_routes_disconnected.verification_notes: We will navigate from the checkout clicking each reassurance link and verify destination + behavior.

remediation_steps: List every alternative purchase flow: WhatsApp, email, phone call, quote form, marketplace.
For each flow, ensure the conversion event is captured (even manually -- log in CRM + custom event in GA4).
Configure UTMs + custom source/medium to separate those flows' conversions from the main site.
Build a GA4 dashboard that consolidates every conversion source so you can see the complete funnel.
alternate_flows_unmeasured.verification_notes: Alternative flows happen off-site -- verification is to re-project after you tag the events.

remediation_steps: Audit whether reassurance components (policy, chat widget, trust badges) throw JS errors that break the page.
Ensure chat widgets load async/defer -- they should not block rendering of the main content.
If a trust badge fails to load (image 404, external script timeout), have a fallback that does not show broken space.
Configure a JS error alert on the checkout to detect regressions before the buyer gets there.
runtime_breaking_reassurance.verification_notes: We will open the checkout headlessly with console capture and verify whether the trust components render without errors.

remediation_steps: If you use a single checkout provider, build contingency: a configured backup gateway + a documented manual process.
Review the contract with the provider to understand the SLA and downtime compensation.
Configure external monitoring (UptimeRobot) specifically against the checkout URL -- do not rely solely on the provider's status page.
Have a written checklist for when the provider goes down: notify the team, activate the backup, serve buyers via WhatsApp temporarily.
checkout_provider_path_weak.verification_notes: Structural single-provider risk -- verification is to re-project after you diversify or document contingency.

remediation_steps: Solve trust first -- it is cheap and has immediate impact (published policies + visible seals in <=1 day).
In parallel, configure basic GA4 + Pixel on home + checkout to have at least 1 measurement layer.
Do not deploy any paid campaign while these two gaps are open -- you are paying for ads without measurement and without closing sales.
Create a weekly checkpoint with the team reviewing progress on both fronts -- trust + measurement must not diverge.
trust_and_measurement_both_absent.verification_notes: We will re-verify the presence of policies, seals, and measurement tags in parallel and return joint progress.

remediation_steps: Review your consent banner: is it blocking GA4 / Pixel BEFORE the buyer even responds? Switch to conditional blocking.
Configure Google Consent Mode v2 -- enables aggregated measurement even without full consent.
On Meta, enable server-side Conversions API as a fallback when the in-browser Pixel is blocked.
Validate in opt-in and opt-out environments: in both cases, some measurement must arrive (even if limited under opt-out).
consent_undermining_measurement.verification_notes: We will navigate headlessly with consent rejected and consent accepted, checking which tags fire in each scenario.

remediation_steps: List how many different gateways / checkouts your flows use (site, mobile app, marketplace, WhatsApp).
Consolidate: ideally 1-2 gateways covering 95% of volume -- fragmentation increases cost + complexity.
For remaining flows that require a dedicated gateway, document the reason (regulatory, geographic) to justify it.
Standardize the visual checkout experience even when the backend varies -- the customer should not notice the fragmentation.
checkout_provider_fragmented.verification_notes: We will re-crawl the known flows' checkouts and identify how many distinct gateways appear.

remediation_steps: Publish a refund policy with the timeframe (14-day cooling-off period), process, and explicit contact email.
Link the policy in the checkout footer and in the post-purchase confirmation email.
Mention the policy on the product page near the buy button -- reduces dispute rate.
refund_policy_gap.verification_notes: We will re-crawl the footer + /refund /refunds /return-policy URLs to confirm presence and minimum density.

remediation_steps: Expose at least 2 support channels in the footer of every page: email + WhatsApp (or phone).
Configure an auto-response in <5 business minutes on primary channels -- the buyer should not wait longer for first contact.
Publish business hours ("Mon-Fri 9am-6pm") to set clear expectations.
Measure average time to first response and first-contact-resolution -- use as support KPIs.
support_unreachable.verification_notes: We will re-parse the footer + contact pages to detect available channels (email, WhatsApp, phone, chat).

remediation_steps: Review delivery times stated on the product vs actual times -- mismatches drive chargebacks.
If the timeline varies by region (shipping), display a postal-code calculator on the product page before checkout.
Communicate delays proactively by email when discovered -- do not let the buyer notice on their own and complain.
Include "estimated delivery time" on the post-purchase confirmation page based on real logistics data.
expectation_misalignment.verification_notes: We will re-crawl product pages looking for delivery/warranty claims and compare against real logistics data.

remediation_steps: Prioritize fixes on ALL fronts simultaneously: refund_policy + support_unreachable + expectation_misalignment cannot stay open together.
Monitor chargeback rate weekly -- alert if it exceeds 0.9% of volume (the gateway attention threshold).
Implement pre-dispute: before the customer files a chargeback, send a proactive "had an issue? we'll fix it" communication.
If the gateway suspends processing for high chargebacks, have plan B (another gateway) already implemented.
dispute_risk_elevated.verification_notes: Compound risk -- verification is to re-project against evidence after you close the individual fronts (refund, support, expectations).

remediation_steps: Expand the refund policy to include: exact timeframe, step-by-step process, explicit exceptions, contact channel.
Avoid legalese -- write it as you would explain to a friend what to do in case of a problem.
Add concrete examples ("If the product arrived broken, email X with a photo").
Link the FAQ with the 5 most common refund questions directly on the checkout.
refund_terms_too_thin.verification_notes: We will re-fetch the refund policy and measure content density + presence of key structural elements.

remediation_steps: On checkout, add microcopy or a banner: "Questions? Talk to our team: WhatsApp XXXX-XXXX".
Chat widget (if any) should be visible in the corner without blocking the payment fields.
The support button should open the PRIMARY channel, not a generic FAQ page.
Configure automatic handover to a human when the buyer is on checkout -- purchase intent > self-service intent.
support_hidden_at_purchase.verification_notes: We will navigate to checkout headlessly and detect the presence of a support channel visible above the fold.

remediation_steps: Map the activation flow: signup -> first login -> first value delivered. Time each step.
Eliminate technical blockers: mandatory email verification that can be deferred, a setup wizard with too many optional steps.
Ensure the first login delivers immediate value -- dashboard with sample data, guided tour, or short wizard.
Measure activation rate (% of users reaching the "aha moment") and configure an alert if it drops below baseline.
activation_blocked.verification_notes: We will run synthetic signup + first login headlessly and measure time to first perceived value.

remediation_steps: Reduce required fields at signup -- ideally only email + password or OAuth.
Additional data (company, role, size) should be asked progressively after the user has experienced the product.
Allow signup via OAuth (Google, GitHub) to reduce the barrier to entry.
If a setup wizard is necessary, show progress and allow "skip for now" on optional steps.
activation_friction_high.verification_notes: We will run signup headlessly and count required fields + steps until first access to the product.

remediation_steps: Audit the first screen after login: is there a clear primary CTA indicating the next step?
Implement a visible onboarding checklist (e.g., "Complete your profile", "Add your first item") with progress.
Empty states (empty lists, dashboard without data) should have a specific CTA to fill that context.
Avoid dense dashboards in the first session -- present the product in layers as the user demonstrates intent.
unclear_next_step.verification_notes: We will log in as a new user headlessly and identify whether there is a clear primary CTA in the first 3 seconds.

remediation_steps: For every list/dashboard that can be empty, design an empty state with: illustration, explanatory copy, specific CTA.
Empty states should guide toward the right action ("Add first item", "Invite team", "Connect integration").
If the empty state is common (e.g., new user), consider pre-populating with removable sample data.
Test in a synthetic session: can the new user exit the empty state in <30s?
empty_state_without_guidance.verification_notes: We will navigate the main surfaces as a user without data and verify that each empty state has a functional CTA.

remediation_steps: Count how many top-level items your navigation has -- if >7, simplify by grouping under categories.
Organize navigation by frequency of use -- most-used items at the top, rarely-used behind "More" or settings.
Implement global search (Cmd+K) to compensate for deep navigation -- a shortcut reduces clicks to hidden features.
Remove orphan items: does analytics show features never accessed? Hide or remove them.
navigation_overcomplex.verification_notes: We will re-parse the navigation structure and count depth + number of top-level items.

remediation_steps: List premium/advanced features and verify that each one has a discoverable entry point in the UI.
Add contextual hints: when the user does X, a tooltip suggests feature Y that complements it.
Configure product tours (Appcues, Intro.js) for recently introduced features.
Measure via analytics the % of users using each feature in 30 days -- features with <5% may need repositioning.
feature_discovery_poor.verification_notes: We will log in as a synthetic user and explore each menu to see if premium features appear at discoverable entry points.

remediation_steps: Add a visible upgrade CTA at touchpoints with gated features (e.g., "Premium" badge on the blocked item).
On the settings/pricing page, display a plan comparison with a clear feature-by-feature matrix.
Configure contextual prompts: when the user hits the plan limit, a modal offers a direct upgrade from that action.
Avoid aggressive paywalls on first contact -- deliver value first, upgrade later.
upgrade_invisible.verification_notes: We will re-crawl settings + pricing + gated features to confirm the presence and clarity of upgrade CTAs.

remediation_steps: Review when upgrade prompts appear -- they must not interrupt a critical task (e.g., during a creation flow).
Ideal timing: after the user has experienced value, is near the current plan's limit, or has tried a premium feature.
Avoid random upgrade popups -- use behavioral triggers (limit hit, feature accessed, 30 days active).
Measure the conversion rate of each upgrade trigger -- disable those with <1% CR.
upgrade_timing_wrong.verification_notes: Upgrade timing is a behavioral + product signal -- re-project against evidence after you adjust the triggers.

remediation_steps: For each current plan, define the next natural step: "Pro: 10 users -> Business: 50 users" is an example of clear expansion.
Configure an expansion-readiness signal: usage near the limit, new users added, advanced features adopted.
Implement self-service upgrade (no sales call required) for automatic expansion.
Measure monthly Net Revenue Retention -- healthy SaaS has NRR >110%.
no_expansion_path.verification_notes: Expansion is a product strategy -- verification is to re-project after you define a clear path and instrument it.

remediation_steps: Compare the landing page with the actual product: do they promise the same thing? Tone, layout, and value prop must align.
If the landing promises "simple and fast", the app's first login must deliver that feeling -- not a 15-step wizard.
Run a test with 5 new users: ask for specific feedback on "what the landing promised vs what the app delivered".
Review monthly -- landing evolves fast in SaaS, the app sometimes does not keep up.
landing_app_mismatch.verification_notes: We will navigate from landing to first login headlessly, comparing key visual elements and copy.

remediation_steps: Add copy next to the pay button reinforcing security: "Encrypted payment", "SSL certificate", "Your data is not shared".
Include microcopy explaining next steps: "After payment you will receive an email with the invoice and tracking".
Avoid generic copy ("Safe and fast") -- use phrases specific to the purchase context.
checkout_trust_language_absent.verification_notes: We will re-fetch the checkout and search for trust-language keywords near the payment fields.

remediation_steps: Replace vague CTAs ("Learn more", "Click here") with specific action verbs ("Buy", "See pricing", "Book a demo").
Each commercial page should have 1 dominant primary CTA -- secondary ones in outline/link style.
CTA copy must communicate what happens on the next click: "Add to cart" != "Go to payment".
cta_clarity_weak_on_commercial.verification_notes: We will re-parse the HTML and audit each CTA's text against a list of vague patterns.

remediation_steps: Rewrite the product description focusing on specific benefits, not generic features.
Replace template copy ("High-quality product") with verifiable claims ("100% organic cotton, GOTS certified").
Include usage context: who the ideal buyer is, when they use it, what problem it solves.
A/B test headlines -- generic copy typically loses 10-20% conversion vs specific copy.
product_page_copy_generic.verification_notes: We will re-crawl product pages and run semantic density analysis + comparison against known generic templates.

remediation_steps: Clarify what differentiates each plan -- specific features, not just numerical limits.
Visually highlight the recommended plan ("Most popular" badge, different color).
Answer the 5 most common questions directly on the pricing: "Can I change plans?", "Is there a commitment?", "Is there a trial?".
Remove pricing with "Contact us" where possible -- transparent prices convert better in SMB.
pricing_page_framing_unclear.verification_notes: We will re-fetch the pricing page and audit structure: distinguishable plans, recommended plan marked, FAQ present.

remediation_steps: Replace generic testimonials with testimonials including full name, photo, company/context.
Add concrete numbers: "12,000 customers", "4.8 stars across 3,200 reviews", "reduced X by Y%".
Include known customer logos (with permission) -- visual social proof has more impact than text.
Avoid generic badges without substance ("#1 in quality") that weaken rather than strengthen.
social_proof_generic.verification_notes: Phase 3 enables LLM enrichment to evaluate social proof quality. Today verification is structural: it counts how many testimonials have concrete attributes.

remediation_steps: Audit error messages on each critical form (signup, checkout) -- they must explain the specific problem, not just "Error".
Errors must indicate how to fix them: "Email already registered -- sign in?" instead of "Invalid email".
Visually highlight the field with the error (red border, icon) and auto-focus for the user to correct it.
Validate client-side in real-time -- do not wait for submit to show that the email has a typo.
form_error_messages_unhelpful.verification_notes: Phase 3 enables LLM enrichment. Today we will navigate headlessly submitting forms with invalid data and capture the error messages.

remediation_steps: Define the product's "aha moment" -- what is the smallest demonstration of value the new user can experience in <5min?
Redesign the first login to deliver that aha moment in the first session -- not the third.
If setup is necessary (import, integration), show a preview with sample data first.
Measure the "aha moment completion" rate and optimize to raise that metric -- it is the best predictor of retention.
onboarding_no_quick_win.verification_notes: Phase 3 enables LLM enrichment. Today we will run signup + first use headlessly and time until first value.

remediation_steps: Rewrite the hero headline to communicate WHAT the product does, FOR WHOM, and WHY it matters -- all in one sentence.
Move the value proposition above the fold: the visitor must understand it in 5 seconds without scrolling.
Reduce elements competing in the hero -- 1 headline, 1 sub-headline, 1 CTA. Remove banners, sliders and secondary widgets.
A/B test headlines using proven formulas: "[Desired result] without [main objection]" or "[Number] [customer type] already [result]".
value_proposition_buried.verification_notes: We will re-crawl the homepage and analyze above-the-fold content: presence of the value proposition, element density, and headline specificity.

remediation_steps: Add security copy next to the pay button: "SSL-encrypted payment", "30-day guarantee", "Your data is protected".
Include microcopy explaining what happens after purchase: "You will receive email confirmation within 2 minutes".
Position trust seals (SSL, Trustpilot, Google Reviews) visible without scrolling on the checkout page.
Remove any artificial urgency (fake timers, fabricated stock) -- replace with authentic urgency based on real data.
Add a satisfaction guarantee or summarized return policy directly on the pricing page.
trust_copy_absent_at_decision.verification_notes: We will re-fetch checkout, pricing and product pages, looking for trust-language keywords, security seals, and absence of dark patterns.

remediation_steps: Replace generic testimonials with testimonials including full name, role, company and a measurable result.
Position social proof next to decision CTAs (buy button, trial form, pricing page).
Add concrete numbers: "12,000 customers", "4.8 stars across 3,200 reviews", "reduced X by Y%".
Include real customer logos with permission -- visual social proof has more impact than generic text.
social_proof_ineffective.verification_notes: We will re-crawl commercial pages and evaluate social proof quality: testimonial specificity, proximity to CTAs, and presence of concrete data.

remediation_steps: Define CTA hierarchy: 1 primary (strong color), 1 secondary (outline), tertiary links -- never 2+ dominant buttons competing.
Replace vague labels ("Learn more", "Click here") with specific action verbs and results: "Start free trial", "See pricing", "Book a demo".
Review navigation labels: replace internal jargon with the language buyers use ("Dashboard" -> "Results panel").
Test heat maps to confirm the primary CTA receives the majority of clicks -- if not, adjust position and contrast.
cta_competing_or_unclear.verification_notes: We will re-parse the HTML of commercial pages and audit CTA hierarchy, button labels, and navigation against clarity standards.

remediation_steps: List the 5 most common buyer objections (research, support, negative reviews) and answer each one on the decision page.
Add an FAQ on the pricing page answering: "Can I cancel?", "Is there a guarantee?", "What happens if I don't like it?".
Include a comparison with alternatives on the product page — the buyer will compare anyway, better that they do it on your site.
Add explicit risk reversal next to the CTA: "30-day guarantee — if it doesn't fit, we refund 100%".
objection_unaddressed.verification_notes: We will re-fetch pricing and product pages, verifying presence of FAQ, guarantees, comparisons and risk reversal elements.

remediation_steps: Create a brand voice guide defining tone, vocabulary and promises — distribute it to everyone who writes copy.
Audit homepage, pricing, product and checkout side by side — identify contradictions in tone and promise.
Unify terminology: if the homepage says "simple", pricing cannot list 47 features in a complex table.
Implement cross-page review before publishing — each page must reinforce, not contradict, the others.
copy_cross_page_inconsistent.verification_notes: We will re-crawl multiple pages and compare tone, terminology and promises cross-page to detect contradictions.

remediation_steps: Map each page to its funnel stage (awareness, consideration, decision) and adjust copy to answer that stage's question.
Rewrite product descriptions focusing on benefits and outcomes — not generic vendor features.
Redesign onboarding copy to promise and deliver a quick win in <5 minutes — "In 2 minutes you'll see X".
Replace technical error messages with human language that explains the problem AND how to fix it.
Test copy with 5 real users: ask "What does this page want you to do?" — if they don't know, rewrite it.
copy_funnel_misalignment.verification_notes: We will re-crawl product pages, onboarding and forms, evaluating copy specificity, presence of quick wins and quality of error messages.

remediation_steps: Compare primary and translated versions side by side — identify where urgency, social proof and CTA were "flattened" into generic text.
Use transcreation (not literal translation) for CTAs, headlines and social proof — hire native copywriters who understand persuasion.
Keep urgency and scarcity elements in translated versions — adapt culturally instead of removing.
Implement a review process for localized copy: translator + native reviewer + marketing approval before publishing.
localization_persuasion_lost.verification_notes: We will re-crawl both versions (primary and translated) and compare persuasive structure: presence of urgency, specificity of social proof, CTA power.

remediation_steps: Replace generic buttons ("Submit", "Send", "OK") with specific action-oriented text ("Start free trial", "Send my proposal").
Review form labels: each label must explain WHAT to fill in and WHY — e.g., "Work email (so we can send your access)" instead of just "Email".
Add inline helper text to the fields that cause the most abandonment — placeholder with a real example, not a repeat of the label.
Rewrite error messages in human language: "The email needs an @ — check it and try again" instead of "Invalid input".
Test micro-copy with 3 users: ask them to fill the form out loud — where they pause or ask is where the copy fails.
micro_copy_friction_high.verification_notes: We will re-crawl pages with forms and evaluate the quality of labels, buttons, placeholders and error messages.

remediation_steps: Rewrite keyword-stuffed H1s to communicate value to the buyer — use the keyword in the title tag and meta, but the H1 must sell.
Separate SEO content blocks from the conversion path: move keyword-rich paragraphs below the fold or to dedicated subpages.
Unify title tag and H1: if the title says "Best CRM Software 2026", the H1 should say something like "The CRM that [specific result]".
Review image alt text: it must describe the image for accessibility AND include the keyword naturally — no keyword spam.
Assess whether large SEO content blocks below the fold are diluting the conversion message — consider moving them to /blog or /resources.
seo_conversion_conflict.verification_notes: We will re-analyze H1, title tags and copy of commercial pages to detect whether SEO-conversion tension has been resolved.

remediation_steps: Update the copyright year in the footer to the current year — configure auto-update if possible.
Search for and remove references to past dates, expired promotions and outdated metrics across all commercial pages.
Implement a quarterly copy review: schedule a content audit every 3 months to detect references that have aged.
Replace social proof metrics with current numbers — if the homepage said "1000+ customers" in 2024, update to the real number.
Configure automated alerts to detect copy with hard-coded dates that will become stale.
copy_stale_references.verification_notes: We will re-crawl pages and verify: updated copyright, no past dates, no expired promotions and current social proof metrics.

remediation_steps: Audit all commercial pages (checkout, pricing, product, homepage) for outdated references — dates, promotions, metrics.
Update or remove any hard-coded dates, expired offers, and stale competitive claims.
Set up a quarterly content review calendar specifically for high-conversion pages.
Implement dynamic copyright year and "last updated" timestamps that auto-refresh.
Create a content freshness checklist for each page type with specific review criteria.
commercial_page_stale.verification_notes: Re-crawl will check for updated dates, removed stale references, and fresh content indicators on commercial pages.

remediation_steps: Review all pricing page claims — competitor comparisons, feature lists, promotional offers — and update to current state.
Remove or update any "limited time" language that has expired.
Verify that plan names, pricing tiers, and feature comparisons match the current product.
Update any ROI calculators, case studies, or metrics cited on the pricing page.
Set a monthly reminder to review pricing page content (pricing pages age fastest).
pricing_page_outdated.verification_notes: Re-crawl will verify pricing page content is updated — no stale dates, expired offers, or outdated competitive claims.

remediation_steps: Update all testimonial dates to recent ones — or remove dates entirely if testimonials are evergreen.
Refresh customer count metrics, revenue numbers, and usage statistics to current values.
Replace logo walls with current customer logos and add "as of [date]" qualifiers to any metrics.
Implement dynamic social proof (real-time user counts, recent review scores) where possible.
Set up quarterly social proof refresh — request new testimonials, update case study numbers.
social_proof_expired.verification_notes: Re-crawl will check for updated social proof — no stale dates on testimonials, current metrics, fresh customer references.

remediation_steps: Run a full content audit across all commercial pages — flag everything with dates > 6 months old.
Prioritize updates by page traffic and conversion impact — start with checkout and pricing pages.
Implement an automated content freshness monitoring system (this Vestigio finding will re-check each cycle).
Assign content ownership per page — each commercial page should have a named owner responsible for quarterly reviews.
Consider AI-assisted content refresh tools to accelerate updates across multiple pages.
content_decay_progression.verification_notes: Multi-cycle monitoring — this finding will track whether staleness scores improve across subsequent audit cycles.

remediation_steps: List HTTP resources loaded on HTTPS pages — use DevTools Console, which reports mixed content warnings.
Migrate all resources to HTTPS: images, scripts, CSS, fonts, iframes.
Configure Content-Security-Policy with `upgrade-insecure-requests` to force the browser to attempt HTTPS automatically.
Fix hardcoded internal links with http:// — migrate to protocol-relative (//) or https://.
mixed_content_exposure.verification_notes: We will load critical URLs in headless and capture mixed content warnings from the console.

remediation_steps: Remove administrative URLs from predictable paths (/admin, /wp-admin, /.env, /api/debug) — 404 or redirect to home.
Add authentication to every administrative endpoint — do not rely on obscurity.
Configure robots.txt to disallow sensitive routes (but know that robots.txt is not a security mechanism).
Run regular scanners (Nuclei, Katana) to detect exposed endpoints in production.
sensitive_endpoint_exposed.verification_notes: We will run the Nuclei + Katana scanner against the public infrastructure to confirm whether sensitive endpoints remain exposed.

remediation_steps: Audit all scripts loaded on checkout — eliminate non-essential third-parties.
For essential third-parties, implement Subresource Integrity (SRI): `<script integrity="sha384-...">` prevents injection if the CDN is compromised.
Configure a restrictive CSP on checkout: only hashes/nonces of known scripts.
Monitor changes in the script inventory — alert if a new script appears in production without a deploy.
checkout_script_hijack_risk.verification_notes: We will load checkout in headless, extract the script list, and compare against a known whitelist.

remediation_steps: Session cookies: configure `HttpOnly`, `Secure`, `SameSite=Lax` or `Strict`.
CSRF tokens on every sensitive form — signup, checkout, password change.
Implement session ID rotation after login to prevent session fixation.
Configure a reasonable session timeout (30-60 min of inactivity) and renewal on user action.
buyer_session_theft_risk.verification_notes: We will navigate in headless and inspect session cookie attributes plus the presence of CSRF tokens in forms.

remediation_steps: Configure X-Frame-Options: DENY (or SAMEORIGIN if you embed checkout in a legitimate own iframe).
Or use CSP `frame-ancestors 'none'` to block any external site from placing your checkout in an iframe.
Test with clickjack testers (e.g., /test-clickjacking tools) to confirm the checkout does not render in an external iframe.
Review sensitive-action pages (password change, delete account) — apply the same protection.
checkout_clickjack_risk.verification_notes: We will re-fetch headers from the checkout URL and validate the presence of X-Frame-Options or CSP frame-ancestors.

remediation_steps: Customize 404 and 500 pages — do not expose stack traces, framework versions, or server paths.
Configure a generic error handler in production that logs details server-side but returns a friendly message.
Audit API JSON responses — do not include exception details in production.
Remove HTML comments with sensitive information (versions, internal paths) from output.
error_page_information_leak.verification_notes: We will request known error URLs (404, 500) and analyze returned content for leaks.

remediation_steps: Verify that payment forms send data via HTTPS — the form's target must be https://.
Never receive the PAN (card number) directly in your backend — use gateway tokenization.
If you need to show the card mask (last 4 digits), store only token + mask, never the full PAN.
Configure PCI-DSS SAQ A or A-EP according to your gateway integration — document the scope.
payment_data_unencrypted.verification_notes: We will submit test data in headless and inspect network traffic to confirm encryption + tokenization.

remediation_steps: Configure correct SPF, DKIM, and DMARC in the DNS of the sending domain.
Use a dedicated subdomain for transactional mail (e.g., mail.domain.com) separate from marketing.
Monitor bounce rate + spam complaint rate — target is <5% bounce and <0.1% complaint.
Use a reputable provider (SendGrid, AWS SES, Postmark) instead of your own SMTP for better deliverability.
email_deliverability_risk.verification_notes: We will query the domain's DNS (SPF/DKIM/DMARC) plus reputation analysis via MX Toolbox.

remediation_steps: Audit Access-Control-Allow-Origin on APIs — never use `*` on endpoints with cookies/auth.
Explicit allowlist of permitted origins — use a list instead of a wildcard in production.
Configure Access-Control-Allow-Credentials only when necessary, paired with a specific origin.
Never expose sensitive endpoints (password change, payment) via CORS to an external origin.
cors_misconfiguration_risk.verification_notes: We will issue OPTIONS/GET requests against critical APIs and analyze the returned CORS headers.

remediation_steps: Implement rate limiting on critical APIs: login (5/min), signup (3/hour), checkout (10/min per IP).
Use a WAF (Cloudflare, AWS WAF) as the first layer — rate limit + bot protection.
Configure alerts for anomalous peaks: 100 attempts/min on login = credential stuffing attack.
Return HTTP 429 with Retry-After header — legitimate clients understand, bots give up.
rate_limiting_absent_on_commerce.verification_notes: We will fire controlled bursts against login/signup endpoints to validate the presence of rate limiting.

remediation_steps: Replace sequential IDs in order URLs (/order/123) with UUIDs or opaque tokens (/order/a7f3...).
Validate authorization on every request — a user can only see their own orders, not by knowing the URL.
Route audit logs for order access — detects enumeration / IDOR attacks.
Configure temporary tokens for public links (tracking, confirmation) with short expiration.
predictable_order_urls.verification_notes: We will analyze the order URL structure and test whether changing the ID returns another user's data.

remediation_steps: Investigate immediately: review recent commits, loaded scripts, and access logs on the checkout.
Rotate all gateway credentials + exposed API keys — a payment surface compromise leaks buyer data.
Temporarily disable checkout until forensics is complete — ongoing fraud compounds fast.
Notify the gateway + authorities (in case of LGPD breach) + affected buyers — required by law.
payment_surface_compromised.verification_notes: Critical incident — triggers Nuclei + Katana full scan + script analysis + diff against known-clean baseline.

remediation_steps: Audit external links on the homepage and product pages — each external link is a traffic diversion opportunity.
If you use affiliates, use official link shorteners (not generic redirects that can be hijacked).
Configure a restrictive CSP to prevent injection of external links via compromised scripts.
Monitor outbound traffic analytics — abnormal spikes may indicate injected malicious redirects.
channel_traffic_divertible.verification_notes: We will navigate the site in headless, collecting all outbound links and comparing to the expected allowlist.

remediation_steps: Identify commerce operation endpoints publicly exposed: dashboards, internal APIs, webhooks.
Implement authentication + IP allowlist on every administrative endpoint.
Remove references to internal endpoints from public HTML/JS — operators should not appear in robots.txt or source.
Segment the network: operational endpoints in a private VPC, not in the same cluster as the public site.
commerce_operations_exposed.verification_notes: We will trigger Katana + Nuclei against the public infrastructure to detect exposed operational endpoints.

remediation_steps: Campaign landing pages must inherit trust markers from the main site — policies, contact, badges visible.
Avoid landings on subdomains or separate domains without trust markers — the buyer does not know it is you.
If you must use a separate domain (e.g., Black Friday campaign), replicate visual identity + trust markers from the main one.
Audit active landings quarterly — some become orphaned without maintenance and regress in trust.
traffic_landing_low_trust_posture.verification_notes: We will re-crawl known landings and measure the presence of trust markers on each one.

remediation_steps: Active incident — escalate to the security team for immediate response.
Identify the vector: injected script, DNS hijack, compromised certificate, or leaked credential.
Isolate the vector (remove script, rotate credential, revoke cert) and document the incident timeline.
Communicate with affected buyers transparently — silence during an incident damages reputation more than the incident itself.
channel_compromise_visible.verification_notes: Critical — triggers a full scan (Nuclei + Katana + brand-intel) to map the extent of the compromise.

remediation_steps: Audit purchase/promotion endpoints for abusable patterns: sequential IDs, promos without validation, accepted price override.
Implement server-side validation on all pricing — never trust the price coming from the client.
Aggressive rate limit on critical endpoints: checkout, apply_coupon, add_to_cart.
Configure fraud detection: suspicious patterns (same card, same IP, multiple emails) block with manual review.
commercial_path_abuse_friendly.verification_notes: We will trigger Katana deep discovery to map abusable endpoints, then revalidate after your fixes.

remediation_steps: Active financial incident — investigate immediately which purchases/promos have been exploited.
Block the specific vector (abused coupon, manipulated price, checkout bypass).
Calculate accumulated loss and decide whether rollback of fraudulent transactions is feasible or chargeback is cheaper.
Audit all active promotions with the same logic — exploits typically work across multiple campaigns.
economic_exploitation_active.verification_notes: Re-trigger Katana against the compromised endpoints to confirm the patch and monitor abuse patterns.

remediation_steps: Audit SSL certificates: expiration, authority, algorithm. Renew before the 30-day expiration window.
Configure auto-renewal (Let's Encrypt, AWS ACM) to avoid an expired cert in production.
Monitor the trust chain: intermediate certs + root trust.
Configure HSTS preload to mitigate downgrade attacks.
checkout_trust_brittle_infrastructure.verification_notes: We will validate cert + chain of the checkout via an SSL scan (openssl s_client / Qualys SSL Labs).

remediation_steps: Migrate discount logic to the backend — the client must never compute promotions.
Validate server-side: coupon code exists, has not expired, applies to these products, has not exceeded its limit.
Log each coupon application with user_id + IP to detect abuse patterns.
Review active coupons quarterly — remove those that do not generate revenue but can still be abused.
promotion_logic_exposed.verification_notes: We will dispatch Katana on cart/coupon endpoints to revalidate the abusable surface.

remediation_steps: All stock + price validation must happen server-side at checkout — never trust the client cart.
Implement a security token per cart session — invalidate it if manipulation is detected.
Review cart PATCH/PUT endpoints: they must authenticate the user and validate ownership before changing.
Log divergences between client and server cart to detect manipulation attempts.
cart_variant_weak_control.verification_notes: We will probe cart manipulation endpoints (change price/qty via direct request) and confirm rejection.

remediation_steps: Audit URLs /refund, /discount, /coupon, /comp — are they publicly accessible? They must require authentication.
Administrative refund/discount routes must sit behind staff auth + audit log.
Remove query strings that accept price override (?discount=50, ?price=1) — a common vulnerability.
Monitor logs for exploitation patterns — spikes in /refund without a corresponding transaction = abuse.
hidden_discount_refund_route.verification_notes: Dispatch Katana against discount/refund paths to confirm concealment + authentication.

remediation_steps: Replace sequential IDs in critical endpoints with opaque UUIDs: /api/order/abc-123-def instead of /api/order/1.
Implement per-endpoint authorization — users only access their own resources (IDOR protection).
Audit public APIs: which ones expose resource enumeration? Add rate limit + auth.
Run an IDOR scanner regularly — test whether changing the ID returns other users' data.
guessable_business_endpoint.verification_notes: Katana dispatch focused on numerically enumerable endpoints + IDOR attempt.

remediation_steps: Identify pricing variants (BRL, USD, promo region, B2B) and consolidate into a single server-side validation.
Eliminate alternative pricing routes without controls — the buyer must not choose which price they pay.
Configure a feature flag with a list of authorized customers for special pricing — not via an exposed URL.
Log transactions outside the default pricing — alert for manual review.
alternate_pricing_safeguard_bypass.verification_notes: Probe alternative pricing paths and compare the final price with the one expected by public pricing.

remediation_steps: Review public JS bundles — remove sensitive paths/endpoints that can be discovered via crawl.
Use source maps in dev, disable them in production.
Obfuscate (not security, but raises the bar) critical pricing/checkout code in the production build.
Audit quarterly: which endpoints are referenced in public JS?
js_discovered_purchase_variant.verification_notes: Katana discovery on JS bundles to map referenced endpoints + compare against authorized surfaces.

remediation_steps: Audit dynamic routes (regex routes, wildcard routes) — they easily introduce ACL gaps.
Use a centralized authorization framework (policies/guards) instead of inline checks per route.
Run automated authorization tests: every protected route with a list of who can access it.
Review after every deploy that adds or modifies dynamic routes.
dynamic_route_weak_control.verification_notes: Katana exploration of dynamic routes with auth bypass attempts.

remediation_steps: Measure average support resolution time broken down by category — identify which ticket type consumes most resources.
Solve the root cause of recurring tickets — each ticket avoided is cost reduction + buyer satisfaction.
Automate responses to frequent questions via FAQ + chatbot — scale support without increasing headcount.
Review monthly: which products/features generate the most support? Prioritize UX there.
hidden_support_burden.verification_notes: Support is an operational metric external to the site — to be re-projected after you document tickets + resolution time.

remediation_steps: Audit variant logic (size, color, region) at checkout — ensure each variant has distinct price + stock validated.
Implement consistent server-side validation: qty, variant, price must match the real catalog.
Test edge cases: buying an out-of-stock variant, a nonexistent variant, a variant of an archived product.
Log divergences — an attempt to buy an invalid variant is a signal of automated probing.
alternate_variant_control_breakdown.verification_notes: Katana + variant manipulation probe (nonexistent IDs, mix of product + orphan variant).

remediation_steps: Compound incident — multiple exploitation vectors active simultaneously. Escalate to the security team.
Run a full scan (Katana + Nuclei) to map the extent of the problem.
Prioritize patching of vectors with the highest financial impact (price, coupon, checkout) first.
Configure continuous monitoring post-remediation — compound exploitation typically retries re-entry.
deep_commerce_exploitation_risk.verification_notes: Full Katana + Nuclei scan + revalidation of each identified vector.

remediation_steps: Measure latency of critical checkout endpoints in production — identify P95 and P99.
Optimize backend queries for checkout: indexes, caching, n+1 query removal.
Configure a CDN for checkout static assets (CSS, JS, images) — reduces TTFB.
Implement a graceful timeout: if the API takes >5s, show a message to the user instead of hanging.
checkout_api_latency_degraded.verification_notes: We will navigate the checkout in headless capturing timing for each request and return P50/P95.

remediation_steps: Run Lighthouse on product and category pages — target LCP <2.5s, TTFB <600ms.
Optimize images: WebP/AVIF format, lazy loading, responsive sizes.
Remove non-essential third-party scripts or move them to async/defer.
Configure aggressive cache for static assets (1 year via Cache-Control) + ETag for HTML.
commercial_pages_slow.verification_notes: We will run Lighthouse in headless against commercial pages and compare against the previous baseline.

remediation_steps: Campaign landing pages must be minimalist — remove widgets, chats, non-essential analytics.
Keep only 1 primary CTA above the fold + a benefits section + social proof.
Test with PageSpeed Insights — ads landings should have LCP <2s to maximize quality score.
Host landings on optimized infra (Vercel, Netlify) instead of the same stack as the main app.
paid_landing_overloaded.verification_notes: Lighthouse + analysis of resource weight loaded on paid landings.

remediation_steps: List third-party scripts on checkout: analytics, chat, pixel, A/B testing. Total weight?
Eliminate non-essentials (A/B testing on checkout is a risk, chat can load later).
For essentials (GA, Pixel), load async + after main content render.
Configure a timeout — if a third-party does not respond in 3s, give up and do not block render.
third_party_weight_delays_trust.verification_notes: Capture network trace of the checkout and classify requests by origin (own vs third-party).

remediation_steps: Identify critical checkout dependencies on third-parties — gateway, anti-fraud, tax calc.
Configure a fallback: if anti-fraud does not respond, allow the purchase with a manual-review flag after the fact.
For the gateway, have a backup configured (Stripe + Mercado Pago) and automatic switchover via feature flag.
Monitor provider status pages + configure an alert on your side if you detect degradation.
checkout_brittle_third_party.verification_notes: Navigate checkout headless + simulate third-party failure to see if graceful degradation works.

remediation_steps: Capture the last 10 network errors on checkout — prioritize those affecting payment.
Configure automatic retry on non-idempotent requests with exponential backoff.
For critical requests (payment_intent), ensure an idempotency key to prevent duplication on retry.
Log success rate per endpoint on checkout — alert if it falls below 98%.
purchase_blocked_failing_requests.verification_notes: Navigate checkout multiple times in headless + collect failed requests + success statistics.

remediation_steps: If an analytics tag blocks or delays the checkout, reconfigure it to load async/defer.
If GA/Pixel breaks the page when blocked by an adblocker, wrap it in try/catch + silent fallback.
Configure tags with a performance budget — if they exceed X ms of execution, abort.
Use Tag Manager with conditional triggers to measure without blocking interaction.
measurement_breaks_revenue_path.verification_notes: Load checkout headless with/without adblocker + measure impact on render time.

remediation_steps: Audit load order: the payment button must not be clickable before critical scripts (anti-fraud, tokenizer) have loaded.
Disable the submit button until essential scripts are ready — show a loading state.
Configure `DOMContentLoaded` events + deps check before activating the purchase flow.
Test on slow 3G — deps must load within 5s or the flow must fail gracefully.
purchase_before_deps_ready.verification_notes: Load checkout under 3G throttle + test clicking submit before load completes.

remediation_steps: Trust markers (SSL badges, payment logos, policy) must be in the initial HTML — not loaded via JS.
Avoid loading logos via an external CDN that may be slow or block — host them internally.
Prioritize LCP — trust badges above the fold must appear in <2.5s.
Use preload hints for critical trust images: `<link rel="preload" as="image">`.
trust_assets_late_load.verification_notes: Capture timing of trust marker loads on checkout + compare against LCP target.

remediation_steps: Mobile has more limited CPU/network — aggressively reduce JS executed on the first paint.
Use code splitting: load only the code needed for the current route, lazy-load the rest.
Remove unnecessary polyfills — modern mobile browsers do not need IE support.
Measure JS main thread blocking under mobile simulation — target <200ms of long tasks.
mobile_heavy_runtime_chain.verification_notes: Lighthouse mobile + analysis of JS bundle size + long tasks on the main thread.

remediation_steps: Test payment deps (gateway scripts, tokenizer) specifically on mobile — web deps sometimes fail in WebView.
Configure a payment method fallback when the primary script fails (e.g., fallback from Stripe Elements to redirect flow).
Monitor mobile-specific JS errors — alert when they diverge from desktop.
Test on real iOS Safari + Chrome Android, not just emulators — each has different quirks.
mobile_trust_payment_deps_failing.verification_notes: Headless mobile viewport + capture payment script failures + success rate per browser.

remediation_steps: Map which trust surfaces depend on third-parties (dynamic badges, review widgets).
For each third-party dep, measure uptime + configure a static fallback if it fails.
Prefer trust markers served from your own domain — self-hosted badges do not depend on an external provider.
Configure specific monitoring for trust surfaces — provider downtime = immediate conversion drop.
trust_surfaces_unstable_deps.verification_notes: Load home + product + checkout + measure % of trust markers that render without failure.

remediation_steps: Rewrite title tags of commercial pages: 50-60 chars, including brand + product + benefit.
Meta description: 140-160 chars describing product + call-to-action. Avoid duplicates between pages.
Ensure the title and H1 of the page are aligned but not identical — title optimized for search, H1 for humans.
Audit quarterly which titles rank and which do not appear in the top 10 — adjust copy of underperformers.
commercial_pages_weak_search_representat.verification_notes: Re-parse HTML + extract title/meta/H1 of commercial pages and compare against best practice.

remediation_steps: Configure Open Graph tags: og:title, og:description, og:image (1200x630), og:url on all commercial pages.
Twitter Card: summary_large_image with an image optimized for sharing.
Test previews in official debuggers: developers.facebook.com/tools/debug + cards-dev.twitter.com.
Customize preview image per product category — a generic logo wastes the share.
social_previews_fail_commercial_value.verification_notes: Re-parse HTML + extract og/twitter meta tags + validate that image URLs return 200.

remediation_steps: Define a short brand guide: logo, palette, typography, tone of voice. Publish it internally.
Audit existing surfaces (home, app, email, landing, social) — eliminate logo/color/copy divergences.
Centralize brand assets in your own CDN — avoid old versions circulating.
Configure a pre-deploy checklist to review visual changes against the brand guide.
brand_inconsistent_across_surfaces.verification_notes: Re-crawl main surfaces + extract logo + palette + compare with baseline brand assets.

remediation_steps: Check robots.txt + meta robots on commercial pages — they must not have accidental noindex.
Configure correct canonical tags — point to the preferred version of the page (without query-param noise).
Submit an updated sitemap.xml to Google Search Console + Bing Webmaster Tools.
Generate internal links to commercial pages from home/blog — pages without internal backlinks rarely rank.
commercial_pages_unlikely_indexed.verification_notes: Re-parse HTML + robots.txt + sitemap.xml + validate presence/absence of noindex.

remediation_steps: Add JSON-LD Schema on commercial pages: Product (price, stock), Offer, Organization, BreadcrumbList.
Use the correct schema.org vocab — Product on product pages, Article on blog, Review on testimonials.
Validate with Google's Rich Results Test — invalid schema does not help SEO.
Monitor rich results in Search Console — schema-error tickets appear there.
weak_semantic_intent_signals.verification_notes: Re-parse HTML + extract JSON-LD + validate schema against expectation (Product on product, etc.).

remediation_steps: Social preview images should emphasize product + benefit, not just the logo.
Preview title should communicate commercial value — "50% OFF" or "Free shipping" works better than a generic title.
A/B test different images for the same URL — measure which generates more share CTR.
Every ad/social campaign should have a preview optimized for the context — do not use the page default.
previews_disconnected_from_conversion.verification_notes: Re-crawl + analyze relevance of og:title + og:image versus the page's commercial copy.

remediation_steps: Add commercial pages to sitemap.xml — prioritize product and category with priority > 0.8.
Build internal linking: home → categories → products (3 clicks max).
If you have filters (color, size), use canonical for variants and avoid duplication.
Request reindexing in Search Console after significant structural changes.
commercial_pages_not_exposed_for_discove.verification_notes: Crawl internal links from the home + validate sitemap.xml + compare coverage vs critical pages.

remediation_steps: Register common defensive domains: typos (.com.br, .net, .shop), variants (with hyphen, without hyphen).
Configure 301 redirects from defensive domains to the main domain — recovers typo traffic.
For lookalikes actively competing: register the trademark and send a takedown notice via the registrar.
Monitor SERPs regularly — detect new lookalikes and act before they gain traction.
lookalike_domain_competing_for_traffic.verification_notes: Re-trigger the brand intelligence scan to list similar domains still active.

remediation_steps: Identify sites copying your visuals/copy — capture screenshots as evidence.
Send a DMCA takedown (US) or LGPD/CDC notice (BR) to the cloned site's host.
If the clone sells counterfeit product, escalate to Federal Police / consumer-protection agencies plus the marketplace where it sells (Mercado Livre, Shopee).
Set up Google Alerts for unauthorized brand mentions.
external_sites_mimicking_brand.verification_notes: Brand intelligence scan re-validating known clone sites plus active search for new ones.

remediation_steps: Audit paid ads using your brand name — competitors may be running branded search on top of you.
Register your trademark to protect it inside Google Ads and Facebook Ads.
Set defensive bids on branded keywords — don't let competitors steal cheap traffic.
Monitor Google search suggestions — "brand X complaint" or "brand X scam" indicates a reputation issue.
brand_traffic_exposed_to_deceptive_surfa.verification_notes: Brand intel scan across SERPs plus bid analysis on branded keywords and search suggestions.

remediation_steps: Identify suspicious domains showing up in search results when users search your brand plus "buy".
Send takedowns to the registrars and hosts of each suspicious domain identified.
If a domain ranks organically above yours, invest in SEO to reclaim position.
Bid on branded plus "buy" queries in Google Ads to dominate the SERP legitimately.
suspicious_domains_capturing_purchase_in.verification_notes: Brand intel re-scan of branded purchase queries plus ranking check versus the official domain.

remediation_steps: Alert buyers via email and social channels about identified phishing sites.
Configure DMARC strict (p=reject) on your domain to prevent email spoofing.
Report phishing pages to Google Safe Browsing and Microsoft Defender SmartScreen — both remove them from browsing.
Create a /security or /phishing page on the official site documenting known scams and how to spot them.
customers_exposed_to_phishing_surfaces.verification_notes: Brand intel scan plus phishing URL reporter (Google/Microsoft APIs) to confirm removal.

remediation_steps: Consolidate domains and subdomains: drop unnecessary variants or set canonical plus redirect.
Unify branding across every active surface — inconsistency confuses the buyer.
Define a single master domain for ads, email, and social — avoids traffic dispersion.
Quarterly, audit which domain variants are still live and why.
brand_presence_diluted_across_variants.verification_notes: Brand intel mapping domain/subdomain variants and reconciling against the master.

remediation_steps: Configure a recovery email sequence for abandoned carts: 1h, 24h, and 72h after abandonment.
Simplify checkout: reduce fields, offer guest checkout, show progress.
Add urgency/scarcity when appropriate (real limited stock, not fake).
Offering a small discount (5-10%) in the last recovery email recovers 5-15% of abandonments.
checkout_abandonment_revenue_leak.verification_notes: Re-pull Shopify metrics to confirm change in abandonment rate plus recovery rate.

remediation_steps: Configure real-time inventory sync between Shopify and promotion channels (ads, email, affiliates).
Auto-pause ad campaigns when a product goes out of stock.
Add "Notify when available" on sold-out products to capture demand.
Prioritize replenishment of the most promoted products — stock lost during a campaign is lost revenue.
promoted_product_out_of_stock.verification_notes: Re-pull Shopify plus cross-reference against active campaigns.

remediation_steps: Audit the top 10 refund reasons in Shopify — identify patterns by product/SKU.
Improve photos and descriptions of the most refunded products — expectation mismatch is the common cause.
If a specific product has >15% refund rate, consider delisting or renegotiating with the supplier.
Set up a pre-sale checklist: visible FAQs, sizing guide, clear refund policy.
high_refund_rate_eroding_revenue.verification_notes: Re-pull Shopify to confirm refund rate trend plus breakdown by product.

remediation_steps: Integrate a secondary gateway (Stripe plus Mercado Pago, for example) for redundancy.
Configure smart routing: PIX for BR (cheaper), card for international.
Document a contingency plan: if the primary gateway goes down, how to switch in <15min.
Negotiate contracts with an SLA — a gateway without uptime guarantee is a bet.
single_payment_gateway_risk.verification_notes: Re-pull Shopify payment config plus validate the presence of active alternative gateways.

remediation_steps: Audit active coupons — limit usage per customer (1x), per email, per IP.
Configure fraud detection: same CPF buying 10x with a coupon equals abuse.
Expire coupons regularly — permanent coupons are an invite to share on sites like Cuponeria.
Measure post-discount margin per campaign — some coupons may be negative.
discount_abuse_pattern.verification_notes: Re-pull Shopify discount analytics plus cross-reference against suspicious usage patterns.

remediation_steps: Identify the second-best platform for your ICP — testing doesn't need to wait until the first one breaks.
Shift 15-25% of budget to the second platform in mirror campaigns (same creative, same audience).
Document the stand-up of a new ad account: credentials, tags, pixel, approval — expected standup time is 1-3 weeks.
Configure account health alerts on both platforms (disable, policy warning, spend anomaly).
ad_spend_platform_concentration_risk.verification_notes: Re-pull ad platform snapshots to confirm spend is distributed across >= 2 platforms without ROAS drop.

remediation_steps: Connect your commerce platform (Shopify, Nuvemshop, or Stripe) in Settings → Data Sources — the wizard runs OAuth and reconciles history in minutes.
Validate that orders from the last 30 days are being imported — calculable ROAS will appear in the dashboard immediately.
Configure UTM tagging on campaigns (utm_source, utm_campaign) for attribution per creative, not just per platform.
After 30 days with conversion tracking active, review creatives: pause the bottom 20% by ROAS, double budget on the top 10%.
ads_without_conversion_visibility.verification_notes: Re-pull the commerce integration to confirm orders are flowing; check total_ad_spend_monthly versus attributed revenue.

remediation_steps: Identify the creative in Ads Manager pointing to the dead URL — the finding carries the creative name plus platform.
Update the creative's destination URL to a live, relevant page for the same audience.
If the page was intentionally removed, pause the creative immediately — every hour live is wasted spend.
Configure a 301 redirect from the old URL to the new one if other sources also link it (SEO, email, etc.).
ad_creative_dead_destination.verification_notes: Re-fetch the destination URL to confirm HTTP 200 without an excessive redirect chain.

remediation_steps: Add a trust badge (SSL seal, reviews widget, security logo) on the landing page the ad points to.
Place testimonials/reviews next to sensitive-data fields — proximity is what matters.
Ensure the privacy policy is linked in the same viewport as the sensitive-data field.
If possible, add structured data such as Organization/LocalBusiness for trust via rich snippets.
ad_creative_landing_trust_gap.verification_notes: Re-crawl the landing page to confirm presence of >= 2 trust signals co-located with sensitive fields.

remediation_steps: Reduce the landing page form to 6 fields or fewer — remove anything not essential for the first conversion.
If more data is needed, split into steps (guest checkout → post-conversion collection) instead of a monolithic form.
Remove fields like "confirm email" or "landline" that add friction without real conversion value.
A/B test: short version versus current version. Measure conversion rate per variant over 14 days.
ad_creative_form_friction_waste.verification_notes: Re-crawl the landing page to confirm the main form has <= 8 fields.

remediation_steps: Test the landing page on a real mobile device (Chrome DevTools mobile viewport doesn't replace a real device).
Identify scripts blocking render — lazy-load anything not above the fold.
Make sure the primary CTA ("Buy", "Subscribe") is visible without scrolling in the mobile viewport.
If possible, use AMP or optimize the critical rendering path for <3s first meaningful paint.
ad_creative_mobile_checkout_degraded.verification_notes: Re-run mobile verification to confirm the commercial path completes without step failures and in <8s.

remediation_steps: Open the ad creative in your Ads Manager and read the headline plus body copy side by side with the landing page.
Update the landing page H1 to echo the ad's primary promise — exact word match increases perceived relevance.
Ensure the CTA on the landing page matches the action the ad promised (e.g., ad says "Try Free" but LP says "Contact Sales" is a mismatch).
If the ad mentions a specific offer (discount, free trial, feature), make sure it's visible above the fold on the LP.
Alternatively, update the ad copy to match what the LP actually delivers, rather than what you wish it delivered.
ad_creative_message_mismatch.verification_notes: Re-fetch the landing page and re-run message-match analysis to confirm the alignment score improved above 60.

remediation_steps: Configure a re-engagement email at 30/60/90 days post-purchase with personalized recommendations.
Implement a loyalty/points program — it reduces friction for the second purchase.
Analyze LTV per cohort — which acquisition channel brings customers who repurchase?
Create a subscription option for consumable products — recurring revenue is the largest lever.
low_repeat_purchase_rate.verification_notes: Re-pull Shopify to confirm repeat purchase rate trend plus cohort analysis.

remediation_steps: List products with zero sales in 30 days — delist or reposition.
Move dead-weight SKUs to a "clearance" category with a real discount to turn inventory.
Analyze whether dead weight is due to price, positioning, or demand — the action varies.
Monthly, purge SKUs with no sales in 90 days — they pollute search and dilute inventory.
dead_weight_products.verification_notes: Re-pull Shopify plus cross-check of sales by SKU in the last 30d.

remediation_steps: Implement a cancellation survey (1-2 questions) to map the real reasons for churn.
Configure dunning automation with 3-5 retries plus an email notifying the customer of failed payment.
Create a retention offer (pause, downgrade, extension) before final cancellation.
Monitor weekly churn rate with an alert when it exceeds 5% monthly.
subscriber_churn_elevated.verification_notes: Re-pull Stripe to verify whether churn rate dropped below 5% in the next 30d period.

remediation_steps: Activate the card updater in Stripe (automatic for expired cards).
Configure smart retry with progressive backoff (Stripe Billing already supports it).
Send the customer an email on the first failure with a link to update their payment method.
Implement a 3-7 day grace period before suspending access after a failure.
failed_payment_rate_high.verification_notes: Re-pull Stripe to verify whether failed_payment_rate dropped below 3% in the next 30d period.

remediation_steps: Review the copy of your policies (refund, privacy, terms) — reading them should reinforce trust, not raise doubts.
Add a subtle CTA in the refund policy: "Any questions? Talk to our team" with a link to support.
If buyers visit a policy and abandon, include post-policy reassurance (modal or banner) suggesting the policy is favorable.
policy_view_then_abandonment.verification_notes: Awaiting session accrual post-adjustment to re-evaluate. Current sessions in window: {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: Detect pages visited by high-intent buyers before abandonment — usually FAQ, comparison, reviews.
Identify the specific objection on those pages and resolve it directly in the main flow.
Test redirecting part of the traffic straight into the main flow without detour — see if conversion lifts.
high_intent_detour_before_abandonment.verification_notes: Awaiting new sessions to re-measure the detour pattern. Current: {current}/{required} sessions.

remediation_steps: Move the support channel to a prominent position in the purchase flow — persistent footer or visible chat.
Proactively offer chat when the buyer spends >60s in checkout without progress.
Measure First Response Time — <5 business minutes on chat reduces friction significantly.
support_discovered_too_late_to_convert.verification_notes: Awaiting post-adjustment traffic to measure whether support discovery has risen. Sessions: {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: The CTA shows in viewport but no one clicks — review copy, contrast, and proximity to the value prop.
A/B test color, size, and copy variants of the CTA — small changes drive big swings.
Make sure the CTA is above the fold on desktop AND mobile — mobile tends to bury the CTA.
cta_visible_but_behaviorally_dead.verification_notes: Awaiting new sessions to measure CTR on the adjusted CTA. {current}/{required} sessions.

remediation_steps: Buyers return to the cart multiple times — the objection is in the data shown there (price? shipping? delivery?).
Expose ALL costs (shipping plus taxes) before checkout — a surprise total is a top cause of abandonment.
Add a "why choose us" recap next to the pay button to reinforce the decision.
purchase_hesitation_with_backtrack.verification_notes: Awaiting post-change sessions to validate the reduction in backtracks. {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: Identify which step retries most before abandonment — usually a rejected card or a ZIP without delivery.
Improve error messages on those steps — explain CAUSE plus NEXT STEP.
Offer alternative paths: rejected card → offer PIX; no-delivery ZIP → offer pickup.
critical_step_retries_before_abandonment.verification_notes: Awaiting post-fix sessions to measure retry rate per step. {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: The first commercial action on mobile (add-to-cart, start checkout) fails more than on desktop — test on real iOS plus Android.
Validate button size (≥44px), viewport, and that the keyboard doesn't cover the active input.
Eliminate modals/overlays that on mobile end up scroll-trapped or without a visible close button.
mobile_fails_first_commercial_action.verification_notes: Awaiting new mobile sessions to validate the fix. {current}/{required} mobile sessions.

remediation_steps: The step has activity (clicks, form fills) but doesn't advance — there's a technical blocker or a silent UX issue.
Review validations: hidden error messages, submit button disabled without clear feedback.
Add analytics on form validation failures to see which field blocks the most.
funnel_step_alive_but_not_advancing.verification_notes: Awaiting sessions to re-measure advance rate per step. {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: The buyer hesitates near the pay button — trust markers aren't where they're needed.
Add seals (SSL, card brands, gateway) VISIBLE in the payment viewport.
Include reassurance microcopy next to the button: "Secure payment via [gateway]".
hesitation_before_conversion_missing_tru.verification_notes: Awaiting sessions post trust markers to measure the reduction in hesitation. {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: The buyer revisits pricing multiple times — an indicator that the value prop isn't clear.
Rewrite each plan in terms of OUTCOME ("save X hours/week"), not features.
Add a visual pros-cons comparison or ROI calculator to help the buyer decide.
pricing_hesitation_unclear_value.verification_notes: Awaiting sessions post pricing rewrite to measure decision time. {current}/{required}.
