remediation_steps: The buyer visits a policy before converting — the concern is on their mind. Address it proactively.
Highlight the favorable refund policy DIRECTLY in checkout (not hidden in the footer).
Use positive language: "30 days to change your mind" instead of "refund policy".
policy_detour_before_conversion.verification_notes: Awaiting sessions to measure whether the detour rate dropped. {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: The CTA shows but engagement is low — the copy doesn't compel action.
Use specific verbs: "Buy now" > "Learn more". Real urgency: "3 left in stock".
Test different variants — small copy changes drive 5-20% swings in CTR.
cta_viewed_not_engaged.verification_notes: Awaiting new sessions to re-measure CTA engagement rate. {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: The buyer abandons on seeing a sensitive field (CPF, card, address) — a trust deficit at that specific field.
Add microcopy explaining WHY the data is needed: "CPF used only for the invoice".
Show a lock icon plus SSL seal next to the sensitive field to reinforce security.
sensitive_input_abandonment.verification_notes: Awaiting sessions to re-measure abandonment rate on those fields. {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: The form has >8 required fields before conversion — each extra field cuts completion by ~5%.
Eliminate optional fields that can be requested later (e.g., NPS, profile survey).
Use aggressive auto-fill: ViaCEP for address, masks for CPF/phone.
form_excessive_fields_before_conversion.verification_notes: Awaiting sessions to re-measure form completion rate. {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: The form returns an error and the buyer retries multiple times — validation isn't clear or UX freezes.
Validate in real time (inline) instead of only on submit — the buyer knows immediately what's wrong.
When submit fails, preserve ALL the data already filled — don't force retyping.
form_submission_retry_friction.verification_notes: Awaiting sessions to re-measure retry rate post fix. {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: The buyer oscillates between surfaces (home ↔ product ↔ cart) before abandoning — decision paralysis.
Reduce alternate paths in the commercial flow — one clear path from product to purchase.
Add direct comparison inside the product page to reduce the need to go back to the category.
surface_oscillation_before_dropoff.verification_notes: Awaiting new sessions to measure the oscillation pattern. {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: The final step (payment submit) retries frequently — the card is declined or anti-fraud blocks.
Improve the post-decline message: explain the likely cause plus suggest an action (new card, PIX).
Add an automatic fallback to other methods when the primary fails.
conversion_final_step_retry.verification_notes: Awaiting sessions to re-measure retry rate on the final step. {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: The CTA appears after full render — scripts delay interactivity.
Pre-render critical CTAs in the initial HTML, without depending on JS.
Measure Time To Interactive specifically for the conversion button — it should be <2s.
cta_late_availability_delays_action.verification_notes: Awaiting post-fix sessions to measure the timing of the first click on the CTA. {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: The buyer abandons checkout without leaving any signal (didn't fill anything, left silently).
Add an exit-intent modal asking "what was missing?" with a free-text field — captures objections.
Configure a recovery email for those who started checkout but didn't finish, with a direct link back to the cart.
checkout_abandon_no_feedback.verification_notes: Awaiting new sessions to measure the captured-feedback rate. {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: Sensitive fields cause an outright drop — perceived risk is stronger than perceived benefit.
Add trust signals CONTIGUOUS to the field: SSL seal, usage explanation, privacy policy link.
Test reordering: ask for sensitive data AFTER account creation or add-to-cart — it drops less.
sensitive_input_perceived_risk_dropoff.verification_notes: Awaiting sessions to measure the drop rate post adjustments. {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: The first session stalls before hitting the value milestone — reduce friction in the first 60s.
Define the "aha moment" of first use and optimize the direct path to it.
Offer an optional guided tour that demonstrates value in <2min.
first_session_milestone_stall.verification_notes: Awaiting new sessions to measure milestone completion rate. {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: New visitors hit a trust barrier — surface social proof plus credentials more prominently.
Customer logos, testimonials with photos, awards visible on the home above the fold.
For a new product, include an explanatory "how it works" plus an explicit guarantee to reduce perceived risk.
first_session_trust_barrier.verification_notes: Awaiting new sessions to re-measure new-visitor bounce rate. {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: The CTA appears too late in the first session — the visitor leaves before seeing the offer.
Show the primary CTA visible on first paint, without depending on scroll.
Configure an exit-intent modal to capture visitors leaving without taking action.
first_session_cta_timing_gap.verification_notes: Awaiting new sessions to measure CTA exposure timing. {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: Low-value actions (scroll, blog reads) dominate over high-value actions (add-to-cart, signup).
Review CTAs on traffic pages — many lead to content instead of conversion.
Add a conversion CTA on popular content pages (blog, help).
low_value_action_dominates.verification_notes: Awaiting sessions to re-measure the ratio of high-value vs low-value actions. {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: High-value actions (purchase, upgrade) don't get enough exposure — position them on high-traffic surfaces.
The home should have a primary CTA pointing to the highest commercial-value action.
Avoid hiding premium features behind multiple clicks — expose them directly with contextual paywall.
high_value_action_underexposed.verification_notes: Awaiting sessions to measure exposure rate of high-value actions. {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: Pages with traffic but zero conversion drain ad budget and split analytics.
Audit the top 10 traffic pages by conversion rate — prioritize fixes on those with 0% CR.
If the page structurally doesn't convert (e.g., a generic blog post), reduce paid spend pointed at it.
dead_weight_surface_traffic.verification_notes: Awaiting sessions to re-measure conversion rate per surface. {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: Paid traffic hits more friction than organic — ad landing pages need to be dedicated.
Build campaign-specific landing pages, without navigation that distracts from the CTA.
Validate message match: ad promises X, the landing delivers X (not the generic home).
paid_traffic_friction_elevated.verification_notes: Awaiting paid-traffic sessions to re-measure friction. {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: Paid traffic has lower trust than organic — reinforce credentials on ad landings.
Social proof (reviews, customer count, media) visible in the first viewport.
A strong, explicit guarantee ("30 days to return, no questions") reduces perceived risk.
paid_traffic_trust_gap.verification_notes: Awaiting paid sessions to measure trust-related bounce rate. {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: Paid plus mobile equals compound waste. Mobile friction plus cold traffic buy 2x less.
Separate mobile vs desktop campaigns and optimize landings independently for each channel.
The mobile landing should have ≤3 elements above the fold: headline plus benefit plus CTA.
paid_mobile_compounding_waste.verification_notes: Awaiting paid-mobile sessions to re-measure conversion rate. {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: Mobile conversion rate is significantly below desktop — mobile UX needs dedicated attention.
Audit mobile form completion: tiny fields, keyboard covering input, submit out of reach.
Simplify the flow on mobile: a single column, numbered steps, save progress.
mobile_conversion_gap.verification_notes: Awaiting mobile sessions to re-measure the conversion gap. {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: Forms on mobile have a higher abandonment rate — fields plus keyboard cause friction.
Use the correct input types (tel, email, number) to trigger optimized keyboards.
Auto-advance between fields when possible (e.g., ZIP auto-fills city/state).
mobile_form_friction_elevated.verification_notes: Awaiting mobile sessions with form interaction. {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: The mobile CTA takes too long to become interactive — heavy scripts block the main thread.
Prioritize TTI (Time To Interactive) on mobile — target <3s on simulated 4G.
Reduce the initial JS bundle, lazy-load the rest.
mobile_cta_timing_degraded.verification_notes: Awaiting mobile sessions post-optimization to measure real timing. {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: Each funnel step has a conversion cost — count the current steps and eliminate redundant ones.
Consolidate "step 1: add address" plus "step 2: confirm address" into a single step.
Test one-page checkout vs multi-step — for simple carts, one-page converts better.
funnel_step_friction_cost.verification_notes: Awaiting post-consolidation sessions to measure step completion. {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: The buyer oscillates between options (plans, variants, payment methods) — decision fatigue.
Highlight a recommended default to reduce cognitive load — "most popular choice" works well.
Limit options — 3 plans is the sweet spot for SaaS, 2-3 variants per product for e-commerce.
oscillation_decision_cost.verification_notes: Awaiting sessions to measure the oscillation pattern. {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: Entry into checkout already has friction — hidden button, interrupting modal, forced login.
Make a "Checkout" CTA visible in the cart and on every product page.
Guest checkout as default — option to create account post-purchase.
checkout_entry_friction.verification_notes: Awaiting sessions to measure entry rate into checkout. {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: A generalized trust deficit is dragging overall conversion down — it's not a point fix, it's systemic reinforcement.
Add trust markers across ALL commercial surfaces: home, product, cart, checkout, confirmation.
Concrete social proof (numbers, names, logos) reduces trust deficit more than generic badges.
trust_deficit_conversion_drag.verification_notes: Awaiting sessions post trust reinforcement to measure conversion uplift. {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: Buyers seek reassurance (FAQ, reviews, contact) before deciding — meet that need proactively.
Place relevant FAQ directly on product pages — don't force the buyer to navigate to find it.
Add "units sold" or "average rating" badges as explicit social proof.
reassurance_seeking_elevated.verification_notes: Awaiting sessions to measure reassurance visits pre-conversion. {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: A trust gap specific to sensitive fields — perceived risk overrides the site's general trust.
Add microcopy explaining the collection plus a privacy policy link next to the field.
Use lock icons plus SSL seal CONTIGUOUS to the field — trust must be perceived where the risk is felt.
sensitive_input_trust_gap.verification_notes: Awaiting sessions to measure completion rate on sensitive fields. {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: The buyer's average path to conversion is too long — every extra page dilutes intent.
Identify shortcuts: featured product on home, direct CTA without an intermediate category, quick-add inside the category.
Minimize pageviews required to purchase — ideally <5 pageviews from entry point to payment.
path_length_exceeds_efficient.verification_notes: Awaiting sessions to measure average path length post optimization. {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: Some surface is absorbing intent without converting — blog post, generic FAQ, rich category page.
Identify the intent absorbers via analytics and add relevant conversion CTAs on those pages.
Or: reduce exposure of those pages in main navigation if they are not convertible.
intent_absorber_detected.verification_notes: Awaiting sessions after adding CTAs on the absorbers. {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: Intent decay is too fast — the buyer cools off if the session exceeds X minutes without conversion.
Use real urgency/scarcity (low stock, promo with timer) to accelerate the decision.
If the buyer returns in a new session, re-engage with an email reminding them where they left off plus a small incentive.
intent_decay_time_excessive.verification_notes: Awaiting new sessions to measure the intent decay curve. {current}/{required}.

remediation_steps: Configure a generic error handler in production — log details server-side, return a friendly message to the client.
Remove the Server header with version (Apache/nginx): use ServerTokens Prod or server_tokens off.
Audit 4xx/5xx responses — none should contain stack trace, internal path, or framework version.
Configure the framework for production mode (DEBUG=false, RAILS_ENV=production, NODE_ENV=production).
information_disclosure.verification_notes: We will request error URLs and check the Server header in responses to confirm removal of sensitive info.

remediation_steps: Add the integrity= attribute (SRI hash) to every external <script> on checkout and commercial pages.
Generate SHA-384 or SHA-512 hash of the versioned script: openssl dgst -sha384 -binary file | base64.
Configure CSP require-sri-for to automatically block scripts without integrity.
Monitor the CDN provider for compromise alerts — swap the hash when the version changes.
script_supply_chain_risk.verification_notes: We will re-fetch commercial pages and verify presence of the integrity attribute on external scripts.

remediation_steps: Fix password fields to type="password" — never use type="text" for passwords.
Ensure the login/signup form action uses HTTPS (never HTTP).
Add autocomplete="current-password" on login inputs and "new-password" on signup inputs.
Implement HSTS to ensure that even HTTP links are redirected to HTTPS before submit.
auth_surface_insecure.verification_notes: We will re-fetch login pages and verify the password field type and the form action protocol.

remediation_steps: Add a clear comparison between tiers: feature-by-feature table with explicit checkmarks.
Highlight one recommended plan (badge "Most Popular" or "Best Value") to reduce decision paralysis.
Include a 1-line description per tier explaining who it is for (freelancer, startup, enterprise).
If the pricing model is not determinable, simplify — fewer options convert better.
pricing_offer_unclear.verification_notes: We will re-analyze the pricing page with an LLM to validate tier clarity and presence of a recommendation.

remediation_steps: Align the title and H1 with the actual purpose of the page — the pricing page should contain "plans/prices".
If content has migrated, reclassify the page and update navigation plus sitemap.
Revise the meta description to reflect current content — misalignment hurts organic CTR.
Audit funnel analytics — if buyers arrive expecting X and find Y, bounce rate rises.
page_purpose_mismatch.verification_notes: We will re-classify the page and compare H1/title keywords against the detected page type.

remediation_steps: Audit JSON-LD vs. visible content: the price in the schema must be identical to the price on the page.
Use Google Rich Results Test to validate that schema data reflects reality.
Automate JSON-LD generation from the CMS/database — avoid hardcoded values that go stale.
Monitor price/name changes — update the schema together when the product changes.
structured_data_mismatch.verification_notes: We will extract JSON-LD and compare claims (price, name, rating) against the page's visible content.

remediation_steps: Monitor the return rate after payment handoff — identify which gateway has the biggest drop.
Replace external redirect with embedded checkout (Stripe Elements, PayPal Smart Buttons) when possible.
If the redirect is unavoidable, add the store logo and a security badge on the gateway page.
Implement callback/webhook to detect sessions that never return and trigger email recovery.
Test the flow on mobile and desktop — gateways with pop-ups may be blocked on Safari/iOS.
payment_handoff_dropoff.verification_notes: We will run the full checkout in a headless browser, follow the handoff to the gateway, and validate whether the session returns to the store's domain with confirmation.

remediation_steps: Map the first meaningful action and measure how many trial users complete it.
Reduce onboarding to a maximum of 3 steps before the first win.
Eliminate optional fields at signup — ask only for email and password, complete the profile later.
Add empty states with clear CTAs that guide to the first meaningful action.
Implement a progress indicator showing how much remains to activate the product.
saas_activation_gap_heuristic.verification_notes: We will simulate signup plus first action in a headless browser and measure whether the flow completes without error or abandonment.

remediation_steps: Identify the page pair with the highest oscillation and analyze what is missing on each to resolve the doubt.
Add an inline comparison or summary on the source page (e.g., pricing table on the features page).
Implement a breadcrumb or progress indicator showing where the user is in the decision flow.
Test a sticky CTA or sidebar with a summary of options to reduce the need to navigate back.
oscillation_clustering.verification_notes: We will monitor the page pair after the change and verify whether the oscillation rate dropped below the threshold.

remediation_steps: Prioritize fixes by weight: payment failures (x3) > measurement failures (x2) > trust/third-party (x1).
Configure automatic retry with exponential backoff for payment and analytics scripts.
Implement a visual fallback when trust badges fail (e.g., static text instead of dynamic widget).
Monitor uptime of critical providers with sub-5-min alerting and automatic failover.
Reduce third-party dependencies in checkout — inline what you can.
network_error_weighted.verification_notes: We will re-execute network capture on commercial pages and validate whether the weighted score dropped below 5.

remediation_steps: Audit mobile vs. desktop side by side — list every trust signal visible only on desktop.
Ensure security badges, reviews, and guarantees are visible above-the-fold on mobile.
Avoid trust badges in carousels or tabs — on mobile they must be inline and visible without scrolling.
Test review widget loading on throttled 3G — replace with a static version if they fail.
Add visible security microdata in the mobile header (SSL lock icon plus "Secure Purchase" text).
mobile_trust_gap.verification_notes: We will verify mobile vs. desktop in headless and confirm that trust signals are present and loading within 3s on mobile.

remediation_steps: Do not treat each symptom in isolation — the composite pattern indicates that the entire decision flow needs restructuring.
Simplify the decision page: reduce options, eliminate dead clicks, and centralize price plus value information.
Add progressive reassurance throughout the funnel instead of concentrating it at checkout.
Implement inline form validation with clear messages — eliminate retries caused by UX errors.
Monitor the cascade score after each change to validate that the pattern is dissolving.
behavioral_micro_pattern_cascade.verification_notes: We will monitor the 5 behavioral indicators together and verify whether fewer than 2 remain above the threshold.

remediation_steps: Create a measurements table with bust, waist, hip, and length for each product category.
Add a visible "Size Guide" button next to the size selector on the product page.
Include measuring instructions with a tape measure inside the guide modal.
Consider adding a size recommendation widget based on height and weight.
size_guide_missing.verification_notes: We will re-crawl product pages and verify the presence of a size guide linked next to the size selector.

remediation_steps: Photograph each product from at least 5 angles: front, side, back, detail, and worn/in use.
Add hover- or touch-enabled zoom on each image on the product page.
Include at least one lifestyle photo showing the product in real-use context.
product_images_insufficient.verification_notes: We will re-crawl product pages and count the number of images per product — minimum 4 to pass.

remediation_steps: Display stock level when fewer than 10 units remain (e.g., "Last 3 units").
Add a recent buyers counter (e.g., "12 people bought today").
Implement "Limited time offer" badges when active promotions are running.
no_urgency_indicators.verification_notes: We will re-crawl product pages and verify the presence of urgency or scarcity indicators.

remediation_steps: Add a "Complete the Look" or "Buy Together" section on the product page below the description.
Configure automatic recommendations based on frequently-bought-together products.
Add cross-sell suggestions also on the cart page before checkout.
cross_sell_absent.verification_notes: We will re-crawl product and cart pages and verify the presence of related or complementary product sections.

remediation_steps: Add a return policy summary directly on the product page, below the purchase button.
Include return window, accepted conditions, and who pays return shipping.
Link to the full policy but keep the essential points inline and visible.
return_policy_not_on_product.verification_notes: We will re-crawl product pages and verify the presence of return information near the purchase CTA.

remediation_steps: Implement a free trial of 7-14 days with no credit card required.
Display the trial CTA prominently on the homepage and pricing page.
Create an onboarding flow that delivers value in the first trial session.
Add an automated follow-up email showing what the user can try.
no_free_trial_offered.verification_notes: We will re-crawl the homepage and pricing page and verify the presence of a free trial or freemium CTA.

remediation_steps: Create an "Integrations" section on the homepage with logos of your audience's most popular tools.
Add a dedicated /integrations page with descriptions and status of each integration.
Mention key integrations on the pricing page next to the plans that include them.
integration_ecosystem_invisible.verification_notes: We will re-crawl the homepage and pricing page and verify the presence of an integrations section with at least 3 logos.

remediation_steps: Create a /changelog or /updates page with the latest product changes.
Publish at least one update per month with date, title, and description.
Link the changelog in the footer and consider a "Recently updated" badge on the homepage.
changelog_stale_or_missing.verification_notes: We will verify whether an accessible changelog page exists and whether the latest entry is less than 60 days old.

remediation_steps: Add a "Monthly / Annual" toggle on the pricing page with the percentage discount visible.
Display annual savings in absolute value (e.g., "Save $240/year").
Pre-select the annual plan as the default on the pricing page.
annual_discount_not_highlighted.verification_notes: We will re-crawl the pricing page and verify whether an annual option with visible, calculated discount exists.

remediation_steps: Add at least 3 product-in-use screenshots above the fold on the homepage.
Include in-context screenshots showing real flows (not generic mockups).
Consider adding a 60-second demo video showing the product in action.
no_product_screenshot_visible.verification_notes: We will re-crawl the homepage and landing pages and verify the presence of product images (screenshots or video).

remediation_steps: Remove the login/signup gate for menu viewing — allow full public access.
Move the signup requirement to the order moment, not browsing.
Ensure the full menu is indexable by search engines (HTML, not just PDF).
menu_requires_signup.verification_notes: We will access the menu URL without authentication and verify whether the content is visible without login.

remediation_steps: Professionally photograph at least the 10 best-selling dishes.
Add the photo next to each item on the digital menu.
Use real images (no stock photos) with good lighting and faithful presentation of the served dish.
no_food_photos.verification_notes: We will re-crawl the menu and verify whether at least 50% of items have an associated image.

remediation_steps: Add an interactive map or list of served neighborhoods/ZIP codes on the main page.
Allow the customer to check coverage by ZIP code before building the order.
Display the delivery area permanently in the header or banner of the site.
delivery_area_unclear.verification_notes: We will verify whether coverage area information is visible on the main page or menu.

remediation_steps: Display the estimated delivery time on the menu page (e.g., "30-45 min").
Update the estimate in real time based on demand and distance.
Show the time also in the cart before order confirmation.
delivery_time_not_shown.verification_notes: We will re-crawl menu and cart and verify the presence of a delivery time estimate.

remediation_steps: Add allergen icons (gluten, lactose, peanut, etc.) on each menu item.
Include an explanatory legend of the icons at the top or footer of the menu.
Add a filter by dietary restriction to ease navigation.
allergen_info_missing.verification_notes: We will re-crawl the menu and verify the presence of allergen or dietary restriction information on items.

remediation_steps: List the full composition (INCI) on each product page.
Highlight key ingredients and their benefits in accessible language.
Add an accordion or "Ingredients" tab visible without excessive scrolling.
ingredients_not_listed.verification_notes: We will re-crawl product pages and verify the presence of an ingredient list or composition.

remediation_steps: Obtain and display a dermatological or relevant health professional endorsement.
Publish clinical test or study results with sample size and efficacy percentages.
Add a "Dermatologically Tested" badge or equivalent with reference to the study.
no_clinical_endorsement.verification_notes: We will re-crawl product pages and verify the presence of clinical badges, mentions of health professionals, or test results.

remediation_steps: Add step-by-step usage instructions on the product page (frequency, quantity, application method).
Include tips on when and how to use for best results.
Consider a short video demonstrating the correct application of the product.
usage_instructions_absent.verification_notes: We will re-crawl product pages and verify the presence of a usage or instructions section.

remediation_steps: Implement a "Subscribe and Save" option with a 10-15% discount on the product page.
Offer flexible frequencies (monthly, bi-monthly, quarterly) based on average consumption.
Highlight accumulated savings and the convenience of not having to remember to reorder.
subscription_not_offered.verification_notes: We will re-crawl product pages and verify the presence of a subscription or recurring purchase option.

remediation_steps: Add before/after photos with the consent of real customers.
Publish test results with percentages (e.g., "89% noticed improvement in 4 weeks").
Include reviews with customer photos showing results on the product page.
no_results_evidence.verification_notes: We will re-crawl product pages and verify the presence of visual evidence of results or efficacy data.

remediation_steps: Publish the full course syllabus with modules, lessons, and the duration of each section.
Add the syllabus on the course sales page in expandable (accordion) format.
Include clear learning objectives for each module.
curriculum_not_visible.verification_notes: We will re-crawl the course page and verify the presence of a detailed syllabus or curriculum.

remediation_steps: Add a complete instructor bio with education, experience, and relevant results.
Include a professional photo and links to LinkedIn or portfolio.
Highlight specific achievements that prove authority on the course topic.
instructor_credentials_missing.verification_notes: We will re-crawl the course page and verify the presence of an instructor section with credentials.

remediation_steps: Implement automatic generation of a completion certificate upon finishing the course.
Display an example of the certificate on the course sales page.
Mention the certificate in the hero or benefits of the sales page.
completion_certificate_absent.verification_notes: We will re-crawl the course page and verify mention of a completion certificate.

remediation_steps: Display the total course workload prominently on the sales page.
Add an estimated duration per module/lesson on the syllabus.
Include a study pace suggestion (e.g., "2h per week for 8 weeks").
time_commitment_unclear.verification_notes: We will re-crawl the course page and verify the presence of workload or duration information.

remediation_steps: Release 1-2 course lessons for free as a preview on the sales page.
Add a 2-3 minute trailer video showing teaching style and quality.
Allow access to the first module without signup to lower the evaluation barrier.
no_sample_content.verification_notes: We will re-crawl the course page and verify the presence of free sample content (video or open lesson).

remediation_steps: Publish at least 3 case studies with problem, solution, and measurable results (%, $, time).
Include client logo and name (with permission) for credibility.
Link relevant case studies on the homepage and on related service pages.
Format each case study with the result highlighted (headline) before the context.
no_case_study_with_metrics.verification_notes: We will re-crawl the site and verify the presence of case studies with numerical metrics on commercial pages.

remediation_steps: Create a "How It Works" section with 3-5 visual steps of your process.
Explain each step with concrete deliverables and expected timelines.
Add this section on the homepage and on each service's page.
methodology_not_explained.verification_notes: We will re-crawl service pages and the homepage and verify the presence of a methodology or process section.

remediation_steps: Add enterprise customer logos on the homepage (with permission).
Display relevant certifications (ISO, SOC2, GDPR compliance) in the footer or on a security page.
Create a /security or /compliance page with details of your practices.
Add the number of customers served and processed volume as social proof.
enterprise_signals_missing.verification_notes: We will re-crawl the homepage and footer and verify the presence of customer logos, certifications, or enterprise signals.

remediation_steps: Reduce the contact form to a maximum of 4-5 essential fields (name, email, company, message).
Move qualification fields (budget, segment, role) to after the first contact.
Add the option to book a meeting directly (Calendly/Cal.com) as an alternative to the form.
contact_form_excessive_fields.verification_notes: We will re-crawl the contact page and count the number of required fields — maximum 5 to pass.

remediation_steps: Add a response time commitment on the form (e.g., "We respond within 24 business hours").
Configure an autoresponder confirming receipt and informing the return time.
Display the response SLA next to the form's submit button.
response_time_not_promised.verification_notes: We will re-crawl the contact page and verify the presence of a response time commitment near the form.

remediation_steps: Rewrite each page's meta description to reflect exactly what the visitor finds in the H1 and the first paragraph.
Ensure the main benefit mentioned in the meta description appears above the fold on the page.
Revise meta descriptions of the 10 pages with the most organic traffic — start with the commercial ones.
Configure a Search Console alert to monitor bounce rate per page and identify new divergences.
meta_promise_content_mismatch.verification_notes: We will re-crawl the affected pages, extract meta description and H1/first paragraph, and compare semantic alignment between the two.

remediation_steps: Audit every page that mentions price — product, pricing, landing pages, FAQ — and unify the values.
Centralize the price table in a reusable component to avoid future divergences.
Remove hardcoded prices from free text — the reference must always come from a single source.
Configure an automated test that compares displayed prices between commercial pages.
pricing_terms_contradictory.verification_notes: We will re-crawl every page mentioning price and verify consistency between the values found.

remediation_steps: Remove all urgency language without a real expiration date (permanent "last spots," "limited offer" that never ends).
If the offer is genuinely limited, tie it to a real countdown with an end date.
Replace artificial urgency with real social proof ("127 people bought this week").
If you use scarcity, connect it to real stock — "3 units left" with an inventory backend.
urgency_claim_unverifiable.verification_notes: We will re-crawl the pages and verify whether the urgency language was removed or tied to a real temporal mechanism (countdown, expiration date).

remediation_steps: Choose ONE main value proposition and position it as H1 plus subtitle above the fold.
Move secondary propositions to sections below the fold, each with its specific CTA.
Create a clear visual hierarchy: the main proposition should have 3x the prominence of the secondary ones.
Test with 5 people: ask them to describe what you do in one sentence after 5 seconds on the homepage. If they can't, simplify.
value_prop_diluted_by_navigation.verification_notes: We will re-crawl the homepage and count H2s/value propositions above the pricing link. Passes if there are ≤3 propositions with clear hierarchy.

remediation_steps: Remove or rewrite every sentence in the checkout that uses negative framing ("not guaranteed," "non-refundable").
Replace with positive copy: "Delivery guaranteed within X days" instead of "Delivery time not guaranteed."
Add reassurance micro-copy next to each payment field ("Your data is protected," "Satisfaction guaranteed").
Display security badges plus summarized refund policy at checkout, not just in the footer.
checkout_copy_creates_anxiety.verification_notes: We will re-crawl the checkout and search for anxiety-inducing language patterns. Passes when no negative sentence about payment/delivery is found.

remediation_steps: Add the 5 most common purchase objections to the FAQ: price, refund, guarantee, deadline, and "will it work for me?".
Place purchase questions at the top of the FAQ — before the technical questions.
Each answer should remove the objection and end with a link to the CTA ("Ready to start? Begin here").
Review the FAQ monthly with the most frequent support tickets.
faq_answers_wrong_questions.verification_notes: We will re-crawl the FAQ and verify the presence of questions about price, refund, guarantee, and deadline. Passes when at least 3 of these objections are covered.

remediation_steps: Replace generic testimonials with testimonials containing specific results ("Grew 32% in 60 days").
Vary the length and format of testimonials — some short, some long, some on video.
Add a real photo, full name, and company/role next to each testimonial.
Include at least one testimonial with a concrete result figure per social proof section.
If possible, link to LinkedIn profile or verified review (Google, G2, Trustpilot).
testimonials_feel_fabricated.verification_notes: We will re-crawl pages with testimonials and assess length diversity, presence of concrete results, and verifiable identification of the authors.

remediation_steps: Move the checkout to the same domain as the store or use an embedded checkout that keeps your site's URL.
If the domain change is unavoidable, add the logo, brand colors, and a security badge on the external page.
Add copy on the checkout button explaining where the buyer will be taken ("Secure payment via [gateway]").
Ensure the SSL certificate is valid and the green padlock is visible on both URLs.
brand_trust_cliff_at_payment.verification_notes: We will run the checkout flow in a headless browser, follow redirects, and verify whether the brand remains visible on the payment page.

remediation_steps: Revise meta descriptions and OG tags so they reflect exactly the content above the fold.
Ensure the page's H1 contains the same keywords promised in the meta description.
Create landing page variations for each meta tag/campaign with consistent messaging.
Test the Google preview (Search Console) and social networks (Facebook Debugger) to confirm alignment.
ad_landing_experience_disconnect.verification_notes: We will re-crawl the pages and compare keywords from the meta description/OG to the H1 and above-fold content to confirm alignment.

remediation_steps: Add the correct autocomplete attribute on each field (name, email, tel, address, cc-number).
Use input type="email" for email, type="tel" for phone, and inputmode="numeric" for numbers.
Reduce fields to the minimum necessary — eliminate optional fields or move them to post-purchase.
Increase field size to a minimum tap target of 44px on mobile.
checkout_form_mobile_hostile.verification_notes: We will re-crawl the checkout form and verify the presence of autocomplete, specialized input types, and field count.

remediation_steps: Reduce to a maximum of 3 visible plans — hide the 4th behind "see all plans".
Highlight one plan as "Most Popular" or "Recommended" with a visual badge and distinct border.
Simplify the feature table — show only the 5-7 most relevant differences.
Add a clear CTA on each plan indicating the immediate next step.
pricing_page_complexity_paralysis.verification_notes: We will re-crawl the pricing page and verify whether there is a recommended plan indication and whether the plan/feature count is within the ideal range.

remediation_steps: Remove SLA promises you cannot keep — replace them with realistic expectations ("We respond within 24 business hours").
Install a functional chat widget if you promise "live chat" or "real time".
Configure an autoresponder on the contact form confirming receipt and informing the real timeframe.
If you promise 24/7, ensure real coverage or replace with honest business hours.
support_promise_impossible_to_fulfill.verification_notes: We will verify whether the chat widget loads and responds, whether SLA promises were updated, and whether the form's autoresponder is active.

remediation_steps: Add at least 2 trust badges (SSL, reviews, guarantee) on the checkout and product pages.
Replicate the most relevant testimonials/reviews on the checkout page near the purchase button.
Include a satisfaction guarantee or return badge visible on all commercial pages.
Standardize a trust footer with badges and policies on ALL pages of the funnel.
trust_journey_inconsistency.verification_notes: We will re-crawl the homepage, checkout, and product pages to verify the consistent presence of trust elements at each tier.

remediation_steps: Ensure the HTML lang attribute is consistent across all pages of the same funnel.
Implement automatic language detection per session — once chosen, keep it across the whole flow.
Fully translate the checkout and product pages — do not mix languages on the same page.
Add a visible language selector in the header with a flag so the user controls the choice.
multilingual_conversion_leak.verification_notes: We will navigate the funnel in a headless browser and verify whether the lang attribute and the content language remain consistent between pages.

remediation_steps: Restrict access to the staging/dev subdomain with basic authentication (HTTP Basic Auth) or VPN.
Configure firewall rules or Cloudflare Access to allow only internal IPs.
Remove real customer data from the staging environment — use synthetic data.
Add the subdomain to robots.txt with Disallow: / to prevent indexing.
Configure monitoring to alert if the environment becomes public again.
staging_environment_publicly_accessible.verification_notes: We will re-probe the staging subdomain and verify whether it returns 401/403 or is publicly inaccessible.

remediation_steps: Restrict access to the admin panel with VPN, IP whitelist, or Cloudflare Access.
Implement mandatory two-factor authentication (2FA/MFA) for all admin access.
Move the panel to an unguessable subdomain or an internal non-public path.
Configure aggressive rate limiting (max 5 attempts/minute) on login routes.
Monitor access attempts and configure alerts for brute-force attempts.
admin_panel_exposed_to_internet.verification_notes: We will re-probe the admin subdomain and verify whether it returns 401/403 or redirects to a VPN/protected login.

remediation_steps: Create a shared design system (header, footer, colors, logo) across all subdomains.
Implement a consistent global header via iframe, web component, or shared CDN.
Ensure navigation between subdomains preserves session and continuous visual identity.
Document the brand guidelines for teams maintaining different subdomains.
subdomain_brand_visual_fragmentation.verification_notes: We will visit each active subdomain in a headless browser and compare the presence of brand elements (logo, colors, header) across the surfaces.

remediation_steps: Add a visible "Sign in" or "My Account" link in the main site header pointing to the app.
Include the app link in the footer of every commercial page.
Create a /login or /access page on the main domain that redirects to the app.
Add the app subdomain prominently to the mobile menu.
app_subdomain_disconnected_from_site.verification_notes: We will re-crawl the main site and check for the presence of a link to the app subdomain in the header, footer, or navigation.

remediation_steps: Add a floating WhatsApp button or link in the header/footer of commercial pages.
Include a WhatsApp CTA on the product page and pricing page.
Configure a direct deep link (wa.me/number) instead of relying on the subdomain.
Test that the WhatsApp link works on desktop and mobile.
whatsapp_channel_disconnected.verification_notes: We will re-crawl the commercial pages and check for the presence of a WhatsApp link (wa.me, whatsapp.com, or subdomain) in the visible content.

remediation_steps: Consolidate checkout on the same domain using the gateway's embedded checkout (Stripe Elements, MercadoPago Bricks).
If a domain change is unavoidable, add the store logo and a security badge on the payment page.
Implement a loading state between domains explaining "You are being directed to secure payment".
Ensure HTTPS and a valid certificate on the payment subdomain — visible to the buyer.
Add a visual breadcrumb showing progress (Cart → Details → Payment → Confirmation).
multiple_payment_subdomains_fragmenting_.verification_notes: We will simulate the checkout flow in a headless browser, follow redirects, and verify whether payment stays on the main domain or if there are trust signals on the external domain.

remediation_steps: Add position:sticky or position:fixed to the submit button in forms with more than 5 fields.
Reduce the number of required fields — move optional fields to after the first submission.
Test the form in a 375px-wide viewport and verify that the button stays always visible.
Consider splitting the form into steps (wizard) with a visible next button on each step.
form_submit_unreachable_mobile.verification_notes: We will open the form in a mobile viewport (375×667) and check whether the submit button is visible without scrolling or fixed on the screen.

remediation_steps: Move trust badges (SSL, gateway, guarantee) close to the payment button — not in the footer.
Add a "Secure Purchase" or "Protected Payment" badge immediately above or beside the card form.
Ensure the badges are visible without scrolling at the most common checkout resolution (mobile and desktop).
Test with a heatmap to confirm that the trust area receives visual attention before the purchase click.
trust_badges_invisible_at_checkout.verification_notes: We will open the checkout and check whether trust elements (seal, badge, guarantee) are visible above the fold without scrolling.

remediation_steps: Add a conversion CTA (link to /pricing, /product, or /checkout) on every blog and about page.
Insert a sidebar banner or fixed bottom bar with a commercial link on content pages.
Ensure the main navigation includes a direct link to the pricing or product page on every page.
Review most-visited pages via Google Analytics and prioritize those without a conversion CTA.
navigation_traps_commercial_flow.verification_notes: We will re-crawl the content pages and check for links to commercial URLs (/pricing, /checkout, /product).

remediation_steps: Remove lazy-load from the first 2-3 testimonials — load them inline in the static HTML.
Move at least one social proof block above the fold on the homepage and product page.
If using an external widget (Trustpilot, Google Reviews), configure server-side rendering or static caching.
Add a review count (e.g., "4.8/5 — 127 reviews") as static text, without JS dependency.
social_proof_loads_too_late.verification_notes: We will check whether social proof elements are present in the initial static HTML (without lazy-load) and above the fold.

remediation_steps: Configure the consent banner so it does not overlap the primary CTA — position it at the footer or as a thin bar at the top.
Reduce the cookie banner height to a maximum of 80px on mobile.
Implement automatic dismiss after interaction with any page element (not just the "Accept" button).
A/B test the banner placement — bottom-bar vs overlay — and measure impact on CTA click-through rate.
consent_banner_obscures_first_action.verification_notes: We will load the page in a headless browser and check whether the primary CTA is clickable without interacting with the consent banner first.

remediation_steps: Render at least the base price of each plan in the static HTML of the pricing page (server-side).
If the price depends on configuration, show a "from $X/month" visible without JavaScript.
Add price schema markup (PriceSpecification) so search engines and AI also see the price.
Test the page with JavaScript disabled — if no price appears, the issue is confirmed.
price_hidden_behind_interaction.verification_notes: We will perform an HTTP GET request to the pricing page and search for price patterns ($, €, /month) in the returned HTML — without executing JavaScript.

remediation_steps: Rewrite the homepage main headline to answer "what the buyer gains" instead of "what you do".
Add a subheadline with a concrete outcome (number, timeline, tangible benefit) right below the headline.
A/B test the new headline against the current one and measure scroll rate and CTA click-through.
hero_outcome_absent.verification_notes: We will fetch the homepage HTML and check whether the h1 or hero section contains buyer-outcome-oriented language.

remediation_steps: Reduce the number of messages above the fold to a maximum of 1 value proposition + 1 CTA.
Remove sliders, carousels, and competing banners from the first screen.
Prioritize visual hierarchy: headline > subheadline > CTA — nothing else should compete for attention.
cognitive_load_first_screen.verification_notes: We will analyze the HTML above the fold and count how many text elements and CTAs compete for attention in the first viewport.

remediation_steps: Move the primary CTA above the fold — the visitor should see the first action without scrolling.
Add a sticky CTA in the header or a bottom bar for mobile.
Reduce the content between the hero and the first action button to a maximum of 2 blocks.
primary_cta_delayed.verification_notes: We will check whether a CTA element (action link or button) exists within the first 600px of the homepage HTML.

remediation_steps: Replace generic adjectives (best, innovative, leader) with concrete numbers and outcomes.
Include at least 1 quantitative data point in the hero (e.g., "used by 2,000 companies", "delivery in 24h").
Review all commercial copy and replace vendor language with buyer language.
specificity_deficit.verification_notes: We will re-analyze the homepage copy looking for the presence of concrete numbers and the absence of generic adjectives in the hero.

remediation_steps: Publish at least 3 testimonials from real customers with name, company, and measurable outcome.
Create a dedicated cases/results page with before-and-after metrics.
Add customer logos or "Used by X companies" on the homepage and product pages.
proof_of_work_missing.verification_notes: We will crawl the homepage and commercial pages looking for testimonial patterns (name + outcome) and customer logos.

remediation_steps: Add a CTA returning to the purchase journey on every support, FAQ, and help page.
Include a sidebar or side banner with a link to product/pricing on content pages.
Review the breadcrumb and footer to ensure every page has a way back to the commercial flow.
navigation_dead_ends.verification_notes: We will check whether support and help pages contain links that lead back to commercial pages (pricing, product, checkout).

remediation_steps: Map the most common path between landing and checkout — reduce it to a maximum of 3 clicks.
Add a direct shortcut to checkout/pricing in the homepage hero and product pages.
Remove mandatory intermediate steps that do not add value to the purchase decision.
page_depth_before_conversion.verification_notes: We will navigate the homepage links to checkout and count how many clicks are required to reach the conversion page.

remediation_steps: Rewrite each listed feature in the format "Feature → What this means for you".
Add real-use examples or mini-cases next to each main feature.
Prioritize business benefits (time savings, revenue increase) over technical specifications.
feature_benefit_disconnect.verification_notes: We will re-analyze the features and product pages looking for benefit-oriented language instead of technical specification.

remediation_steps: Create a comparison page positioning your product against the 2-3 main alternatives.
Add a comparison table on the pricing page with clear differentiators.
Use "why we're different" language instead of "why we're better" to sound genuine.
comparison_absent.verification_notes: We will check whether a comparison page (vs, compare, alternatives) or a comparison table on the pricing page exists.

remediation_steps: Rewrite the FAQ with the 5-7 main purchase objections (price, timeline, guarantee, support, security).
Place the FAQ near the primary CTA or on the pricing page — not on a separate page.
Each answer should defuse the objection and end with a trust reinforcement (data, guarantee, testimonial).
objection_echo_chamber.verification_notes: We will analyze the FAQ content looking for purchase objection language (price, refund, guarantee) vs technical questions.

remediation_steps: Remove links to inactive social channels or those with fewer than 1 post in the last 30 days.
If keeping the links, ensure the profiles have recent and professional content.
Replace decorative links with real social proof (follower count, ratings, mentions).
social_channels_decorative.verification_notes: We will verify whether the social media links in the footer and header point to profiles with recent content.

remediation_steps: Add ROI context next to the price (e.g., "equivalent to $X per lead" or "saves Y hours/month").
Include a value comparison: what the buyer spends today vs what they spend with your product.
Place a customer testimonial with a measurable result near the pricing table.
pricing_without_context.verification_notes: We will check the pricing page looking for ROI, savings, or comparative value language near the prices.

remediation_steps: Apply brand logo, colors, and typography on the checkout/payment page.
If using external checkout (Stripe, PayPal), customize it with your brand or use embedded checkout.
Add a progress bar with consistent visual identity in the payment flow.
checkout_identity_break.verification_notes: We will compare the visual elements of the homepage with those of the checkout to verify brand continuity.

remediation_steps: Show the logos of accepted payment methods on the pricing page and in the footer.
Add card brand icons, Pix, boleto (if applicable) near the purchase button.
Mention installment options before checkout — don't wait for the buyer to discover them on their own.
payment_options_invisible.verification_notes: We will check whether images or text of payment methods are visible on the pricing and product pages.

remediation_steps: Move the guarantee mention close to the purchase button on the pricing and checkout pages.
Create a visual "X-day Guarantee" badge and place it near the primary CTA.
Repeat the guarantee at no fewer than 3 funnel points: product, pricing, and checkout.
guarantee_invisible_at_decision.verification_notes: We will check whether a guarantee or refund mention exists on the pricing and checkout pages, near the CTAs.

remediation_steps: Implement at least 1 legitimate urgency mechanism (limited slots, time-bound price, temporary bonus).
Avoid fake urgency (permanent countdowns) — use verifiable, time-limited mechanisms.
Communicate real scarcity when it exists: "X slots left" or "offer valid until DD/MM".
urgency_mechanics_absent.verification_notes: We will check whether temporal or scarcity language exists on the pricing and product pages.

remediation_steps: Create an onboarding with 3 clear steps that takes the new user to the first result in under 5 minutes.
Send a post-purchase email with a visual "first result" guide — not just a payment confirmation.
Add a progress checklist on the dashboard showing the path to first value.
first_value_path_unclear.verification_notes: We will check whether onboarding content or a first-use guide exists on the post-login pages and transactional emails.

remediation_steps: Publish a response SLA on the support page (e.g., "we respond within 4 business hours").
Add an estimated response time on the contact form and in the chat.
If you can't promise a quick response, offer a knowledge base or FAQ as an immediate alternative.
support_response_expectation_gap.verification_notes: We will check whether the support/contact page mentions response time or SLA.

remediation_steps: Create a dedicated page explaining the billing cycle, how to cancel, and what happens to the data.
Add a link to this page in the footer and at checkout, near the payment button.
Include cancellation information in the welcome email and the customer area.
billing_transparency_absent.verification_notes: We will check whether a page exists explaining billing, cancellation, and data retention, linked from the footer and checkout.

remediation_steps: Rewrite the plan comparison to show the business value of each premium feature (not just the name).
Add an ROI calculator or real-use example to justify the upgrade.
Show a testimonial from a customer who upgraded and the result they achieved.
upgrade_value_gap.verification_notes: We will check whether the pricing/plans page contains business value language in the features of the higher tiers.

remediation_steps: Implement a simple referral program with a shareable link and clear benefit for both sides.
Add a "Refer and earn" button in the customer area and in the post-purchase email.
Offer a relevant incentive (discount, credit, extra feature) that motivates the referral.
referral_path_nonexistent.verification_notes: We will check whether a referral page or link is accessible in the logged-in area or on the website pages.

remediation_steps: Create an automatic process to collect testimonials after customer success milestones (e.g., 30 days, first result).
Publish the collected testimonials on commercial pages, pricing, and homepage.
Integrate Google, Trustpilot, or Reclame Aqui reviews directly on the product pages.
success_story_feedback_loop_broken.verification_notes: We will check whether recent testimonials with name and result exist on the main commercial pages.

remediation_steps: Document the brand tone of voice with "do" and "don't" examples for each type of page.
Review homepage, pricing, checkout, and support ensuring consistency of tone and vocabulary.
Use the same level of formality and personality across all commercial pages.
tone_shift_across_journey.verification_notes: We will re-analyze the tone and vocabulary of the main pages looking for voice inconsistencies between them.

remediation_steps: Audit the complete funnel on mobile: homepage → product → pricing → checkout — note every friction point.
Fix buttons that are too small, forms that don't fit on the screen, and elements that block scrolling.
Prioritize mobile-first: if it works well on mobile, it works on everything.
mobile_journey_friction_compound.verification_notes: We will re-analyze the complete mobile journey looking for cumulative frictions that impact the purchase experience.

remediation_steps: Map where your trust signals (badges, guarantees, testimonials) are and where they are missing.
Move trust signals close to decision points: pricing, checkout, and payment forms.
Reduce redundant trust signals on the homepage and redistribute them to the conversion pages.
trust_gradient_inverted.verification_notes: We will compare the density of trust signals between the homepage and decision pages (pricing, checkout).

remediation_steps: Identify the commercial page without a CTA to the next funnel stage.
Add a clear CTA button or link directing to the next step (e.g., "See Pricing", "Get Started").
Place the CTA above the fold or in a high-visibility location.
Verify that the CTA text communicates the value of the next step, not just the action.
funnel_dead_end_page.verification_notes: We will re-analyze the page links to confirm that a CTA to the next funnel stage has been added.

remediation_steps: Audit which AI crawlers are blocked in /robots.txt and unblock GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot, Google-Extended.
Publish /llms.txt at the root with a one-page summary of what the product does and links to pricing + docs.
Add JSON-LD (Organization on the home + Product/Offer on /pricing + FAQPage where there is Q&A) — AIs prefer rich-schema sites.
Claim profiles in your category's directories (G2/Capterra for SaaS, independent listicles for ecommerce).
Build "<your brand> vs <top competitor>" pages on your own domain before competitors own that narrative.
discoverability_critically_weak.verification_notes: We will re-run the external recon (DDG SERP + robots.txt + JSON-LD parse) and confirm that AI Visibility Score rose above 60.

remediation_steps: Start with quick wins: /llms.txt + Product schema on /pricing (30 minutes each).
Claim listings on third parties (G2 / Capterra / Product Hunt) — AIs cite more brands that appear in third-party sources.
Solve the most visible point: if the brand doesn't appear in branded search, fix title + canonical + schema on the home.
Publish a "best <category>" page on your domain targeting comparison queries you don't currently cover.
discoverability_gaps_significant.verification_notes: We will re-run the external recon in 30-60 days and measure the lift in the AI Visibility Score (target: +10 points).

remediation_steps: Discoverability core is already OK — refine for AI search to compound visibility.
Consider /llms.txt + /pricing.md if you don't have them yet, to unlock AI agent parsing.
Audit schema markup: add FAQPage where there is relevant Q&A, HowTo in the docs.
Monitor the AI Visibility Score quarterly and investigate any drop.
discoverability_improvable.verification_notes: We will re-run the external recon quarterly to track the trajectory of the AI Visibility Score.

remediation_steps: Healthy discoverability — keep feeding fresh content + structure.
Schedule quarterly external recon to catch regressions early (lost citation, removed schema).
Maintain third-party presence: reply to new reviews, update listings, monitor mentions.
discoverability_adequate.verification_notes: We will re-run the external recon to confirm that your AI visibility remains solid.

remediation_steps: Resolve pending public complaints on Trustpilot / Reclame Aqui — BR/EU buyers check before purchasing.
Set up an alert for new reviews and aim for a sub-48h response rate above 70%.
Challenge competitor pages running paid ads on your trademark via Google Ads Trademark Complaints.
Open a UDRP or buy lookalike domains capturing branded traffic.
Submit takedown requests to sites mimicking the brand (Google Safe Browsing + Microsoft Defender).
brand_integrity_critical.verification_notes: We will re-run the external recon (Trustpilot + Reclame Aqui + brand monitoring) and confirm that review responses + reputation labels improved.

remediation_steps: Reply to pending negative reviews on Trustpilot/RA with empathetic acknowledgment + concrete next step.
Negotiate direct deals with top affiliates earning commission on your branded traffic — better margin than the network.
Publish a press kit + about page that owns brand authority in the SERPs.
Monitor lookalike domains weekly; submit takedowns on confirmed ones.
brand_integrity_elevated.verification_notes: We will re-run the external recon in 60 days to confirm that reputation + SERP signals have improved.

remediation_steps: Maintain a response cadence on review platforms (Trustpilot, G2, Reclame Aqui).
Add a monthly brand-monitoring sweep (DDG search "<brand>" + lookalike domain queries).
Update the press kit + about page with recent facts for Wikipedia editors to use as sources.
brand_integrity_weak.verification_notes: We will re-run the external recon monthly to detect new lookalike threats or reputation degradation.

remediation_steps: Brand integrity is strong — keep monitoring review platforms and lookalike domains.
Maintain a sub-48h response cadence for any new review.
Schedule quarterly external recon to catch regressions.
brand_integrity_strong.verification_notes: We will re-run the quarterly external recon to confirm that brand integrity remains solid.

remediation_steps: Open robots.txt at the root of the site (e.g., https://yourdomain.com/robots.txt).
Remove any Disallow: / applied to the User-agents GPTBot, ChatGPT-User, ClaudeBot, anthropic-ai, PerplexityBot, Google-Extended, Bingbot, Applebot-Extended.
If there is a wildcard block ("User-agent: * → Disallow: /"), add explicit permissive stanzas for each AI bot.
Confirm deploy + test each blocked bot in an online robots.txt validator.
ai_bots_blocked.verification_notes: We will re-fetch /robots.txt and parse it to confirm that each AI crawler has access.

remediation_steps: Create /llms.txt at the root of the site in markdown: line 1 = "# <Product Name>", following lines = 1-2 paragraph summary of what it does + who it's for.
Include links to key pages: /pricing, /docs, /about, /contact.
Keep the file under 2KB so agents can parse it quickly.
Full spec: https://llmstxt.org
no_llms_txt.verification_notes: We will fetch /llms.txt and confirm that it has substantive content (>100 chars).

remediation_steps: Create /pricing.md at the root reflecting your plans.
For each plan: name, monthly + annual price, main limits (seats, requests, etc), and a bullet list of what's included.
Add a "last updated" date at the top of the file.
Keep it in sync when you change public pricing.
no_machine_readable_pricing.verification_notes: We will fetch /pricing.md (or /pricing.txt) and confirm that it has substantive parseable content.

remediation_steps: Add Product (or SoftwareApplication for SaaS) JSON-LD on /pricing as the first priority.
Include nested Offer with price + priceCurrency + availability.
Add Organization schema on the homepage with logo + sameAs (social profiles).
Validate with Google Rich Results Test before promoting.
schema_markup_missing_for_product.verification_notes: We will re-parse the JSON-LD on the homepage + /pricing to confirm that Product/Offer schemas are present.

remediation_steps: Confirm that the homepage <title> starts with the brand name.
Add a visible H1 with the brand name verbatim.
Configure canonical pointing to the domain root on the home.
Submit the domain to Google Search Console and force re-indexing.
Add Organization schema with sameAs pointing to all social profiles.
branded_serp_invisible.verification_notes: We will redo the DDG query for the brand name and confirm that your domain appears in the top 3.

remediation_steps: List all 1-2★ reviews without a response in the Trustpilot dashboard.
Reply to each one within 48h with acknowledgment + concrete next step.
For old reviews (>3 months), reply anyway — credibility still comes back.
Set up a notification alert for any new review.
Document patterns in the complaints and adjust product/UX to reduce recurrence.
trustpilot_complaint_cluster.verification_notes: We will re-fetch your Trustpilot profile and confirm that negative reviews have an owner response.

remediation_steps: Access the company dashboard on Reclame Aqui and list pending complaints.
Resolve each complaint publicly — mark as Resolved after agreement with the customer.
Set a response time under 5 business days for new complaints.
Work to raise the "Solution Index" above 7/10 within 90 days.
BR buyers check RA before paying — this is one of the most critical trust signals for the BR market.
reclame_aqui_reputation_critical.verification_notes: We will re-fetch your Reclame Aqui profile via SERP and confirm that the reputation label has moved away from Bad/Not Recommended.

remediation_steps: List the competitor domains that rank above yours in the SERP for the brand name.
For competitors running Google Ads on your trademark: open a Google Ads Trademark Complaint.
For misleading review/affiliate pages: notify via DMCA or direct contact.
Reinforce authority signals: Wikipedia article, Organization schema, recent press releases.
Publish a "brand vs competitor" comparison page on your domain to own the narrative.
competitor_brand_hijack_serp.verification_notes: We will re-search the brand name via DDG and confirm that your domain returned to the top 3.

remediation_steps: Publish a "best <category> [year]" listicle on your domain covering 5-7 tools (including yours, fairly).
Target alternative keywords: "<category> alternatives", "best <category> for <use case>".
Do outreach to 3-5 independent "best of" listicle authors offering inclusion.
Consider short-term paid ads while organic SEO matures.
category_intent_invisible.verification_notes: We will redo the category SERP query via DDG and confirm that your domain appears in the top 10.

remediation_steps: Visit g2.com/sellers/contact and claim your product profile (free).
Add a complete description, 5+ screenshots, listed integrations, and relevant categories.
Invite 10-15 happy customers to leave an honest review in the first 30 days.
Set up an alert for new reviews and respond within 48h.
Goal: reach 50+ reviews in 90 days — AIs prefer brands with verified social proof volume.
g2_listing_void.verification_notes: We will re-try the G2 URL of your product and confirm that there is an active profile with reviews.

remediation_steps: Claim profiles on Capterra + GetApp + SoftwareAdvice (all Gartner, single onboarding).
Add description, features, integrations, and screenshots.
Set up a monthly review collection cadence with active customers.
Submit to relevant categories — Gartner curated, takes 1-2 weeks to approve.
capterra_listing_void.verification_notes: We will re-try the Capterra search for your brand and confirm a listed profile.

remediation_steps: List existing independent sources about the brand: press, editorial blogs, papers, awards.
Recruit independent Wikipedia editors (DO NOT write it yourself — violates NPOV).
Provide source material: press kit + factsheet with dates, team, milestones, customer logos.
Editing increases brand authority in AI responses — Wikipedia is ~7.8% of ChatGPT citations.
wikipedia_article_thin_or_outdated.verification_notes: We will re-check the brand's Wikipedia article and confirm that extract length > 800 chars and the last edit is < 18 months.

remediation_steps: Identify which critical pages (checkout, thank_you, cart) are missing the pixel snippet — inspect the HTML in incognito mode looking for the <script src="/snippet/vestigio.js">.
Install the snippet in the <head> of those pages. If your platform uses a shared template (Shopify, Nuvemshop, etc.), a single theme-wide edit covers it.
Confirm the page_view event fires on each installed page via DevTools > Network filtering for "vestigio.js".
Run a new audit cycle: the pixel_coverage_gap signal should disappear and checkout-abandonment / conversion-rate findings will surface with real data.
pixel_coverage_gap.verification_notes: We'll run a headless browser against each page flagged as missing and verify the pixel snippet is present + fires the page_view event.

privacy_policy.meta_description: Vestigio's Privacy Policy. Learn how we collect, use, and protect your personal information.
privacy_policy.heading: Privacy Policy
privacy_policy.translation_disclaimer: This is a courtesy English translation of our Portuguese Privacy Policy. In the event of any conflict or inconsistency between versions, the Portuguese version prevails.
privacy_policy.intro_1: VESTIGIO TECNOLOGIA LTDA, registered under CNPJ No. 65,445,297/0001-44, trading as VESTIGIO TECNOLOGIA, with headquarters at Av. Prefeito Osmar Cunha, 416, Sala 1108, Centro, Florianópolis/SC, ZIP 88015-100, Brazil (“Vestigio”, “we”, “our”, or “us”), respects your privacy and describes in this Privacy Policy how we collect, use, store, share, and protect data related to your use of our website, applications, dashboards, APIs, pixels, integrations, and other services associated with the vestigio.io domain, its subdomains, and any other domains or environments we may operate in the future (“Services”).
privacy_policy.intro_2: By accessing or using the Services, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agreed to this Policy.
s1_items: applications, dashboards, APIs, pixels, snippets, integrations, and other surfaces operated by Vestigio;
trial accounts, free trials, paid plans, AI credits, and other features we make available.
privacy_policy.s2_body: We may collect and process, as applicable, the following categories of data:
privacy_policy.s2_1_body: We may collect data such as name, email, phone number, company, role, domain, registered URLs, login information, plan history, support information, preferences, and other data you provide when creating or managing your account.
privacy_policy.s2_2_body: We may collect IP address, browser and device identifiers, operating system, access logs, date and time of use, pages accessed, navigation events, errors, performance metrics, environment configuration, analysis history, findings, snapshots, reports, generated outputs, feature consumption, and AI credit consumption.
privacy_policy.s2_3_body: We may collect and process URLs, domains, subdomains, routes, pages, structural elements, visible content, public metadata, technical signals, results of automated browsing, interactions equivalent to those of a regular user, data obtained through DevTools, the DOM, browser automation, and other techniques compatible with the purpose of the Service.
privacy_policy.s2_4_title: 2.4. Data collected via pixel, snippet, or instrumentation
privacy_policy.s2_4_body: When you install the Vestigio Pixel, snippet, script, or other instrumentation technology in an environment under your control, we may receive technical, analytical, behavioral, structural, and operational data related to that environment, to the extent necessary to provide the Services.
privacy_policy.s2_5_body: When you connect Vestigio to third-party services, we may collect and process everything that is technically possible and legitimately accessible in read-only mode, including tokens, metadata, snapshots, operational data, catalog data, configuration data, campaign data, event data, billing data, performance data, store data, and other information necessary for the contracted functionality.
privacy_policy.s2_5_items_intro: Current or planned integrations may include, among others:
privacy_policy.s3_title: 3. Nature of analyses performed by Vestigio
s3_items: data publicly accessible on the internet;
structures and visible elements of websites, stores, pages, and digital surfaces;
results of automated interactions equivalent to those of a regular customer or visitor;
data collected through pixel, snippet, or integration authorized by you.
privacy_policy.s3_body_2: You are solely responsible for registering, connecting, or installing Vestigio only in assets, environments, accounts, domains, pages, and integrations for which you hold legitimate authorization.
privacy_policy.s3_body_3: Vestigio is not liable for acts performed by you outside the platform, nor for improper use of the Services on third-party assets without adequate authorization.
s4_items: create, authenticate, maintain, and administer your account;
provide, operate, run, monitor, and improve the Services;
process audits, analyses, findings, reports, answers, suggestions, and outputs;
offer and manage free trials, paid plans, and AI credits;
process billing, payments, renewals, reconciliations, and financial history;
respond to tickets, inquiries, and support requests;
detect, prevent, and investigate fraud, abuse, misuse, technical failures, and security incidents;
monitor performance, stability, availability, and integrity of the platform;
comply with legal, regulatory, contractual, and rights-defense obligations;
conduct legitimate operational, administrative, technical, and commercial communications;
maintain audit trails, logs, and operational evidence.
privacy_policy.s5_body: When applicable, Vestigio may process personal data based on one or more of the following legal grounds:
s5_items: performance of a contract or preliminary procedures related to a contract;
compliance with a legal or regulatory obligation;
regular exercise of rights in judicial, administrative, or arbitration proceedings;
legitimate interest, within the limits of applicable law;
fraud prevention and security of the data subject and platform.
privacy_policy.s6_body: Vestigio payments may be processed by Paddle and by partners, sub-processors, or financial institutions involved in the billing flow. Vestigio does not store full sensitive card data when processing occurs through specialized third parties, but may store transaction identifiers, payment status, plan, billing history, invoices, financial metadata, and information necessary for subscription management.
privacy_policy.s7_title: 7. Cookies, pixels, analytics, and similar technologies
privacy_policy.s7_body_1: Vestigio may use cookies, local storage, pixels, tags, scripts, logs, identifiers, and similar technologies for:
privacy_policy.s7_body_2: Vestigio may currently use, among others:
privacy_policy.s7_body_3: Tools, vendors, and technologies may be changed, added, or removed over time.
privacy_policy.s8_body_1: Vestigio does not sell personal data.
privacy_policy.s8_body_2: We may share data, to the extent necessary, with:
s8_items: hosting, infrastructure, observability, analytics, email, security, authentication, and support providers;
platforms and integrations connected by you;
companies of the same economic group, affiliates, successors, or acquirers, in the event of corporate reorganization, merger, acquisition, or asset sale;
consultants, auditors, legal, accounting, and technical advisors, under confidentiality obligations;
administrative, regulatory, or judicial authorities, when required by law or valid order.
privacy_policy.s9_body: Your data may be stored, processed, or accessed on servers and systems located outside Brazil, including by technology and infrastructure providers. In such cases, Vestigio will adopt reasonable measures to ensure adequate protection in compliance with the LGPD (Brazilian General Data Protection Law) and other applicable regulations.
privacy_policy.s10_body_1: Vestigio may retain data for the time necessary to fulfill the purposes of this Policy, meet legal and regulatory obligations, preserve evidence, exercise rights, and maintain operational continuity of the platform.
privacy_policy.s10_body_2: Without prejudice to legal deadlines or specific retention needs:
s10_items: operational data related to analyses, findings, reports, snapshots, outputs, and equivalent materials may be retained for up to 30 (thirty) days;

financial, tax, contractual data, security logs, and records necessary for the defense of rights may be retained for longer periods, as required by law, regulation, or duly justified legitimate interest.
privacy_policy.s11_body_1: Vestigio adopts reasonable technical, administrative, and organizational measures to protect data against unauthorized access, destruction, loss, alteration, improper disclosure, or any form of inappropriate or unlawful processing.
privacy_policy.s11_body_2: Vestigio seeks to operate with controls aligned with the LGPD and recognized market best practices for security, governance, and control, including reference to frameworks and standards widely used in the market, such as those associated with auditable environments and corporate compliance programs.
privacy_policy.s11_body_3: Nonetheless, no environment is absolutely inviolable, and we cannot guarantee absolute security.
privacy_policy.s12_body: Under applicable law, you may request, where applicable:
s12_items: confirmation of the existence of processing;
correction of incomplete, inaccurate, or outdated data;
review of automated decisions, where applicable;
revocation of consent, where that is the legal basis;
objection to specific processing, under legal terms.
privacy_policy.s13_body_1: If you wish to request the deletion of your data, send an email to support@vestigio.io with the subject “Data deletion request” and, if possible, include:
s13_items: description of the data or account to be deleted.
privacy_policy.s13_body_2: Vestigio will process valid deletion requests within 7 (seven) days, when legally and technically possible, except in cases of mandatory retention, fraud prevention, security, compliance with legal obligations, preservation of evidence, and regular exercise of rights.
privacy_policy.s14_body: Vestigio is not intended for minors under 18 years of age without the applicable legal and contractual authorization.
privacy_policy.s15_body: Vestigio may update this Policy at any time. The version in force will always be the most recently published on our official channels.
privacy_policy.s16_body: For questions, privacy requests, data deletion, or support, contact us through:
privacy_policy.s16_address: Address: Av. Prefeito Osmar Cunha, 416, Sala 1108, Centro, Florianópolis/SC, ZIP 88015-100, Brazil

terms_of_use.meta_description: Vestigio Terms of Use. Read the rules and conditions governing the use of the platform.
